Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition cumulative net investment loss in subsection 110.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the investment expense of the individual for the year or a preceding taxation year ending after 1987 (except any portions included in subparagraph (ii) of the description of H in paragraph 110.61(2)(b) and subparagraph (ii) of the description of H in paragraph 110.62(2)(b) to the extent they reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual under each of subsections 110.61(2) and 110.62(2))

Subsection.Text: The first formula in paragraph 110.6(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 110.6(2.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In computing the taxable income of an individual (other than a trust) for the individualâs taxation year that includes June 25, 2024 (referred to in this subsection as the âtransition yearâ), there may be deducted, where that individual was resident in Canada throughout the transition year and that individual disposed of in the transition year, and on or after June 25, 2024, a qualified small business corporation share of the individual or a qualified farm or fishing property of the individual, such amount as the individual may claim not exceeding the least of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the individualâs cumulative gains limit at the end of the transition year exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the individual under subsection (2) or (2.1) in computing the individualâs taxable income for the transition year,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the individualâs annual gains limit for the transition year exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the individual under subsection (2) or (2.1) in computing the individualâs taxable income for the transition year, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that would be determined in respect of the individual for the transition year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and capital losses if the only properties referred to in that paragraph were qualified small business corporation shares of the individual and qualified farm or fishing properties of the individual, disposed of by the individual on or after June 25, 2024.

Subsection.Text: Section 110.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (31):
Subsection (33) applies to an individual for the 2025 taxation year, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the taxation year the individual has a taxable capital gain from the disposition, before June 25, 2024, by a partnership with a fiscal period that begins before June 25, 2024 and ends after 2024 or a trust with a taxation year that begins before June 25, 2024 and ends after 2024, of a qualified small business corporation share of the individual or a qualified farm or fishing property of the individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount of a taxable capital gain of the individual described in paragraph (a) exceeds the amount that would be determined under paragraph (2)(a) in respect of the individual for the taxation year if the reference to â$625,000â in that paragraph read as â$522,145â (the amount of which excess is referred to in subsection (33) as the âdenied excessâ).

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (2) to (2.2), if this subsection applies to an individual for a taxation year, no amount may be deducted under this section for the taxation year by the individual in respect of the individualâs taxable capital gains for the year described in paragraph (32)(a) to the extent of the denied excess.
Subsections (1) to (4) apply in respect of dispositions that occur on or after August 12, 2024.
Subsection (5) applies to taxation years that begin after 2024.
Subsections (6) and (7) apply to taxation years that begin after 2023.
Paragraph 110.61(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no individual has prior to the disposition time sought a deduction under this section or section 110.62 in respect of a disposition of shares that, at the time of that disposition, derived their value directly or indirectly, from an active business that is also relevant to the determination of whether the disposition of the subject shares satisfies the condition set out in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying business transfer in subsection 248(1);

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 110.61(1)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the subject shares were not owned by anyone other than the individual or a person or partnership related to the individual, except that if, at any time in the 24-month period immediately preceding the disposition time, the subject shares were substituted for other shares (in this paragraph referred to as the âsubstituted sharesâ), the subject shares shall be considered to have met the requirements of this subparagraph only where the substituted shares were not owned by any person or partnership other than a person or partnership described in this subparagraph throughout the period beginning 24 months before the disposition time and ending at the time of substitution, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 50% of the fair market value of the subject shares and the substituted shares, if any, was derived, directly or indirectly, from assets which were used principally in an active business;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 110.61(1)(d)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: throughout any 24-month period ending before the disposition time, the individual, or a spouse or common-law partner of the individual, was actively engaged on a regular, continuous and substantial basis (including within the meaning of paragraph 120.4(1.1)(a)) in the activities of the business that is relevant to the determination of whether the subject shares satisfy the condition set out in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying business transfer in subsection 248(1), and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 110.61(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: A Ã B Ã C â D

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the elected amount (within the meaning of clause (1)(e)(ii)(A)) included in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e),
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, if only one individual is entitled to a deduction under this subsection in respect of the qualifying business transfer,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the percentage assigned to the individual in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e), if a percentage is assigned to the individual in accordance with clause (1)(e)(ii)(B), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fraction of the taxpayerâs capital gain from the disposition of the subject shares that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) that applies to the subject shares in the year, and

is the total of each amount claimed by the taxpayer under this subsection in a prior taxation year in respect of the disposition of the subject shares multiplied by the amount determined by the formula
is the fraction of a capital gain that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) in the current year, and
is the fraction of a capital gain that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) in the prior year in respect of the disposition of the subject shares; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and losses (except any portion related to a deduction previously claimed by the individual in the year under this subsection), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that would be determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and losses if the only properties referred to in that paragraph were the subject shares, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individualâs allowable business investment losses for the year (except any portion that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under this subsection),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the individualâs investment expense for the year exceeds the individualâs investment income for the year (except any portion of the excess that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under this subsection), and for the purposes of this subparagraph,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: investment expense of an individual for a year, has the same meaning as in subsection 110.6(1), except that the reference to âamount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph (a) of the description of B in the definition annual gains limitâ in paragraph (f) of that definition is to be read as âtotal of all amounts determined in respect of the individual for the year under subparagraph (iii) of the description of H in subsection 110.61(2) (to the extent that amount reduces the amount otherwise deductible under that subsection)â, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: investment income of an individual for a year, has the same meaning as in subsection 110.6(1), except that the reference to âamount determined in respect of the individual for the year for A in the definition annual gains limitâ in paragraph (f) of that definition is to be read as âtotal of all amounts determined in respect of the individual for the year for the description of G in subsection 110.61(2) (except any amount that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under subsection 110.61(2))â, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individualâs net capital losses for other taxation years deducted under paragraph 111(1)(b) in computing the individualâs taxable income for the year

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the amount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and capital losses (except any portion related to a deduction previously claimed by the individual in respect of other subject shares under this subsection) exceeds the amount determined for G.

Subsection.Text: Section 110.61 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If an individual claims more than one deduction under subsection (2) in a taxation year, the individual is to designate the order in which the deductions are claimed, and if the individual does not designate an order, the Minister may designate the order.
Paragraph 110.61(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time that is the beginning of the taxation year of a qualifying business of the trust in which less than 50% of the fair market value of the shares of the qualifying business is derived, directly or indirectly, from assets used principally in an active business carried on by one or more qualifying businesses controlled by the trust at both that time and at the beginning of the preceding taxation year of the qualifying business (unless the active business has ceased to be carried on at that time due to the disposition of all the assets that were used to carry on the business in order to satisfy debts owed to creditors of the trust or of the qualifying business).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 110.61(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within eight years of the day that is 24 months after the disposition time for the qualifying business transfer, in computing the income of the trust that participated in the qualifying business transfer, the trust is deemed to have a gain equal to the elected amount (within the meaning of clause (1)(e)(ii)(A)) included in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e), for the year in which the disqualifying event occurs, from the disposition of a capital property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 110.61(11) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: a spouse or common-law partner of a particular individual includes another individual who was a spouse or common-law partner of the particular individual immediately before the death of the other individual;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) and (6) to (8) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsections (4) and (5) are deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 110.61:

Section.MarginalNote: Deduction for qualifying cooperative conversion â conditions

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to an individual (other than a trust) if, at the time of a disposition (referred to in this section as the âdisposition timeâ) of shares of the capital stock (referred to in this section as the âsubject sharesâ) of a corporation (referred to in this section as the âsubject corporationâ) to another corporation (referred to in this section as the âpurchaser corporationâ) that occurred after 2023 and before 2027 under a qualifying cooperative conversion, the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no individual has prior to the disposition time sought a deduction under this section or section 110.61 in respect of a disposition of shares that, at the time of that disposition, derived their value directly or indirectly, from an active business that is also relevant to the determination of whether the disposition of the subject shares satisfies the condition set out in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying cooperative conversion in subsection 248(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: throughout the 24 months immediately preceding the disposition time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the subject shares were not owned by anyone other than the individual or a person or partnership related to the individual, except that if, at any time in the 24-month period immediately preceding the disposition time, the subject shares were substituted for other shares (in this paragraph referred to as the âsubstituted sharesâ), the subject shares shall be considered to have met the requirements of this subparagraph only where the substituted shares were not owned by any person or partnership other than a person or partnership described in this subparagraph throughout the period beginning 24 months before the disposition time and ending at the time of substitution, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 50% of the fair market value of the subject shares and the substituted shares, if any, was derived directly or indirectly from assets which were used principally in an active business;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before the disposition time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the subject corporation and each corporation affiliated with the subject corporation in which the subject corporation owns (directly or indirectly) shares is not a professional corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation is not established for the purposes of providing employment to its members who are its employees at that time (excluding any officer or director of the purchaser corporation) or the employees of another corporation controlled by the purchaser corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: at the disposition time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual is at least 18 years of age,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: throughout any 24-month period ending before the disposition time, the individual, or a spouse or common-law partner of the individual, was actively engaged on a regular, continuous and substantial basis (including within the meaning of paragraph 120.4(1.1)(a)) in the activities of the business that is relevant to the determination of whether the subject shares satisfy the condition set out in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying cooperative conversion in subsection 248(1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation is a worker cooperative, of which at least 75% of its

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: qualifying cooperative workers described in paragraph (d) of the definition worker cooperative in subsection 248(1) are resident in Canada, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: individual employee members described in paragraph (e) of the definition worker cooperative in subsection 248(1) are resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the purchaser corporation, the individual and any other individual entitled to a deduction under subsection (2) in respect of the qualifying cooperative conversion

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: jointly elect, in prescribed form, for the deduction provided under subsection (2) to apply in respect of the disposition of the subject shares,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: include the following information in the election:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an amount (in this paragraph referred to as the âelected amountâ) equal to the total amount of capital gains that the parties agree may be eligible for a deduction under subsection (2) with respect to the qualifying cooperative conversion, not exceeding $10,000,000, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if more than one individual is eligible for a deduction in respect of the qualifying cooperative conversion, the percentage of the elected amount that is assigned to each eligible individual (provided that the total percentages assigned to all individuals cannot exceed 100%), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: file the election with the Minister on or before the earlier of the individualâs and the worker cooperativeâs filing-due date for the taxation year that includes the disposition time.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Capital gains deduction â qualifying cooperative conversions
Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to an individual, in computing the taxable income for a taxation year of the individual, there may be deducted such amount as the individual may claim not exceeding the least of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: A Ã B Ã C â D

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the elected amount (within the meaning of clause (1)(e)(ii)(A)) included in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e),
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, if only one individual is entitled to a deduction under this subsection in respect of the qualifying cooperative conversion,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the percentage assigned to the individual in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e), if a percentage is assigned to the individual in accordance with clause (1)(e)(ii)(B), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fraction of the taxpayerâs capital gain from the disposition of the subject shares that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) that applies to the subject shares in the year, and

is the total of each amount claimed by the taxpayer under this subsection in a prior taxation year in respect of the disposition of the subject shares multiplied by the amount determined by the formula
is the fraction of a capital gain that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) in the current year, and
is the fraction of a capital gain that is a taxable capital gain under paragraph 38(a) in the prior year in respect of the disposition of the subject shares; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that would be determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) (to the extent that that amount is not included in computing an amount determined under paragraph 110.6(2)(d) or (2.1)(d) for the individual) in respect of capital gains and capital losses if the only properties referred to in paragraph 3(b) were the subject shares of the individual.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, a disqualifying event in respect of a qualifying cooperative conversion occurs at the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time when the worker cooperative that participated in the qualifying cooperative conversion ceases to be a worker cooperative, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time that is the beginning of the taxation year of the worker cooperative in which less than 50% of the fair market value of the shares of the worker cooperative is derived, directly or indirectly, from assets used principally in an active business carried on by the worker cooperative (or by a qualifying cooperative business controlled by the worker cooperative) at both that time and at the beginning of the preceding taxation year of the worker cooperative (unless the active business has ceased to be carried on at that time due to the disposition of all the assets that were used to carry on the business in order to satisfy debts owed to creditors of the worker cooperative or of the qualifying cooperative business).

Subsection.Text: If a disqualifying event in respect of a qualifying cooperative conversion occurs

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within 24 months after the disposition time for the qualifying cooperative conversion, subsection (2) is deemed to have never applied in respect of the subject shares disposed of under the qualifying cooperative conversion; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 8 years of the day that is 24 months after the disposition time for the qualifying cooperative conversion, in computing the income of the worker cooperative that participated in the qualifying cooperative conversion, the worker cooperative is deemed to have a gain equal to the elected amount (within the meaning of clause (1)(e)(ii)(A)) included in the joint election referred to in paragraph (1)(e), for the year in which the disqualifying event occurs, from the disposition of a capital property.

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision in this section, subsection (2) does not apply in respect of a qualifying cooperative conversion if it is reasonable to consider that one of the purposes of any transaction (as defined in subsection 245(1)), or series of transactions, is to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: involve the subject corporation (or the purchaser corporation) in the qualifying cooperative conversion to accommodate the direct or indirect acquisition of subject shares (or the acquisition of all or substantially all of the risk of loss and opportunity for gain or profit in respect of the subject shares) by another person or partnership (other than the subject corporation or the purchaser corporation) in a manner that permits an individual to claim a deduction under subsection (2) that would otherwise not be available; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: organize or reorganize a subject corporation or any other corporation, partnership or trust in a manner that allows a deduction to be claimed under subsection (2) in respect of more than one qualifying cooperative conversion of a business that is relevant to the determination of whether subject shares satisfied the condition set out in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying cooperative conversion in subsection 248(1).

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), no amount may be deducted under this section in respect of a capital gain of an individual for a particular taxation year in computing the individualâs taxable income for the particular taxation year or any subsequent year, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual knowingly or under circumstances amounting to gross negligence

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: fails to file the individualâs return of income for the particular taxation year within one year after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the particular taxation year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: fails to report the capital gain in the individualâs return of income for the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister establishes the facts justifying the denial of such an amount under this section.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), no amount may be deducted under this section in computing an individualâs taxable income for a taxation year in respect of a capital gain of the individual for the taxation year if the capital gain is from a disposition of property which disposition is part of a series of transactions or events

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that includes a dividend received by a corporation to which dividend subsection 55(2) does not apply but would apply if this Act were read without reference to paragraph 55(3)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in which any property is acquired by a corporation or partnership for consideration that is significantly less than the fair market value of the property at the time of acquisition (other than an acquisition as the result of an amalgamation or merger of corporations or the winding-up of a corporation or partnership or a distribution of property of a trust in satisfaction of all or part of a corporationâs capital interest in the trust).

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), if an individual has a capital gain for a taxation year from the disposition of a property and it can reasonably be concluded, having regard to all the circumstances, that a significant part of the capital gain is attributable to the fact that dividends were not paid on a share (other than a prescribed share within the meaning of subsection 110.6(8)) or that dividends paid on such a share in the taxation year or in any preceding taxation year were less than 90% of the average annual rate of return on that share for that year, no amount in respect of that capital gain shall be deducted under this section in computing the individualâs taxable income for the year.

For the purpose of subsection (8), the average annual rate of return on a share (other than a prescribed share within the meaning of subsection 110.6(8)) of a corporation for a taxation year is the annual rate of return by way of dividends that a knowledgeable and prudent investor who purchased the share on the day it was issued would expect to receive in that year, other than the first year after the issue, in respect of the share if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there was no delay or postponement of the payment of dividends and no failure to pay dividends in respect of the share;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there was no variation from year to year in the amount of dividends payable in respect of the share (other than where the amount of dividends payable is expressed as an invariant percentage of or by reference to an invariant difference between the dividend expressed as a rate of interest and a generally quoted market interest rate); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the proceeds to be received by the investor on the disposition of the share are the same amount the corporation received as consideration on the issue of the share.

Subsection.Text: If it is reasonable to consider that one of the main reasons for an individual acquiring, holding or having an interest in a partnership or trust (other than an interest in a personal trust) or for the existence of any terms, conditions, rights or other attributes of the interest is to enable the individual to receive or have allocated to the individual a percentage of any capital gain or taxable capital gain of the partnership or trust that is larger than the individualâs percentage of the income of the partnership or trust, as the case may be, despite any other provision of this Act, no amount may be deducted under subsection (2) by the individual in respect of any such gain allocated or distributed to the individual.
For the purposes of this section,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a taxpayer is deemed to have disposed of shares that are identical properties in the order in which the taxpayer acquired them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a personal trust is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to be related to a person or partnership for any period throughout which the person or partnership was a beneficiary of the trust, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of shares of the capital stock of a corporation, to be related to the person from whom it acquired those shares if, at the time the trust disposed of the shares, all of the beneficiaries (other than registered charities) of the trust were related to that person or would have been so related if that person were living at that time;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a spouse or common-law partner of a particular individual includes another individual who was a spouse or common-law partner of the particular individual immediately before the death of the other individual;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership is deemed to be related to a person for any period throughout which the person was a member of the partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a member of a partnership that is a member of another partnership is deemed to be a member of the other partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if a corporation acquires shares of a class of the capital stock of another corporation from any person, it is deemed in respect of those shares to be related to the person if all or substantially all the consideration received by that person from the corporation in respect of those shares was common shares of the capital stock of the corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: shares issued by a corporation to a particular person or partnership are deemed to have been owned immediately before their issue by a person who was not related to the particular person or partnership unless the shares were issued

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as consideration for other shares,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: as part of a transaction or series of transactions in which the person or partnership disposed of property to the corporation that consisted of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: all or substantially all the assets used in an active business carried on by that person or the members of that partnership, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: an interest in a partnership all or substantially all the assets of which were used in an active business carried on by the members of the partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: as payment of a stock dividend.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 110.62(2)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and capital losses (except any portion related to a deduction previously claimed by the individual in the year under this subsection or subsection 110.61(2)), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that would be determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and losses if the only properties referred to in that paragraph were the subject shares, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individualâs allowable business investment losses for the year (except any portion that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under this subsection or subsection 110.61(2)),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the individualâs investment expense for the year exceeds the individualâs investment income for the year (except any portion of the excess that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under this subsection or subsection 110.61(2)), and for the purposes of this subparagraph,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: investment expense of an individual for a year, has the same meaning as in subsection 110.6(1) except that, the reference to âamount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph (a) of the description of B in the definition annual gains limitâ in paragraph (f) of that definition is to be read as âtotal of all amounts determined in respect of the individual for the year under subparagraph (iii) of the description of H in subsection 110.61(2) and subparagraph (iii) of the description of H in subsection 110.62(2) (to the extent that amount reduces the amount otherwise deductible under that subsection)â, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: investment income of an individual for a year, has the same meaning as in subsection 110.6(1) except that, the reference to âamount determined in respect of the individual for the year for A in the definition annual gains limitâ in paragraph (f) of that definition is to be read as âtotal of all amounts determined in respect of the individual for the year for the description of G in subsection 110.61(2) and the description of G in subsection 110.62(2) (except any amount that previously reduced the amount otherwise deductible by the individual in the year under subsection 110.61(2) or 110.62(2))â, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individualâs net capital losses for other taxation years deducted under paragraph 111(1)(b) in computing the individualâs taxable income for the year

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the amount determined in respect of the individual for the year under paragraph 3(b) in respect of capital gains and capital losses (except any portion related to a deduction previously claimed by the individual in respect of other subject shares under this subsection or subsection 110.61(2)) exceeds the amount determined for G.

Subsection.Text: Section 110.62 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If an individual claims more than one deduction under subsection (2) in a taxation year, the individual is to designate the order in which the deductions are claimed, and if the individual does not designate an order, the Minister may designate the order.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsections (2) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on August 12, 2024.
Clause 111(1)(e)(ii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount required by subsection 127(8), 127.44(11), 127.45(8), 127.48(12), 127.49(8) or 127.491(12) in respect of the partnership to be added in computing the investment tax credit, the CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)), the clean technology investment tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.45(1)), the clean hydrogen tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.48(1)), the CTM investment tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.49(1)) or the clean electricity investment tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.491(1)) of the taxpayer for the taxation year,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 111(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section 111.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
In computing an individualâs taxable income for a taxation year, the provisions of this Division shall be applied in the following order: sections 110, 110.2, 111, 110.61, 110.62, 110.6 and 110.7.

No amount may be deducted for a taxation year of an individual, under section 110.6, in respect of any portion of a taxable capital gain to the extent that the portion of the taxable capital gain has been deducted under section 110.61 or 110.62.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2023.

The definition qualifying expenditure in subsection 118.041(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (i), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (j) and by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Subsections (1) and (2) come into force or are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2026.

Section.Text: Section 118.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (28):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of applying this section, a gift made by an individual before March 2025 and after the end of a taxation year of the individual that ended after November 14, 2024 and before 2025 (referred to in this subsection as the âdonation yearâ) is deemed to have been made by the individual in the donation year and not in the individualâs 2025 taxation year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a credit for the gift would be deductible under this section in computing the individualâs tax payable under this Part for the donation year if it were made immediately before the end of that year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual claims the amount of the gift under subsection (3) for the donation year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the gift was in the form of cash or was transferred by way of cheque, credit card, money order or electronic payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the gift was not made

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: through a payroll deduction, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the individual died after 2024, by the individualâs will.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 122.62(10) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (a), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at the beginning of the month, the person satisfied the conditions set out in paragraphs (c) to (e) of the definition eligible individual in section 122.6.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to months that begin after August 31, 2025.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 122.92:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: eligible health care establishmentâmeans a hospital, nursing care facility, residential care facility, community care facility for the elderly, home health care establishment and similar regulated health care establishments.â(Ã©tablissement de soins de santÃ© admissible)
eligible personal support worker, for a taxation year, means an individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: who performs duties of employment in the capacity of a personal support worker for an eligible health care establishment during the taxation year (in this definition referred to as the âduties for the yearâ);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: who, in the course of performing the duties for the year, ordinarily provides one-on-one care and essential support to optimize and maintain another individualâs health, well-being, safety, autonomy and comfort consistent with that other individualâs health care needs as directed by a regulated health care professional or a provincial or community health organization; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whose main duties of employment, in respect of the duties for the year, include assisting individuals with activities of daily living and mobilization.â(prÃ©posÃ© aux services de soutien Ã la personne admissible)

Definition.Text: return of income, filed by an eligible personal support worker for a taxation year, means a return of income (other than a return of income under subsection 70(2) or 104(23), paragraph 128(2)(e) or subsection 150(4)) that is required to be filed for the taxation year or that would be required to be filed if the eligible personal support worker had tax payable under this Part for the taxation year.â(dÃ©claration de revenu)
yearly eligible remunerationâof an individual for a taxation year means the total of all amounts, each of which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: would be, in the absence of section 8 and paragraph 81(1)(a), the individualâs income for the taxation year from an office or employment as an eligible personal support worker for an eligible health care establishment in a province, other than duties performed in Newfoundland and Labrador, the Northwest Territories and British Columbia; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is certified by the individualâs employer in the prescribed form and manner to be an amount that satisfies the description under paragraph (a).â(rÃ©munÃ©ration annuelle admissible)

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed overpayment â yearly eligible remuneration
Subsection.Text: An eligible personal support worker, for a taxation year that begins after 2025 and that ends before 2031, who files a return of income for the taxation year and makes a claim under this subsection, is deemed to have paid, at the end of the taxation year, on account of tax payable under this Part for the taxation year, an amount equal to the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 5% of the eligible personal support workerâs yearly eligible remuneration for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this Subdivision, if an individual becomes bankrupt in a particular calendar year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: despite subsection 128(2), any reference to a taxation year of the individual (other than in this subsection) is deemed to be a reference to the particular calendar year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individualâs yearly eligible remuneration for the taxation year ending on December 31 of the particular calendar year is deemed to include the individualâs yearly eligible remuneration for the taxation year that begins on January 1 of the particular calendar year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rules in the event of death
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this Subdivision, if an individual dies before the end of a calendar year, any return of income filed by a legal representative of the individual is deemed to be a return of income filed by the individual.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Requirement â certification of yearly eligible remuneration
Subsection.Text: Every employer who pays an amount described in paragraph (a) of the definition yearly eligible remuneration in subsection (1) to an individual in a taxation year must provide the certification described in paragraph (b) of that definition in respect of the total amount described in paragraph (a) of that definition for that individual for that year.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2026 and subsequent taxation years.
Subclause 126(1)(b)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6, 110.61 or 110.62 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (g) and sections 112 and 113, in computing the taxpayerâs taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (6) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
The definition critical mineral in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: critical mineralâmeans bismuth, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, fluorspar, gallium, germanium, graphite, indium, lithium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, nickel, niobium, phosphate, a platinum group metal, a rare earth element, scandium, tantalum, tellurium, tin, titanium, tungsten, uranium, vanadium or zinc;â(minÃ©ral critique)

Subsection.Text: The definition first term shared-use equipment in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: first term shared-use-equipment, of a taxpayer, means depreciable property of the taxpayer (other than prescribed depreciable property of a taxpayer) that is used by the taxpayer, during its operating time in the period (in this subsection and subsection (11.5) referred to as the âfirst periodâ) beginning at the time the property was acquired by the taxpayer and ending at the end of the taxpayerâs first taxation year ending at least 12 months after that time, primarily for the prosecution of scientific research and experimental development in Canada, but does not include general purpose office equipment or furniture;â(matÃ©riel Ã vocations multiples de premiÃ¨re pÃ©riode)

Subsection.Text: The definition government assistance in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: government assistanceâmeans assistance from a government, municipality or other public authority whether as a grant, subsidy, forgivable loan, deduction from tax, investment allowance or as any other form of assistance, other than as an excluded loan (as defined in subsection 12(11)), as a deduction under subsection (5) or (6) or as a deemed payment on account of tax payable under subsection 127.44(2), 127.45(2), 127.48(2), 127.49(2) or 127.491(2);â(aide gouvernementale)

Subsection.Text: The definition second term shared-use equipment in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: second term shared-use-equipment, of a taxpayer, means property of the taxpayer that was first term shared-use-equipment and that is used by the taxpayer, during its operating time in the period (in this subsection and subsection (11.5) referred to as the âsecond periodâ) beginning at the time the property was acquired by the taxpayer and ending at the end of the taxpayerâs first taxation year ending at least 24 months after that time, primarily for the prosecution of scientific research and experimental development in Canada;â(matÃ©riel Ã vocations multiples de deuxiÃ¨me pÃ©riode)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition contract payment in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount, other than a prescribed amount, payable by a Canadian government or municipality or other Canadian public authority or by a person exempt, because of section 149, from tax under this Part on all or part of the personâs taxable income for scientific research and experimental development to be performed for it or on its behalf; (paiement contractuel)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition flow-through mining expenditure in subsection 127(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is a Canadian exploration expense incurred by a corporation after March 2025 and before 2028 (including, for greater certainty, an expense that is deemed by subsection 66(12.66) to be incurred before 2028) in conducting mining exploration activity from or above the surface of the earth for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or quality of a mineral resource described in paragraph (a) or (d) of the definition mineral resource in subsection 248(1),

Paragraph.Label: (c)

Paragraph.Text: that is not an expense that was renounced under subsection 66(12.6) to the corporation (or a partnership of which the corporation is a member), unless that renunciation was under an agreement described in that subsection and made after March 2025 and before April 2027, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition qualified expenditure in subsection 127(9) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of subparagraph (i) and at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an expenditure for first term shared-use-equipment or second term shared-use-equipment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: an expenditure described in paragraph 37(1)(b), or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition qualified expenditure in subsection 127(9) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 127(9) is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: consolidated financial statementsâmeans financial statements in which the assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows of the members of a group are presented as those of a single economic entity;â(Ã©tats financiers consolidÃ©s)

consolidated groupâmeans a group of entities in respect of which an ultimate parent entity is required to prepare consolidated financial statements, or would be so required if equity interests in any of the entities were traded on a public securities exchange;â(groupe consolidÃ©)
eligible Canadian public corporation, at the relevant time in a taxation year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is resident in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a public corporation, or would be a public corporation if the words âdesignated stock exchange in Canadaâ in paragraph (a) of the definition public corporation in subsection 89(1) were read as âdesignated stock exchangeâ,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not controlled, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by one or more non-resident persons, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: would not, if each share of its capital stock that is owned by a non-resident person (as determined, absent actual knowledge, based on publicly available information, including information filed pursuant to applicable securities laws before the year) were owned by a particular person, be controlled by the particular person, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an eligible subsidiary;â(sociÃ©tÃ© publique canadienne admissible)

Definition.Text: eligible subsidiaryâmeans a corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is resident in Canada, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: not less than 90% of the issued shares of each class of the capital stock of which is owned, directly or indirectly, by one or more corporations that are eligible Canadian public corporations because of paragraph (a) of that definition;â(filiale admissible)

Definition.Text: entityâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation, partnership or trust, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other arrangement, association, organization or body whether registered or unregistered for which separate financial accounts are prepared;â(entitÃ©)

Definition.Text: financial statementsâmeans financial statements prepared in accordance with acceptable accounting standards, as defined in subsection 18.21(1);â(Ã©tats financiers)
fiscal yearâmeans an annual accounting period in respect of which a corporation prepares its financial statements;â(exercice)

ultimate parent entity, in respect of a group of entities, means the member of the group that would be the ultimate parent entity, as defined in subsection 233.8(1), of the group if the group were a multinational enterprise group as defined in subsection 233.8(1).â(entitÃ© mÃ¨re ultime)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 127(10.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (e) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection (9), if a corporation was throughout a taxation year a Canadian-controlled private corporation or an eligible Canadian public corporation, there shall be added in computing the corporationâs investment tax credit at the end of the year the amount that is 20% of the least of
Subsection 127(10.2) is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of subsection (10.1), a particular Canadian-controlled private corporationâs expenditure limit for a particular taxation year is the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: $6 million Ã [($60 million â A) Ã· $60 million]

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if the following amount is less than or equal to $15 million:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the particular corporation is not associated with any other corporation in the particular taxation year, the amount that is its taxable capital employed in Canada (within the meaning assigned by section 181.2 or 181.3) for its immediately preceding taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the particular corporation is associated with one or more other corporations in the particular taxation year, the amount that is the total of all amounts, each of which is the taxable capital employed in Canada (within the meaning assigned by section 181.2 or 181.3) of the particular corporation for its, or of one of the other corporations for its, last taxation year that ended in the last calendar year that ended before the end of the particular taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of $60 million and the amount by which the amount determined under subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii), as the case may be, exceeds $15 million.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127(10.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If all of the Canadian-controlled private corporations that are associated with each other in a taxation year file with the Minister in prescribed form an agreement under which, for the purpose of subsection (10.1), they allocate an amount to one or more of them for the year, the expenditure limit for the year of each of the corporations is the amount so allocated if the total of the amounts so allocated does not exceed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: unless subsection (10.32) applies, the amount determined for the year by the formula in subsection (10.2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if subsection (10.32) applies, the amount determined for the year under that subsection.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (10.2), for the purpose of subsection (10.1), if throughout a taxation year a particular Canadian-controlled private corporation is not associated with another corporation and the particular corporation files with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner, the particular corporation may elect that its expenditure limit for the year be determined under subsection (10.6) as if the particular corporation were an eligible Canadian public corporation that is not a member of a consolidated group.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Revenue election for CCPC having associated corporations
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (10.2), and subject to subsections (10.21) to (10.4), for the purpose of subsection (10.1), if at any time in a taxation year one or more particular Canadian-controlled private corporations are members of a group of associated corporations and all those particular corporations file with the Minister a prescribed form, those particular corporations may elect that the expenditure limit for the particular corporations be determined for the year under subsection (10.6), calculated as if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each Canadian-controlled private corporation in the group were an eligible Canadian public corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the group were a consolidated group;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined under subparagraph (a)(ii) of the description of A in subsection (10.6) were the total of all amounts, each of which is the average, over the period of three fiscal years immediately preceding and ending in the last calendar year that ended before the end of the particular taxation year, of annual revenue reflected in the financial statements of each corporation that is a member of the group; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the annual revenue described in paragraph (c)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: must include each corporationâs reasonable share of annual revenue reflected in the financial statements of any partnership or trust in which the corporation held an interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: may include reasonable adjustments to reflect the annual revenue of the group as that of a single economic entity.

Subsection.Text: Section 127(10.6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Expenditure limit determination in certain cases
Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where a Canadian-controlled private corporation (in this paragraph referred to as the âfirst corporationâ) has more than one taxation year ending in the same calendar year and it is associated in two or more of those taxation years with another Canadian-controlled private corporation that has a taxation year ending in that calendar year, the expenditure limit of the first corporation for each taxation year in which it is associated with the other corporation ending in that calendar year is, subject to the application of paragraph (b), an amount equal to its expenditure limit for the first such taxation year determined without reference to paragraph (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where a Canadian-controlled private corporation has a taxation year that is less than 51 weeks, its expenditure limit for the year is that proportion of its expenditure limit for the year determined without reference to this paragraph that the number of days in the year is of 365.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (10.1), a particular eligible Canadian public corporationâs expenditure limit for a particular taxation year is the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: $6 million Ã [($60 million â A) Ã· $60 million]

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if the following amount is less than or equal to $15 million:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the particular corporation is not a member of a consolidated group in the particular taxation year, the amount that is the average, over the period of three fiscal years immediately preceding and ending before the particular taxation year, of its annual revenue based on the amounts reflected in the financial statements of the corporation, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the particular corporation is a member of a consolidated group in the particular taxation year, the amount that is the average, over the period of three fiscal years immediately preceding and ending before the particular taxation year, of the annual revenue reflected in the consolidated financial statements of the group, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of $60 million and the amount by which the amount determined under subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii), as the case may be, exceeds $15 million.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (10.6), the expenditure limit for a taxation year of an eligible Canadian public corporation that is, at any time in the year, a member of a consolidated group is, except as otherwise provided in this section, nil.

If all of the eligible Canadian public corporations that are members of a consolidated group file with the Minister in prescribed form an agreement under which, for the purpose of subsection (10.1), they allocate an amount to one or more of them for the year and the amount so allocated or the total of the amounts so allocated, as the case may be, does not exceed the amount determined for the year by the formula in subsection (10.6), the expenditure limit for the year of each of the corporations is the amount so allocated to it.

If any of the eligible Canadian public corporations that are members of a consolidated group fails to file with the Minister an agreement as contemplated by subsection (10.62) within 30 days after notice in writing by the Minister is forwarded to any of them that such an agreement is required for the purposes of this Part, the Minister must, for the purpose of subsection (10.1), allocate an amount to one or more of them for the year, which amount or the total of which amounts, as the case may be, must equal the amount determined for the year by the formula in subsection (10.6), and in any such case the expenditure limit for the year of each of the corporations is the amount so allocated to it.
Despite any other provision of this section,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where an eligible Canadian public corporation (in this paragraph referred to as the âfirst corporationâ) has more than one taxation year ending in the same calendar year and in two or more of those taxation years it is a member of a consolidated group in which another eligible Canadian public corporation has a taxation year ending in that calendar year, the expenditure limit of the first corporation for each taxation year in which it is in the same group as the other corporation ending in that calendar year is, subject to the application of paragraph (b), an amount equal to its expenditure limit for the first such taxation year determined without reference to paragraph (b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where an eligible Canadian public corporation has a taxation year that is less than 51 weeks, its expenditure limit for the year is that proportion of its expenditure limit for the year determined without reference to this paragraph that the number of days in the year is of 365;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (a)(i) of the description of A in subsection (10.6), where one or more of the fiscal years of an eligible Canadian public corporation is less than 51 weeks, the revenue reflected in the financial statements for each of those fiscal years must be determined by multiplying that amount by the ratio that 365 is of the number of days in that year;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraph (a)(ii) of the description of A in subsection (10.6), where one or more of the fiscal years of the ultimate parent entity of a consolidated group is less than 51 weeks, the revenue reflected in the consolidated financial statements of the entity for each of those fiscal years must be determined by multiplying that amount by the ratio that 365 is of the number of days in that year; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of subparagraphs (a)(i) and (ii) of the description of A in the formula in subsection (10.6),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the average annual revenue referred to in each subparagraph is to be calculated over the actual number of fiscal years if there are less than three fiscal periods immediately preceding and ending before the particular taxation year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if paragraph (10.32)(c) applies, the average annual revenue referred to in that paragraph is to be calculated over the actual number of fiscal years if there are less than three fiscal years immediately preceding and ending in the calendar year referred to in that paragraph.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127(11.1)(c.1) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 127(11.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In applying subsections (5), (7) and (8), paragraphs (a) and (a.1) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection (9) and section 127.1, qualified property and first term shared-use-equipment are deemed not to have been acquired by a taxpayer â and expenditures incurred to acquire property described in paragraph 37(1)(b) are deemed not to have been incurred â before the property is considered to have become available for use by the taxpayer, determined without reference to paragraphs 13(27)(c) and (28)(d).
Subsection 127(11.5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of the definition qualified expenditure in subsection (9),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of an expenditure (other than a prescribed proxy amount) incurred by a taxpayer in a taxation year is deemed to be the amount of the expenditure determined without reference to subsections 13(7.1) and (7.4) and after the application of subsection (11.6); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of an expenditure incurred by a taxpayer in the taxation year that ends coincidentally with the end of the first period (within the meaning assigned in the definition first term shared-use-equipment in subsection (9)) or the second period (within the meaning assigned in the definition second term shared-use-equipment in subsection (9)) in respect of first term shared-use-equipment or second term shared-use-equipment, respectively, of the taxpayer is deemed to be 1/4 of the capital cost of the equipment determined after the application of subsection (11.6) in accordance with the following rules:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost to the taxpayer must be computed as if no amount were added because of section 21, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost to the taxpayer is determined without reference to subsections 13(7.1) and (7.4).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 127(11.6) of the Act after paragraph (b) and before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: the amount of the expenditure incurred by the taxpayer for the service or property and the capital cost to the taxpayer of the property are deemed to be

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 127(11.6)(d)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost to the taxpayer of the property otherwise determined, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127(11.8) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (a), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the leasing of a property is deemed to be the rendering of a service.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127(33) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (27) to (29), (34) and (35) do not apply to a taxpayer or partnership (in this subsection referred to as the âtransferorâ) that disposes of a property to a person or partnership (in this subsection and subsections (34) and (35) referred to as the âpurchaserâ), that does not deal at armâs length with the transferor, if the purchaser acquired the property in circumstances where the cost of the property to the purchaser would have been an expenditure of the purchaser described in subclause 37(8)(a)(ii)(A)(III) or (B)(III) but for subparagraph 2902(b)(iii) of the Income Tax Regulations.
Subsection (1) applies in respect of expenses renounced under a flow-through share agreement entered into after November 4, 2025.

Subsections (2), (4), (5), (8), (9) and (15) to (21) apply in respect of property acquired on or after December 16, 2024 and, in the case of lease costs, to expenditures incurred on or after December 16, 2024.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsections (6) and (7) apply in respect of expenses renounced under a flow-through share agreement entered into after March 2025.
Subsections (10) to (14) apply to taxation years that begin on or after December 16, 2024.
The formula and its description in the definition qualifying income limit in subsection 127.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if $15 million is greater than or equal to the amount (in paragraph (b) referred to as the âtaxable capital amountâ) that is the total of the corporationâs taxable capital employed in Canada (within the meaning assigned by section 181.2 or 181.3) for its immediately preceding taxation year and the taxable capital employed in Canada (within the meaning assigned by section 181.2 or 181.3) of each associated corporation for the associated corporationâs last taxation year that ended in the last calendar year that ended before the end of the particular taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of $60 million and the amount by which the taxable capital amount exceeds $15 million;â(plafond de revenu admissible)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin on or after December 16, 2024.
Subsections (2) and (3) apply in respect of property acquired on or after December 16, 2024 and, in the case of lease costs, to expenditures incurred on or after December 16, 2024.
Section 127.42 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed rebate in respect of fuel charges
Subsection.Text: An amount for a designated province included in the total of all amounts deemed by this section to have been paid on account of tax payable for a taxation year is deemed to have been paid during the taxation year as a rebate in respect of charges levied under Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act in respect of the designated province.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2021 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of subsection 127.421(2) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

A corporation that files, on or before December 31, 2024, a return of income for a particular taxation year ending in 2023 (other than a final return on dissolution) is deemed to have paid on a date specified by the Minister of Finance, on account of tax payable under this Part for that taxation year, the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount, for each designated province, for each calendar year that is 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022 and 2023, determined by the formula
The portion of subsection 127.421(3) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

A corporation that files a return of income for a particular taxation year ending in a calendar year after 2023 (other than a final return on dissolution) is, if the return is filed on or before July 15 of the following calendar year, deemed to have paid on October 1st of that calendar year, on account of tax payable under this Part for the particular taxation year, the amount determined by the formula
Subsection 127.421(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

There is not to be included in computing the income of a corporation for a taxation year an amount that is deemed under subsection (2) or (3) to have been paid on account of tax payable under this Part for a taxation year.
Subsections 127.421(8) and (9) of the Act are replaced by the following:

For the purpose of subsection (2), where there has been an amalgamation of two or more corporations before 2023, the corporation filing a return of income in 2023 is deemed to be the same corporation as and a continuation of each predecessor corporation that was registered with the Minister to make remittances required under section 153 under the corporationâs 2023 business number.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Predecessor corporation â 2023 and subsequent years
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections (2) and (3), the number of persons employed by a corporation in a calendar year after 2022 is deemed to be nil in that year if the corporation is formed by an amalgamation in that calendar year.
Subsection 127.421(11) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections (2) and (3), if a corporation has more than one taxation year ending in the same calendar year, the particular taxation year is the first taxation year that ends in that calendar year.
Subsections (1) to (5) are deemed to have come into force on June 20, 2024.
Subparagraph (c)(iii) of the definition dual-use equipment in subsection 127.44(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.44(17) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may accept the late filing by a qualifying taxpayer of the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) until the later of December 31, 2026 and one year after the filing-due date referred to in subsection (2), but no payment by the taxpayer is deemed to arise under that subsection until the prescribed form containing prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.
Subsections (1), (2), (5) and (8) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Subsection (4) is deemed to have come into force on November 4, 2025.
Subsections (6) and (7) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.45(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: nuclear facilityâincludes a single site, contiguous sites and adjacent sites where nuclear fission reactors are located or will be located.â(installation nuclÃ©aire)

preliminary work activityâmeans an activity that is preliminary to the acquisition, construction, fabrication or installation by or on behalf of a taxpayer of property including, but not limited to, a preliminary activity that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obtaining a right of access or right of way to a project site or obtaining permits or regulatory approvals (including conducting environmental assessments);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: performing front-end design or engineering work (including front-end engineering design studies) or process engineering work for the development of the project, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: collecting and analyzing of site data,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: calculating energy, mass, water or air balances,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: simulating and analyzing the performance and cost of process design options,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: selecting the optimum process design, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: conducting feasibility studies or pre-feasibility studies;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: clearing or excavating land, except excavation directly related to the installation of clean technology property;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: constructing a temporary access road to the project site; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: drilling of a well.â(travaux prÃ©liminaires)

Definition.Text: refurbishmentâmeans significant alterations, renovations, improvements or additions to a property to substantially

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend its useful life;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: increase its capacity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: improve its efficiency.â(remise en Ã©tat)

Definition.Text: small nuclear energy propertyâmeans property that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is part of a fixed location system that is used all or substantially all to generate electrical energy or heat energy, or a combination of electrical energy and heat energy, from nuclear fission as determined on an annual basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is located at a nuclear facility where, at the time the property becomes available for use, the total combined gross-rated thermal generating capacity of all planned and existing nuclear fission reactors at the facility is reasonably expected not to exceed 1,400 megawatts thermal;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor vessel,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor control rod,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a moderator,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: cooling equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: heat generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: nuclear fission fuel handling equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a containment structure,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: electrical generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the distribution of heat energy within the system, or

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (x) and that is ancillary equipment (such as control equipment) used solely to support the functioning of property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (x); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: nuclear fission fuel,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: property used in nuclear waste disposal or storage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: transmission equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: distribution equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a vehicle,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: property that would be included in Class 17 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations if that Class were read without reference to its paragraph (a.1),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used to export heat energy from the system, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a building or other structure.â(bien pour lâÃ©nergie nuclÃ©aire de petite taille)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.45(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: eligible bioenergy fuelâmeans fuel that is combusted in the operation of a system described in paragraph (a) of the definition waste biomass electricity generation equipment and that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specified waste material; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fuel that has been produced using equipment that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: part of the system, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: described in subparagraph (b)(v) or (vi) of the definition waste biomass electricity generation equipment.â(carburants admissibles pour la bioÃ©nergie)

Definition.Text: gaseous biofuelâhas the same meaning as in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations.â(biocarburants gazeux)
liquid biofuelâhas the same meaning as in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations.â(biocarburants liquides)
solid biofuelâhas the same meaning as in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations.â(biocarburants solides)
specified waste materialâhas the same meaning as in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations.â(dÃ©chets dÃ©terminÃ©s)
spent pulping liquorâhas the same meaning as in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations.â(liqueur rÃ©siduaire)
waste biomass electricity generation equipmentâmeans property that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is part of a system that meets the following conditions

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
