Subparagraph.Text: the system is used solely for the purpose of generating electrical energy, or a combination of electrical energy and heat energy, determined without reference to the recovery of chemicals from spent pulping liquor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system consumes material all or substantially all of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the material) of which is specified waste material, as determined on an annual basis,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is on a single site, or on contiguous sites or adjacent sites that function as a single integrated site, at which the activities described in subparagraphs (i) and (ii) are carried out, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the system meets the following heat rate on an annual basis

Formula.FormulaText: A â¥ (2 Ã B + C) Ã· (D + E Ã· F)

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 13,000 BTU per kilowatt-hour,
is the energy content of fossil fuel (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) consumed by the system in BTU,
is the energy content of eligible bioenergy fuel or any other fuel other than fossil fuel (expressed as the higher heating value of the fuel) consumed by the system in BTU,
is the gross electrical energy produced by the system in kilowatt-hours,
is the net useful energy in the form of heat exported from the system to a thermal host in BTU, and
is 3,412 BTU per kilowatt-hour;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: electrical generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: heat generating equipment used primarily for the purpose of producing heat energy to operate equipment described in subparagraph (i), determined without reference to the recovery of chemicals from spent pulping liquor,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that generates both electrical and heat energy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: heat recovery equipment used primarily for the purpose of conserving energy, or reducing the requirement to acquire energy, by extracting for reuse thermal waste that is generated by equipment described in this paragraph,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is used to produce solid biofuel, liquid biofuel or gaseous biofuel used solely to operate equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii) or (vi), from material all or substantially all of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the material) of which is specified waste material, as determined on an annual basis, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is described in any of subparagraphs (d)(xi), (xiii), (xvi) or (xx) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is used to upgrade the combustibility of specified waste material used all or substantially all to operate equipment described in this subparagraph or in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii) or (v),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the distribution of heat energy within the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) and that is ancillary equipment (such as control equipment) used primarily to support the functioning of equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (viii) that is incorporated into a system that would not otherwise be described in paragraph (a) if the incorporation causes the system to satisfy the description in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a building or other structure,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: transmission equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: distribution equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used to export heat energy from the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the storage of feedstock or fuel,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: pollution abatement equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a vehicle, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: property described in Class 57 or 58 of Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations. (matÃ©riel gÃ©nÃ©rateur dâÃ©lectricitÃ© Ã partir de dÃ©chets de biomasse)

Definition.Text: waste biomass heat generation equipmentâmeans property that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is part of a system that meets the following conditions

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is used solely for the purpose of generating heat energy,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system consumes material all or substantially all of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the material) of which is specified waste material, other than spent pulping liquor, as determined on an annual basis, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is on a single site, or on contiguous sites or adjacent sites that function as a single integrated site, at which the activities described in subparagraphs (i) and (ii) are carried out;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: heat generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is used to produce solid biofuel, liquid biofuel or gaseous biofuel used solely to operate equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) or (iii), from material all or substantially all of the energy content (expressed as the higher heating value of the material) of which is specified waste material, other than spent pulping liquor, as determined on an annual basis, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is described in any of subparagraphs (d)(xi), (xiii), (xvi) or (xx) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is used to upgrade the combustibility of specified waste material, other than spent pulping liquor, used all or substantially all to operate equipment described in this subparagraph or in subparagraph (i) or (ii),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the distribution of heat energy within the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iv) and that is ancillary equipment (such as control equipment) used primarily to support the functioning of equipment described in subparagraphs (i) to (iv), or

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v) that is incorporated into a system that would not otherwise be described in paragraph (a) if the incorporation causes the system to satisfy the description in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used for the purpose of producing heat energy to operate electrical generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a building or other structure,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used to export heat energy from the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the storage of feedstock or fuel,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: pollution abatement equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a vehicle, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: property described in any of Class 17, 57 or 58 of Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations.â(matÃ©riel gÃ©nÃ©rateur de chaleur Ã partir de dÃ©chets de biomasse)

Subsection.Text: Section 127.45 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interpretive rule â small nuclear energy property
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, where a qualifying taxpayer has a leasehold interest in a small nuclear energy property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (4), the taxpayer is deemed to acquire the property when it acquires the leasehold interest in the property;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost of the leasehold interest in the property to the taxpayer is deemed to be its capital cost of the property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the property is deemed to be disposed of by the taxpayer when it ceases to hold the leasehold interest in the property for proceeds of disposition equal to the fair market value of the property at the time it ceases to hold the leasehold interest.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.45(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A payment on account of tax payable shall not be deemed to be paid under subsection (2) if the taxpayer does not file with the Minister the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) in respect of the amount on or before the later of December 31, 2026 and the day that is one year after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year and, if the prescribed form is filed after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year, no payment by the taxpayer is deemed to arise under that subsection until the prescribed form containing the prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.
Paragraph 127.45(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not include any amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which an amount was previously deducted under this section by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which any other clean economy tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.47(1)) was deducted by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of any part of the capital cost of a property if a CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) or a clean hydrogen tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.48(1)) was deducted by any person in respect of that property,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: that has, by virtue of section 21, been added to the cost of a property, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: that is in respect of an expenditure incurred for a preliminary work activity;

Subsection.Text: Section 127.45 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

A property that would otherwise be clean technology property of a qualifying taxpayer is deemed not to be a clean technology property of the taxpayer if, at the time the property becomes available for use by the taxpayer, there is substantial non-compliance by the taxpayer with the requirements of any environmental law, by-law or regulation of Canada, a province, a municipality, or a municipal or public body performing a function of government in Canada that is applicable in respect of the property.
The following rules apply in respect of a qualifying taxpayerâs property described in subparagraph (d)(viii) of the definition clean technology property in subsection (1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where the property is temporarily operated in a manner that is a non-clean technology use solely because of a deficiency, failing or shutdown of the system of which it is a part, and that deficiency, failing or shutdown is beyond the control of the taxpayer, the property is deemed, for the purposes of subsections (11), (12), (16) and (17), not to be operated in a manner that is a non-clean technology use during the period of the deficiency, failing or shutdown, if the taxpayer makes all reasonable efforts to rectify the circumstances within a reasonable time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of paragraph (a), the system referred to in that paragraph may include property of another person or partnership if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property would reasonably be considered to be part of the system if the property were owned by the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the property utilizes electrical energy or heat energy obtained from the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operation of the property is necessary for the system to avoid operation in a manner that is a non-clean technology use, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: at the time the system first became operational, the deficiency, failing or shutdown in the operation of the property could not reasonably have been anticipated to occur within five calendar years after that time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.45(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, paragraph 12(1)(t), subsection 13(7.1), the description of I in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) and subsection 53(2) and sections 127.44, 127.48, 127.49, 127.491 and 129, the amount deemed under subsection (2) to have been paid by a taxpayer for a taxation year is deemed to have been deducted from the taxpayerâs tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year.
Subsection 127.45(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where a taxpayer has, in a particular taxation year, repaid (or has not received and can no longer reasonably be expected to receive) an amount of government assistance or non-government assistance that was applied to reduce the cost of a particular property under paragraph (5)(b.1) for a preceding taxation year, the amount repaid (or no longer expected to be received) is to be added to the cost to the taxpayer of a separate clean technology property that is deemed to be acquired in the particular year for the purposes of this section, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (11)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.
Subsection 127.45(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, where any part of the capital cost of a taxpayerâs particular clean technology property is unpaid on the day that is 180 days after the end of the taxation year in which a deduction in respect of a clean technology investment tax credit would otherwise be available in respect of the particular property, such amount is to be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: excluded from the capital cost of the particular property in the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: added to the capital cost of a separate clean technology property that is deemed to be acquired at the time the amount is paid, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (11)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.

Subsection.Text: Section 127.45 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (18):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Election by member to pay tax
Subsection.Text: A qualifying taxpayer that is a member of a partnership during a fiscal period of the partnership may elect, in prescribed form and manner, to add to its tax payable under this Part for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period the total amount of tax determined for that fiscal period because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of the partnership.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Joint and several, or solidary, liability
Subsection.Text: Each current or former member of a partnership is jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for any portion of the amount of tax â determined because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of the partnership for a fiscal period â that is not added to the tax payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of a qualifying taxpayer under subsection (17); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of a qualifying taxpayer because of subsection (18.1) and paid by the qualifying taxpayer by its filing-due date for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period.

Subsection.Text: If a particular taxpayer was, at the time that an amount is determined because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of a property of the partnership for a taxation year, no longer a member of the partnership, the particular taxpayerâs liability for tax because of subsection (18.2) is limited to the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined for the particular taxpayer under subsection (2) in respect of the property because of its membership in the partnership.
Subsections (1) to (3), (6) to (8), (10) to (12) and (15) to (17) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Subsections (4), (9) and (13) are deemed to have come into force on November 21, 2023.
Subsection (5) is deemed to have come into force on November 17, 2025.
Subsection (14) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
The definitions regular tax credit rate and specified tax credit in subsection 127.46(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: regular tax credit rateâmeans the specified percentage (as defined in subsections 127.44(1), 127.45(1), 127.48(1) and 127.491(1), as the case may be).â(taux du crÃ©dit dâimpÃ´t rÃ©gulier)

specified tax creditâmeans the CCUS tax credit under subsection 127.44(1), the clean technology investment tax credit under subsection 127.45(1), the clean hydrogen tax credit under subsection 127.48(1) and the clean electricity investment tax credit under subsection 127.491(1).â(crÃ©dit dâimpÃ´t dÃ©terminÃ©)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.46(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite sections 127.44, 127.45, 127.48 and 127.491, the applicable rate for each specified tax credit of an incentive claimant is the reduced tax credit rate unless the incentive claimant elects in prescribed form and manner to meet the prevailing wage requirements under subsection (3) and the apprenticeship requirements under subsection (5) for each installation taxation year in respect of the specified tax credit.
Subsection 127.46(15) of the Act is replaced by the following:

This section does not apply to the preparation or installation of clean technology property as defined in subsection 127.45(1) that is described in subparagraph (d)(i) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations or in Class 56 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on November 28, 2023 and applies to specified property prepared or installed on or after that date.

The definition clean economy allocation provision in subsection 127.47(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 127.491(12). (disposition dâallocation pour lâÃ©conomie propre)

Subsection.Text: The definition clean economy expenditure in subsection 127.47(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost of clean electricity property as determined under section 127.491. (dÃ©pense pour lâÃ©conomie propre)

Subsection.Text: The definition clean economy provision in subsection 127.47(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (e), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 127.491. (disposition pour lâÃ©conomie propre)

Subsection.Text: The definition clean economy tax credit in subsection 127.47(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a clean electricity investment tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.491(1)). (crÃ©dit dâimpÃ´t pour lâÃ©conomie propre)

Subsection.Text: Section 127.47 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

If the cost of a particular property of a partnership is eligible for more than one clean economy tax credit, the partnership may allocate each of those clean economy tax credits to the members of the partnership in accordance with this section and the clean economy allocation provisions, except that no member of the partnership is entitled to more than one clean economy tax credit in respect of that property unless the tax credits are the CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) and the clean hydrogen tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.48(1)) to the extent provided under sections 127.44 and 127.48.
Subsections (1) to (4) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsection (5) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
The definition non-hydrogen or ammonia use in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: non-hydrogen or ammonia useâmeans a use of a particular property at a particular time that would, if the property were acquired at that time, result in the property not being an eligible clean hydrogen property, determined without reference to paragraph (b) of that definition.â(utilisation autre que pour lâhydrogÃ¨ne ou lâammoniac)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (c)(vi) of the definition eligible clean hydrogen property in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: that is incorporated into another property that would not otherwise be described in subparagraphs (i) to (v) if the incorporation causes the other property to satisfy the description in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v). (bien admissible pour lâhydrogÃ¨ne propre)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (e) of the definition preliminary clean hydrogen work activity in subsection 127.48(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: clearing or excavating land, except excavation directly related to the installation of eligible clean hydrogen property.â(travaux prÃ©liminaires pour lâhydrogÃ¨ne propre)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, paragraph 12(1)(t), subsection 13(7.1), the description of I in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21), subsection 53(2) and sections 127.44, 127.45, 127.49, 127.491 and 129, the amount deemed under subsection (2) to have been paid by a taxpayer for a taxation year is deemed to have been deducted from the taxpayerâs tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year.
Subsection 127.48(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A payment on account of tax payable shall not be deemed to be paid under subsection (2) if the taxpayer does not file with the Minister the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) in respect of the amount on or before the later of December 31, 2026 and the day that is one year after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year and, if the prescribed form is filed after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year, no payment by the taxpayer is deemed to arise under that subsection until the prescribed form containing prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.
Paragraph 127.48(6)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (j), in applying the Fuel LCA Model, an assessment of emissions from the production of hydrogen by the project and upstream emissions from the production of inputs to the hydrogen-production process shall be taken into account;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 127.48(6)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer produces hydrogen from eligible hydrocarbons,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any captured carbon that is subject to an ineligible use is deemed not to be captured, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any captured carbon that is subject to an eligible use (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) is deemed to be permanently stored;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 127.48(6)(e) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if, in connection with hydrogen production, the taxpayer generates or purchases, or proposes to generate or purchase, electricity that is

Subsection.Text: Clause 127.48(6)(e)(i)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: on-site generation equipment that is used solely to convert any one or a combination of hydrogen, heat described in subparagraph (i)(i) or (ii) or eligible hydrocarbons (with carbon dioxide captured using a CCUS process) into electricity that supports the production of hydrogen from eligible hydrocarbons, the contribution of the electricity to carbon intensity is to be modelled as part of the project,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(6) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (h) and by replacing paragraph (i) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: if, in connection with hydrogen production or electricity production in support of hydrogen production, the taxpayer uses heat energy

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: recovered from hydrogen production, recovered from electricity production by the taxpayer in support of hydrogen production or produced by the taxpayer from the combustion of hydrogen or eligible hydrocarbons (with carbon dioxide captured using a CCUS process), the contribution of the heat to carbon intensity is to be modelled as part of the project,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: recovered from a non-hydrogen production process of the taxpayer, or purchased from a vendor that produced the heat from eligible hydrocarbons or recovered the waste heat from a production process, the contribution of the heat to carbon intensity is to correspond with the input carbon intensity of purchased steam in the Fuel LCA Model, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: from a source other than as described in subparagraph (i) or (ii), the carbon intensity of the project is deemed to be greater than 4.5;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the contribution to carbon intensity of the following may be disregarded:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: delivering, collecting, recovering, treating or recirculating water, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: energy used to compress hydrogen beyond 30 bar;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: emissions related to the production of the following substances or types of energy produced in conjunction with hydrogen shall be attributed to hydrogen production:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: off-gas (including tail gas and other fuel gas),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: oxygen,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: nitrogen, if the nitrogen is not used by the taxpayer in another production process or sold for commercial use, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: heat from hydrogen production;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: emissions related to heat produced in conjunction with electricity production by the taxpayer in support of hydrogen production shall be attributed to hydrogen production, if the heat is not used by the taxpayer in another production process or sold for commercial use;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: emissions associated with energy used in the purification of hydrogen shall be attributed to hydrogen production; and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the Clean Hydrogen Investment Tax Credit â Carbon Intensity Modelling Guidance Document published by the Government of Canada is to apply conclusively with respect to the calculation of carbon intensity, except as otherwise required under this section.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 127.48(10)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (iii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not include any amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which an amount was previously deducted under this section by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which any other clean economy tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.47(1)) was deducted by any person, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(11) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where a taxpayer has, in a particular taxation year, repaid (or has not received and can no longer reasonably be expected to receive) an amount of government assistance or non-government assistance that was applied to reduce the capital cost of a particular eligible clean hydrogen property under paragraph (10)(c) for a preceding taxation year, the amount repaid (or no longer expected to be received) is to be added to the cost to the taxpayer of a separate eligible clean hydrogen property that is deemed to be acquired in the particular year for the purposes of this section, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (21)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.
Subsection 127.48(13) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, where any part of the capital cost of a taxpayerâs particular eligible clean hydrogen property is unpaid on the day that is 180 days after the end of the taxation year in which a deduction in respect of a clean hydrogen tax credit would otherwise be available in respect of the particular property, such amount is to be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: excluded from the capital cost of the particular property in the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: added to the capital cost of a separate eligible clean hydrogen property that is deemed to be acquired by the taxpayer at the time the amount is paid, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (21)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Property deemed in respect of qualified project
Subsection.Text: A property is deemed to have been acquired in respect of a qualified clean hydrogen project if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property was acquired in respect of a clean hydrogen project that was not a qualified clean hydrogen project because the Minister of Natural Resources was not accepting the filing of clean hydrogen project plans during the taxation year in which the property was acquired; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in a subsequent taxation year, the project becomes a qualified clean hydrogen project.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.48(15) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a clean hydrogen tax credit was deducted in any taxation year by a taxpayer in respect of a qualified clean hydrogen project, the taxpayer shall file, with its return of income for each taxation year that begins or ends during the compliance period in respect of the project, a prescribed form containing prescribed information in respect of the operations of the project.
Section 127.48 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (16):

If more than one person is required by this section to file any documentation or information with the Minister or the Minister of Natural Resources in respect of a clean hydrogen project (including, but not limited to, a revised clean hydrogen project plan, a form described in subsection (15) or a compliance report described in subsection (16)), the filing with full and accurate disclosure by any one of such persons of the documentation or information is deemed to have been made by each person to whom the relevant requirement applies.
Subsection 127.48(25) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to section 127.47, if subsection (12) has at any time applied to add an amount in computing the clean hydrogen tax credit of a current or former member of a partnership, subsections (18) to (23) apply to determine amounts in respect of the partnership as if the partnership was a taxable Canadian corporation, its fiscal period were its taxation year and it had deducted all of the clean hydrogen tax credits that were previously added in computing the clean hydrogen tax credit of any member of the partnership because of the application of subsection (12) in respect of its partnership interest.
Subsections 127.48(27) and (28) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A taxable Canadian corporation that is a member of a partnership during a fiscal period of the partnership may elect, in prescribed form and manner, to add to its tax payable under this Part for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period the total amount of tax determined for that fiscal period because of subsection (25) in respect of the partnership.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Joint and several, or solidary, liability
Subsection.Text: Each current or former member of a partnership is jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for any portion of the amount of tax â determined because of subsection (25) in respect of the partnership for a fiscal period â that is not added to the tax payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of a qualifying taxpayer under subsection (26); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of a taxable Canadian corporation because of subsection (27) and paid by the corporation by its filing-due date for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period.

Subsection.Text: If a particular taxpayer was, at the time that an amount is determined because of subsection (25) in respect of the partnership for a taxation year, no longer a member of the partnership, the particular taxpayerâs liability for tax because of subsection (28) is limited to the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined for the particular taxpayer under subsection (2) because of its membership in the partnership.
Subsections (1) to (3) and (5) to (17) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Subsection (4) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
The definition CTM use in subsection 127.49(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: CTM useâmeans the use of a property all or substantially all in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: activities described in paragraph (a) or (c) of the definition qualified zero emission technology manufacturing activities in section 5202 of the Income Tax Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: activities described in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition qualifying mineral activity if the property is used to produce primarily qualifying materials, determined based on the value of all commercial outputs in accordance with subsection (2.2); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: activities described in any of paragraphs (c) to (f) of the definition qualifying mineral activity if the property is used to produce all or substantially all qualifying materials, determined based on the value of all commercial outputs in accordance with subsection (2.2).â(utilisation pour la FTP)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition CTM property in subsection 127.49(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of subparagraph (v), by adding âorâ at the end of subparagraph (vi) and by adding the following after subparagraph (vi):

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: is incorporated into another property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vi), as part of a refurbishment of the other property provided that on completion of the refurbishment the other property is still described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vi). (bien de FTP)

Subsection.Text: The definition qualifying material in subsection 127.49(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: antimony;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: gallium;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: germanium;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: indium; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: scandium. (matÃ©riau admissible)

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (b) to (e) of the definition qualifying mineral activity in subsection 127.49(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a specified mineral processing activity that is performed at a mine site or well site;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a specified mineral processing activity that is performed at a location other than a location described in paragraph (b);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a recycling activity that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: sorting, disassembly or shredding of a recyclable material, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a material processing activity substantially similar to a specified mineral processing activity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a synthetic graphite activity that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: performed during or after the graphitization stage, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a material processing activity substantially similar to a specified mineral processing activity; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: spheronization of graphite or coating of spheronized graphite.â(activitÃ© miniÃ¨re admissible)

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (b) to (f) of the definition specified percentage in subsection 127.49(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (a)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: after December 31, 2023 and before January 1, 2032, 30%,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: after December 31, 2031 and before January 1, 2033, 20%,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: after December 31, 2032 and before January 1, 2034, 10%, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: after December 31, 2033 and before January 1, 2035, 5%; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: after December 31, 2034, nil. (pourcentage dÃ©terminÃ©)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.49(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: independent engineer or geoscientistâmeans an individual who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a qualified professional engineer or professional geoscientist as defined in subsection 127(9); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is at all times at armâs length with, independent of, and not employed by, each taxpayer claiming a related CTM investment tax credit.â(ingÃ©nieur ou gÃ©oscientifique indÃ©pendant)

Definition.Text: refurbishmentâmeans significant alterations, renovations, improvements or additions to a property to substantially

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend its useful life;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: increase its capacity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: improve its efficiency.â(remise en Ã©tat)

Definition.Text: safe harbour priceâof a commercial output means the five-year historical average spot price, determined at the end of the taxation year of a taxpayer in which a CTM investment tax credit is deducted in respect of a CTM property, of that output calculated

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: using prices from a recognized commodities exchange; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if prices referred to in paragraph (a) are not available in respect of the output, in accordance with normal and accepted commercial practices in the industry.â(prix au titre de la rÃ¨gle dâexonÃ©ration)

Definition.Text: safe harbour price method, in respect of a CTM property of a taxpayer, means the determination, on an annual basis, of the value of all commercial outputs from the property based on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the safe harbour price of the expected commercial outputs from the property if the year for which the determination is made is prior to a year in which the property was used by the taxpayer in commercial production; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the safe harbour price of the actual commercial outputs from the property in a year in which the property was used by the taxpayer in commercial production.â(mÃ©thode de dÃ©termination du prix au titre de la rÃ¨gle dâexonÃ©ration)

Definition.Text: specified fair market value method, in respect of a CTM property, means the determination, on an annual basis, of the value of all the commercial outputs from the property based on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fair market value, determined at the end of the relevant taxation year, of the expected commercial outputs from the property if the year for which the determination is made is prior to a year in which the property was used by the taxpayer in commercial production; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fair market value, determined at the end of the relevant taxation year, of the actual commercial outputs from the property in a year in which the property was used by the taxpayer in commercial production.â(mÃ©thode de dÃ©termination de la juste valeur marchande dÃ©terminÃ©e)

Definition.Text: specified mineral processing activityâmeans a mineral processing activity (including crushing, grinding, milling, separation, sieving, screening, froth floatation, leaching, recrystallization, precipitation, drying, evaporation, heating, calcinating, roasting, smelting, casting of ingots, refining, purification, distillation, electrodeposition and surface roughening of electrodeposited foil) that occurs prior to or as part of a process intended to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: increase the purity of at least one qualifying material; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: produce a material with non-trace amounts of a single qualifying material, and without non-trace amounts of any elements other than permitted elements.â(activitÃ© de traitement des minÃ©raux dÃ©terminÃ©e)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.49(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding subsection (2), a CTM investment tax credit in respect of property used, or to be used, by the taxpayer or by a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member, in any activity described in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition qualifying mineral activity in subsection (1), is deemed to be nil unless a taxpayer files with the Minister, together with the form and information described in subsection (2), a certification by an independent engineer or geoscientist in respect of the taxpayer in the prescribed form attesting that the property is being used, or is to be used,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at a particular mine site or well site of the taxpayer or of a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member, as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in accordance with a plan that primarily targets qualifying materials, determined based on the value of all commercial outputs expected to be produced in accordance with subsection (2.2).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Valuation of qualifying mineral activity outputs
Subsection.Text: In the prescribed form referred to in subsection (2) that is filed by a taxpayer for a taxation year, the taxpayer must elect, in respect of each CTM property of the taxpayer for which the taxpayer claims a CTM investment tax credit for that year, that the determination of the value of all commercial outputs from that property is to be based on either

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the specified fair market value method, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the safe harbour price method.

Subsection.Text: A payment on account of tax payable shall not be deemed to be paid under subsection (2) if the taxpayer does not file with the Minister the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) in respect of the amount on or before the later of December 31, 2026 and the day that is one year after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year and, if the prescribed form is filed after the taxpayerâs filing-due date for the year, no payment by the taxpayer is deemed to arise under that subsection until the prescribed form containing the prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.
The portion of paragraph 127.49(5)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (iii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not include any amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which an amount was previously deducted under this section by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which any other clean economy tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.47(1)) was deducted by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of any part of the capital cost of a property if a CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) or a clean hydrogen tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.48(1)) was deducted by any person in respect of that property, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 127.49(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, paragraph 12(1)(t), subsection 13(7.1), the description of I in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) and subsection 53(2) and sections 127.44, 127.45, 127.48, 127.491 and 129, the amount deemed under subsection (2) to have been paid by a taxpayer for a taxation year is deemed to have been deducted from the taxpayerâs tax otherwise payable under this Part for the year.
Subsection 127.49(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where a taxpayer has, in a particular taxation year, repaid (or has not received and can no longer reasonably be expected to receive) an amount of government assistance or non-government assistance that was applied to reduce the cost of a particular property under paragraph (5)(c) for a preceding taxation year, the amount repaid (or no longer expected to be received) is to be added to the cost to the taxpayer of a separate CTM property that is deemed to be acquired in the particular year for the purposes of this section, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (11)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.
Subsection 127.49(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, where any part of the capital cost of a taxpayerâs particular CTM property is unpaid on the day that is 180 days after the end of the taxation year in which a deduction in respect of a CTM investment tax credit would otherwise be available in respect of the particular property, such amount is to be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: excluded from the capital cost of the particular property in the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: added to the capital cost of a separate CTM property that is deemed to be acquired at the time the amount is paid, provided that a transaction or event described in paragraph (11)(c) has not occurred in respect of the particular property.

Subsection.Text: Section 127.49 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (18):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Election by member to pay tax
Subsection.Text: A qualifying taxpayer that is a member of a partnership during a fiscal period of the partnership may elect, in prescribed form and manner, to add to its tax payable under this Part for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period the total amount of tax determined for that fiscal period because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of the partnership.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Joint and several, or solidary, liability
Subsection.Text: Each current or former member of a partnership is jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for any portion of the amount of tax â determined because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of the partnership for a fiscal period â that is not added to the tax payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of a qualifying taxpayer under subsection (17); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of a qualifying taxpayer because of subsection (18.1) and paid by the qualifying taxpayer by its filing-due date for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period.

Subsection.Text: If a particular taxpayer was, at the time that an amount is determined because of subsections (16) and (17) in respect of a property of the partnership for a taxation year, no longer a member of the partnership, the particular taxpayerâs liability for tax because of subsection (18.2) is limited to the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined for the particular taxpayer under subsection (2) in respect of the property because of its membership in the partnership.
Subsections (1), (2), (4) to (8) and (10) to (12) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsection (3) applies in respect of property that is acquired and becomes available for use on or after November 4, 2025.
Subsection (9) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 127.49:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: actual emission intensityâmeans the emission intensity of a specified natural gas energy system of a qualifying entity, based on the actual carbon dioxide emissions from the production of electrical energy by the system.â(intensitÃ© des Ã©missions rÃ©elle)
average actual emission intensityâmeans, for the compliance period of a qualified natural gas energy system, the number determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: ((A Ã B) + (C Ã D) + (E Ã F) + (G Ã H) + (I Ã J)) Ã· K

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the actual emission intensity of the system for the first operating year of the compliance period;
is the quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system in the first operating year of the compliance period;
is the actual emission intensity of the system for the second operating year of the compliance period;
is the quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system in the second operating year of the compliance period;
is the actual emission intensity of the system for the third operating year of the compliance period;
is the quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system in the third operating year of the compliance period;
is the actual emission intensity of the system for the fourth operating year of the compliance period;
is the quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system in the fourth operating year of the compliance period;
is the actual emission intensity of the system for the fifth operating year of the compliance period;
is the quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system in the fifth operating year of the compliance period; and
is the total quantity, in gigawatt hours, of electrical energy produced by the system during the compliance period.â(intensitÃ© des Ã©missions rÃ©elle moyenne)

Definition.Text: clean electricity investment tax creditâof a qualifying entity for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the specified percentage of the capital cost to the qualifying entity of clean electricity property that is acquired by the entity in the year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount required by subsection (12) to be added in computing the entityâs clean electricity investment tax credit at the end of the year.â(crÃ©dit dâimpÃ´t Ã lâinvestissement pour lâÃ©lectricitÃ© propre)

Definition.Text: clean electricity propertyâmeans property of a qualifying entity

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is not part of a project the construction of which started before March 28, 2023 (and for this purpose, construction does not include obtaining permits or regulatory approval, conducting environmental assessments, community consultations or impact assessment studies or similar activities);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is situated in Canada (including property described in subparagraph (d)â(v) or (xiv) of Class 43.â1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations that is installed in the exclusive economic zone of Canada) and intended for use exclusively in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that has not been used, or acquired for use or lease, for any purpose whatever before it was acquired by the entity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that, if it is to be leased by a qualifying entity to another person or partnership, is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: leased to a qualifying entity or a partnership all the members of which are qualifying entities, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: leased in the ordinary course of carrying on a business in Canada by the qualifying entity whose principal business is selling or servicing property of that type, or whose principal business is leasing property, lending money, purchasing conditional sales contracts, accounts receivable, bills of sale, chattel mortgages or hypothecary claims on movables, bills of exchange or other obligations representing all or part of the sale price of merchandise or services, or any combination thereof; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: property that would be described in subparagraph (d)(ii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations, if that subparagraph were read without reference to its clause (A),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: described in subparagraph (d)(v), (vi) or (xiv) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations, but excluding a test wind turbine (within the meaning assigned by subsection 1219(3) of the Income Tax Regulations),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: concentrated solar energy equipment, as defined in subsection 127.45(1), that is part of a system used solely for the purpose of generating electrical energy, exclusively from concentrated sunlight,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: nuclear energy property,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is part of a system that

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: exports more electrical energy than heat energy on a net basis, as determined on an annual basis, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: does not extract fossil fuels for sale,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is used exclusively for the purpose of generating electrical energy, or a combination of electrical energy and heat energy, solely from geothermal energy, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: is described in subparagraph (d)(vii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: waste biomass electricity generation equipment, as defined in subsection 127.45(1), that is part of a system that exports more electrical energy than heat energy on a net basis, as determined on an annual basis,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: described in subparagraph (d)(xviii) or (xix) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations, but excluding equipment that uses any fossil fuel in operation,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: qualified natural gas energy equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: qualified interprovincial transmission equipment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: incorporated into another property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (ix), as part of a refurbishment of the other property provided that on completion of the refurbishment the other property is still described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (ix).â(bien pour lâÃ©lectricitÃ© propre)

Definition.Text: compliance periodâin respect of a specified natural gas energy system of a taxpayer, means the period beginning on the start-up date of the system and ending on the last day of the fifth operating year of the system.â(pÃ©riode de conformitÃ©)
dedicated geological storageâhas the same meaning as in subsection 127.44(1).â(stockage gÃ©ologique dÃ©diÃ©)
designated provincial Crown corporationâmeans a corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not less than 90% of the shares (except directorsâ qualifying shares) or of the capital of which is owned by one or more persons each of which is His Majesty in right of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is Northwest Territories Power Corporation, Qulliq Energy Corporation or Yukon Energy Corporation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all of the shares (except directorsâ qualifying shares) or of the capital of which is owned by one or more persons each of which is a corporation described in paragraph (a) or (b).â(sociÃ©tÃ© dâÃtat provinciale dÃ©signÃ©e)

Definition.Text: emission intensityâin respect of a qualified natural gas energy system, means the tonnes of carbon dioxide emissions released into the atmosphere for each gigawatt hour of electrical energy produced as determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number determined by the formula

is the quantity of carbon dioxide emissions, expressed in tonnes, during an operating year, from the combustion of fuel in the system, as determined in a manner that is acceptable to the Minister of Natural Resources,

is the quantity of carbon dioxide emissions, expressed in tonnes, attributable to the production of useful thermal energy exported by the system, during the operating year, as determined in a manner that is acceptable to the Minister of Natural Resources, and

is the quantity of carbon dioxide captured from the system and stored in dedicated geological storage, expressed in tonnes, during the operating year, as determined in a manner that is acceptable to the Minister of Natural Resources; and

is the quantity of electrical energy produced by the system during the operating year, expressed in gigawatt hours, as determined in a manner that is acceptable to the Minister of Natural Resources.â(intensitÃ© des Ã©missions)

Definition.Text: government assistanceâhas the same meaning as in subsection 127(9).â(aide gouvernementale)
ineligible useâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a property other than qualified natural gas energy equipment, use of the property at a time that would, if the property were acquired at that time, result in the property not being a clean electricity property, determined without reference to paragraph (c) of the definition clean electricity property; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a property that is qualified natural gas energy equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: use of the property at a time that would, if the property were acquired at that time, result in the property not being a clean electricity property, determined without reference to subparagraph (a)(vi) of the definition qualified natural gas energy equipment and paragraph (c) of the definition clean electricity property, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any use of the system described in paragraph (a) of the definition qualified natural gas energy equipment, if the actual emission intensity of the system in an operating year is greater than 65 tonnes of carbon dioxide per gigawatt hour of electrical energy, for an operating year that begins after the fifth operating year but before the twenty-first operating year of the system.â(utilisation non admissible)

Definition.Text: non-government assistanceâhas the same meaning as in subsection 127(9).â(aide non gouvernementale)
nuclear energy propertyâmeans property that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is part of a fixed location system that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is used all or substantially all to generate electrical energy, or a combination of electrical energy and heat energy, from nuclear fission, as determined on an annual basis, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: exports more electrical energy than heat energy on a net basis, as determined on an annual basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor vessel,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a reactor control rod,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a moderator,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: cooling equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: heat generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: nuclear fission fuel handling equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a containment structure,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: electrical generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the distribution of heat energy within the system, or

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (x) and that is ancillary equipment (such as control equipment) used solely to support the functioning of property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (x); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: nuclear fission fuel,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: property used in nuclear waste disposal or storage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: transmission equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: distribution equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a vehicle,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: property that would be included in Class 17 in Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations if that Class were read without reference to its paragraph (a.â1),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used to export heat energy from the system, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a building or other structure.â(bien pour lâÃ©nergie nuclÃ©aire)

Definition.Text: operating yearâof a specified natural gas energy system, means each cumulative 365-day period, the first of which begins on the start-up date of a qualifying entityâs specified natural gas energy system, disregarding any period during which the system is not operating.ââ(annÃ©e dâexploitation)

preliminary work activityâhas the same meaning as in subsection 127.45(1), except that the reference to âclean technology propertyâ in paragraph (c) shall be read as âclean electricity propertyâ.â(travaux prÃ©liminaires)

qualified interprovincial transmission equipmentâmeans property that is primarily used, as determined on an annual basis, to transmit or manage electrical energy that originates in, or is destined for, a province other than the province in which the property is located and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the transmission of electrical energy, including cables and switches, that is rated for voltages of at least 69 kilovolts,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: electrical transmission structures, including towers and lattices, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: related equipment used to manage electrical energy, including transformers, electric power conditioning equipment and control equipment, that is directly connected to equipment described in subparagraph (i) or (ii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is not a building or distribution equipment.â(matÃ©riel de transmission interprovinciale admissible)

Definition.Text: qualified natural gas energy equipmentâmeans property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is part of a system that meets the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the system

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is fuelled all or substantially all by the combustion of natural gas, as determined on an annual basis, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is not fuelled by anything other than the combustion of gaseous fuels,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is used solely for the purpose of generating electrical energy, or a combination of electrical energy and heat energy, determined without reference to capturing carbon dioxide,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the system exports more electrical energy than heat energy on a net basis as determined on an annual basis,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is physically and functionally integrated with equipment that captures and prepares or compresses carbon dioxide for transportation,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: less than 50% of the gross electrical energy generated by the system is used to power the equipment referred to in subparagraph (iv) as determined on an annual basis,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the system is not expected to exceed an emission intensity of 65 tonnes of carbon dioxide per gigawatt hour of gross electrical energy generated, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a system evaluation has been issued for the system by the Minister of Natural Resources, in the form and manner determined by the Minister of Natural Resources;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: electrical generating equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: heat generating equipment used primarily for the purpose of producing heat energy to operate the electrical generating equipment described in subparagraph (i),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that generates both electrical and heat energy,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is to be used solely

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: for capturing carbon dioxide that is generated by the system, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: to prepare or compress for transportation carbon dioxide captured from the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: heat recovery equipment used primarily for the purpose of conserving energy, or reducing the requirement to acquire energy, by extracting for reuse thermal waste that is generated by equipment referred to in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iv) or (vi),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment for the distribution of heat energy within the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment that is physically and functionally integrated with equipment described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vi) and that is ancillary equipment (such as control equipment) used solely to support the functioning of equipment described in any of those subparagraphs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) that is incorporated into a system that would not otherwise be described in paragraph (a) if the incorporation causes the system to satisfy the description in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of equipment that is acquired before the start-up date of the system referred to in paragraph (a), that is verified by the Minister of Natural Resources as being equipment described in paragraph (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that is not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a building or other structure,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: transmission equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: distribution equipment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: equipment used to export heat energy from the system,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: fuel storage or fuel handling equipment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: pollution abatement equipment.â(matÃ©riel dâÃ©nergie alimentÃ© au gaz naturel admissible)

Definition.Text: qualified natural gas energy systemâmeans a system that is described in paragraph (a) of the definition qualified natural gas energy equipment.â(systÃ¨me Ã©nergÃ©tique alimentÃ© au gaz naturel admissible)
qualified validation firm, in respect of a specified natural gas energy system of a taxpayer, means an engineer or engineering firm that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is registered and in good standing with a professional association that has the authority or recognition by law of a jurisdiction in Canada to regulate the profession of engineering in

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the jurisdiction where the system is located, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is no professional association in the jurisdiction described in subparagraph (i), a jurisdiction in Canada where a professional association regulates the profession of engineering;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has appropriate insurance coverage;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at all times, is independent of, deals at armâs length with and is not an employee of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: meets the requirements described in guidelines published by the Minister of Natural Resources.â(firme admissible de validation)

Definition.Text: qualified verification firm, in respect of a specified natural gas energy system of a taxpayer, means an individual or firm that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an engineer or an engineering firm that is registered and in good standing with a professional association that has the authority or recognition by law of a jurisdiction in Canada to regulate the profession of engineering in

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the jurisdiction where the system is located, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if there is no professional association in the jurisdiction described in clause (A), a jurisdiction in Canada where a professional association regulates the profession of engineering, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a verification body accredited and in good standing under the Clean Fuel Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has appropriate insurance coverage;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at all times, is independent of, deals at armâs length with and is not an employee of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the qualified validation firm in respect of the system;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: meets the requirements described in guidelines published by the Minister of Natural Resources; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: has expertise in auditing natural gas systems to demonstrate compliance with the Regulations Limiting Carbon Dioxide Emissions from Natural Gas-fired Generation of Electricity.â(firme admissible de vÃ©rification)

Definition.Text: qualifying corporationâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a taxable Canadian corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a designated provincial Crown corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation described in paragraph 149(1)(d.5) not less than 90% of the shares or the capital of which are owned by one or more entities each of which is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality in Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an aboriginal government (as defined in subsection 241(10)) â or a similar Indigenous governing body â described in paragraph 149(1)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation described in paragraph 149(1)(d.6) all of the shares (except directorsâ qualifying shares) or the capital of which are owned by one or more of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an aboriginal government (as defined in subsection 241(10)) â or a similar Indigenous governing body â described in paragraph 149(1)(c), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation described in paragraph 149(1)(d.5);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation all of the shares (except directorsâ qualifying shares) or of the capital of which are owned by one or more municipalities in Canada in combination with one or more persons each of which is described in the definition designated provincial Crown corporation; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation to which paragraph 149(1)(o.2) applies.â(sociÃ©tÃ© admissible)

Definition.Text: qualifying entityâmeans a qualifying corporation or a qualifying trust.â(entitÃ© admissible)
qualifying trustâmeans, at all relevant times, a trust

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each beneficiary of which is a corporation described in paragraph 149(1)(o.2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is a limited partner of a partnership; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the sole undertaking of which is the holding of its interest in the partnership together with any ancillary activities.â(fiducie admissible)

Definition.Text: refurbishmentâmeans significant alterations, renovations, improvements or additions to a property to substantially

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend its useful life;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: increase its capacity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: improve its efficiency.â(remise en Ã©tat)

Definition.Text: specified natural gas energy systemâmeans a system that is, or was at any time, a qualified natural gas energy system.â(systÃ¨me Ã©nergÃ©tique alimentÃ© au gaz naturel dÃ©terminÃ©)
specified percentageâmeans, in respect of a clean electricity property of a qualifying entity that is acquired

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before April 16, 2024, determined without reference to subsection (7), nil;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (a), on or after April 16, 2024 and before January 1, 2035, 15%; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: after December 31, 2034, nil.â(pourcentage dÃ©terminÃ©)

Definition.Text: start-up dateâof a specified natural gas energy system means the first day on which the system generates electrical energy for sale.â(jour du dÃ©but du projet)
system planâmeans a plan for a qualified natural gas energy system of a qualifying entity that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has been prepared by a qualified validation firm;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: includes a front-end engineering design study (or an equivalent study as determined by the Minister of Natural Resources) for the system;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sets out

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an expected emission intensity of the electrical energy to be produced by the system that is below 65 tonnes carbon dioxide per gigawatt hour,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an expected ratio of net electrical energy to net heat energy exported that is above 1, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an expected ratio of electrical energy used to power equipment that captures and prepares or compresses carbon dioxide to gross electrical energy produced that is below 0.5;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contains any information required in guidelines published by the Minister of Natural Resources; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is filed by the entity with the Minister of Natural Resources, in the form and manner determined by the Minister of Natural Resources.â(plan du systÃ¨me)

Subsection.Text: If a qualifying entity files a prescribed form containing prescribed information with the Minister on or before its filing-due date for its taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an entity that is a taxable Canadian corporation or a qualifying trust, the entity is deemed to have paid on its balance-due day for the year an amount on account of the entityâs tax payable under this Part for the year equal to the entityâs clean electricity investment tax credit for the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (3), in the case of an entity that is described in any of paragraphs (b) to (f) of the definition qualifying corporation in subsection (1), the Minister shall, with all due dispatch, pay to the entity an amount equal to its clean electricity investment tax credit for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply to an entity that is described in any of paragraphs (b) to (f) of the definition qualifying corporation in subsection (1) unless the entity agrees in writing with the Minister to be subject to the provisions of this Act in respect of the entityâs clean electricity investment tax credit, with any modifications to those provisions that the circumstances require.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interpretive rule â nuclear energy property
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, where a qualifying entity has a leasehold interest in a nuclear energy property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (7), the entity is deemed to acquire the property when it acquires the leasehold interest in the property;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost of the leasehold interest in the property to the entity is deemed to be its capital cost of the property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the property is deemed to be disposed of by the entity when it ceases to hold the leasehold interest in the property for proceeds of disposition equal to the fair market value of the property at the time it ceases to hold the leasehold interest.

Subsection.Text: Any amount payable by a qualifying entity under this section is deemed to be payable as a tax or as a payment in lieu of tax, as the case may be.

If the qualifying entity files with the Minister the prescribed form containing prescribed information referred to in subsection (2) after its filing-due date for the year but on or before the later of the day that is one year after that date and December 31, 2026, subsection (2) applies to the entity except that no payment is deemed to arise under that subsection until the prescribed form containing prescribed information has been filed with the Minister.

For the purpose of this section, clean electricity property is deemed not to have been acquired by a qualifying entity before the property is considered to have become available for use by the entity, determined without reference to paragraphs 13(27)(c) and (28)(d).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Qualified natural gas energy system evaluation
Subsection.Text: The Minister of Natural Resources may request from a qualifying entity all documentation and information necessary for the Minister of Natural Resources to complete a qualified natural gas energy system evaluation, including a system plan, and may refuse to complete the evaluation if such documentation or information is not provided by the entity.
For the purpose of the definition clean electricity investment tax credit in subsection (1), the capital cost of a clean electricity property to a qualifying entity shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not include any amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which an amount was previously deducted under this section by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which any other clean economy tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.47(1)) was deducted by any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: that has, by virtue of section 21, been added to the cost of a property,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
