Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
The definitions armâs length allocation, armâs length transfer price, tax benefit and transfer price in subsection 247(1) of the Act are repealed.
The definitions transfer pricing income adjustment and transfer pricing income setoff adjustment in subsection 247(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: transfer pricing income adjustmentâof a taxpayer for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, if any, by which an adjustment made under subsection (2.02) (other than an adjustment included in determining a transfer pricing capital adjustment of the taxpayer for a taxation year) would result in an increase in the taxpayerâs income for the year or a decrease in a loss of the taxpayer for the year from a source if that adjustment were the only adjustment made under subsection (2.02).â(redressement de revenu)

transfer pricing income setoff adjustmentâof a taxpayer for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, if any, by which an adjustment made under subsection (2.02) (other than an adjustment included in determining a transfer pricing capital setoff adjustment of the taxpayer for a taxation year) would result in a decrease in the taxpayerâs income for the year or an increase in a loss of the taxpayer for the year from a source if that adjustment were the only adjustment made under subsection (2.02).â(redressement compensatoire de revenu)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(i) of the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection 247(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 1/2 of the amount, if any, by which the adjusted cost base to the taxpayer of a capital property (other than a depreciable property) is reduced in the year because of an adjustment made under subsection (2.02), or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(iii) of the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection 247(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the capital cost to the taxpayer of a depreciable property is reduced in the year because of an adjustment made under subsection (2.02); and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection 247(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 1/2 of the amount, if any, by which the adjusted cost base to a partnership of a capital property (other than a depreciable property) is reduced in a fiscal period that ends in the year because of an adjustment made under subsection (2.02), and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(iii) of the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection 247(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the capital cost to a partnership of a depreciable property is reduced in the period because of an adjustment made under subsection (2.02),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: actual conditions, in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, means the conditions that actually apply between any of the participants in the transaction or series.â(conditions rÃ©elles)

armâs length conditions, in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, means the conditions that would have applied had the participants been dealing at armâs length in comparable circumstances, including the possibility that no transaction or series, or a different transaction or series, would have been concluded had the participants been dealing at armâs length in comparable circumstances.â(conditions de pleine concurrence)
economically relevant characteristics, in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that the following contractual terms are not inconsistent with the actual conduct of the participants in the transaction or series,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the contractual terms of the transaction or series, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contractual terms of each other transaction or series that is relevant to the transaction or series and that involves at least one of the participants or any other member of the multinational enterprise group;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the actual conduct of the participants in the transaction or series, and in particular the functions performed by those participants, taking into account

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: assets used and risks assumed,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: how those functions relate to the wider generation of value by the multinational enterprise group to which the participants belong,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: circumstances surrounding the transaction or series, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: industry practices;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the characteristics of any property transferred or service provided;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the economic circumstances of the participants and of the market in which the participants operate; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the business strategies pursued by the participants.â(caractÃ©ristiques Ã©conomiquement pertinentes)

Definition.Text: multinational enterprise groupâmeans the group made up of the taxpayer or the partnership, or member of the partnership, and the non-resident person (or a partnership of which the non-resident person is a member) who are participants in a transaction or series of transactions referred to in subsection (2), as well as any other person that does not deal at armâs length with at least one of the participants.â(groupe dâentreprises multinationales)
Transfer Pricing Guidelinesâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if no text is prescribed under paragraph (b), the OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises and Tax Administrations, as adopted by the Committee on Fiscal Affairs on January 7, 2022; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any text prescribed by regulation.â(Principes applicables en matiÃ¨re de prix de transfert)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this section, a transaction or series of transactions is to be analyzed and determined with reference to the economically relevant characteristics of the transaction or series.

For the purposes of the definitions actual conditions and armâs length conditions in subsection (1), the word âconditionsâ is to be interpreted broadly, and includes, but is not limited to, price, rate, gross margin, net margin, the division of profit, contributions to costs and any commercial or financial information relevant to the determination of the quantum or nature of initial amounts or adjusted amounts, as the case may be.
Subsection (2.02) applies to a taxpayer or a partnership in respect of a transaction or series of transactions if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer or the partnership and a non-resident person with whom the taxpayer or the partnership, or a member of the partnership, does not deal at armâs length (or a partnership of which the non-resident person is a member) are participants in the transaction or series; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series includes actual conditions different from armâs length conditions.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer pricing adjustment â deeming rule
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (2)(b), a transaction or series of transactions is deemed to include actual conditions different from armâs length conditions if a condition does not exist in respect of the transaction or series, but would have existed had the participants in the transaction or series been dealing at armâs length in comparable circumstances.

If this subsection applies to a taxpayer or a partnership in respect of a transaction or series of transactions, any amounts (in this section referred to as the âinitial amountsâ) that would be determined for the purposes of applying the provisions of this Act (if this Act were read without reference to this section and section 245) in respect of the taxpayer or the partnership for a taxation year or fiscal period are to be adjusted (in this section referred to as an âadjustmentâ) to the quantum or nature of the amounts (in this section referred to as the âadjusted amountsâ) that would have been determined if armâs length conditions in respect of the transaction or series had applied.

For the purposes of determining the effect of this Part in relation to a taxpayer or a partnership, each of the analysis and determination of a transaction or series of transactions under subsection (1.1), the identification of armâs length conditions under paragraph (2)(b) and the determination of amounts under subsection (2.02) are to be made so as to best achieve consistency with the Transfer Pricing Guidelines.

For the purposes of this Part, whether a transaction or series of transactions includes actual conditions that differ from armâs length conditions is to be determined through an analysis where the most appropriate method is selected and applied in accordance with the Transfer Pricing Guidelines.
The portion of subsection 247(2.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of applying subsection (2.02) in the context of the other provisions of this Act, the following order is to be applied:
Paragraph 247(2.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: then apply each of the provisions of this Act (other than subsection (2.02) and, for greater certainty, including section 245) using the adjusted amounts.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 247(3)(a)(ii) and (iii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the portion of the taxpayerâs transfer pricing capital adjustment or transfer pricing income adjustment for the year that can reasonably be considered to relate to a particular transaction or series of transactions, where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the transaction or series is a qualifying cost contribution arrangement in which the taxpayer or a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member is a participant, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in any other case, the taxpayer or a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member made reasonable efforts to determine amounts that are based on armâs length conditions in respect of the transaction or series, and to use those amounts for the purposes of this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts, each of which is the portion of the taxpayerâs transfer pricing capital setoff adjustment or transfer pricing income setoff adjustment for the year that can reasonably be considered to relate to a particular transaction or series, where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the transaction or series is a qualifying cost contribution arrangement in which the taxpayer or a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member is a participant, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in any other case, the taxpayer or a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member made reasonable efforts to determine amounts that are based on armâs length conditions in respect of the transaction or series, and to use those amounts for the purposes of this Act,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 247(3)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 10% of the amount that would be the taxpayerâs gross revenue for the year if this Act were read without reference to subsection (2.02), subsections 69(1) and (1.2) and section 245, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (3) and the definition qualifying cost contribution arrangement in subsection (1), a taxpayer or a partnership is deemed not to have made reasonable efforts to determine and use amounts that are based on armâs length conditions in respect of a transaction or series of transactions or not to have participated in a transaction or series that is a qualifying cost contribution arrangement, unless the taxpayer or the partnership, as the case may be,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: makes or obtains, on or before the taxpayerâs or partnershipâs documentation-due date for the taxation year or fiscal period, as the case may be, in which the transaction or series is entered into, records or documents that provide a description that is complete and accurate in all material respects of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property or services to which the transaction or series relates,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contractual terms of the transaction or series and their relationship, if any, to the contractual terms of each other transaction or series that is relevant to the transaction or series and that involves at least one of the participants or any other member of the multinational enterprise group,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the identity of the participants and their relationship to each other at the time the transaction or series was entered into,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the functions performed by each of the participants in the transaction or series, based on their actual conduct, taking into account

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: assets used and risks assumed,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: how those functions relate to the wider generation of value by the multinational enterprise group to which the participants belong,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: circumstances surrounding the transaction or series, and

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: industry practices,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the data and methods considered and the analysis performed to determine amounts that are based on armâs length conditions and to select and apply the most appropriate method in accordance with the Transfer Pricing Guidelines in respect of the transaction or series, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the economic circumstances, assumptions, policies and business strategies, if any, that influenced the determination of the amounts that are based on armâs length conditions in respect of the transaction or series;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for each subsequent taxation year or fiscal period, if any, in which the transaction or series continues, makes or obtains, on or before the taxpayerâs or partnershipâs documentation-due date for that year or period, as the case may be, records or documents that completely and accurately describe each material change in the year or period to the matters referred to in any of subparagraphs (a)(i) to (vi) in respect of the transaction or series; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provides the records or documents described in paragraphs (a) and (b) to the Minister within 30 days after service, made personally or by registered or certified mail, of a written request therefor.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (4) does not apply to a taxpayer or partnership for a particular taxation year or fiscal period in respect of a transaction or series if the taxpayer or partnership

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: meets prescribed conditions; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: makes, obtains and provides prescribed documentation in prescribed manner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exclusion â loans to certain controlled foreign affiliates
Subsection.Text: Where, in a taxation year of a corporation resident in Canada, a non-resident person owes an amount to the corporation, the non-resident person is a controlled foreign affiliate of the corporation for the purpose of section 17 throughout the period in the year during which the amount is owing and it is established that the amount owing is an amount owing described in paragraph 17(8)(a) or (b), subsection (2.02) does not apply to adjust the amount of interest paid, payable or accruing in the year on the amount owing.
The portion of subsection 247(7.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection (2.02) does not apply to adjust an amount of consideration paid, payable or accruing to a corporation resident in Canada (in this subsection referred to as the âparentâ) in a taxation year of the parent for the provision of a guarantee to a person or partnership (in this subsection referred to as the âlenderâ) for the repayment, in whole or in part, of a particular amount owing to the lender by a non-resident person, if
Subsection 247(10) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An adjustment (other than an adjustment that results in or increases a transfer pricing capital adjustment or a transfer pricing income adjustment of a taxpayer for a taxation year) shall not be made under subsection (2.02) unless, in the opinion of the Minister, the circumstances are such that it would be appropriate that the adjustment be made.
Subparagraph 247(12)(b)(i) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of clause (A) and by replacing clause (B) with the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection (1) were read without reference to the references therein to â1/2 ofâ, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the definition transfer pricing income adjustment in subsection (1) were read as follows: âof a taxpayer for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, if any, that would result, from an adjustment made under subsection (2.02) (other than an adjustment included in determining a transfer pricing capital adjustment of the taxpayer for a taxation year), in an increase in the taxpayerâs income for the year or a decrease in a loss of the taxpayer for the year from a source if that adjustment were the only adjustment made under subsection (2.02)â

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 247(12)(b)(ii) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of clause (A) and by replacing clause (B) with the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the definition transfer pricing capital adjustment in subsection (1) were read without reference to the references therein to â1/2 ofâ,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the definition transfer pricing income adjustment in subsection (1) were read as follows: âof a taxpayer for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, if any, that would result, from an adjustment made under subsection (2.02) (other than an adjustment included in determining a transfer pricing capital adjustment of the taxpayer for a taxation year), in an increase in the taxpayerâs income for the year or a decrease in a loss of the taxpayer for the year from a source if that adjustment were the only adjustment made under subsection (2.02)â, and

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: the definition transfer pricing income setoff adjustment in subsection (1) were read as follows: âof a taxpayer for a taxation year means the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, if any, that would result, from an adjustment made under subsection (2.02) (other than an adjustment included in determining a transfer pricing capital set-off adjustment of the taxpayer for the taxation year), in a decrease in the taxpayerâs income for the year or in an increase in a loss of the taxpayer for the year from a source if that adjustment were the only adjustment made under subsection (2.02)â.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (18) apply to taxation years and fiscal periods that begin after November 4, 2025.

The definitions recognized derivatives exchange, specified mutual fund trust, specified synthetic equity arrangement, synthetic equity arrangement chain and tax-indifferent investor in subsection 248(1) of the Act are repealed.
The definition superannuation or pension benefit in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: superannuation or pension benefitâincludes any amount received out of or under a superannuation or pension fund or plan (including, except for the purposes of subparagraph 56(1)(a)(i), a pooled registered pension plan) and, without restricting the generality of the foregoing, includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any payment made to a beneficiary under the fund or plan or to an employer or former employer of the beneficiary under the fund or plan

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in accordance with the terms of the fund or plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: resulting from an amendment to or modification of the fund or plan, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: resulting from the termination of the fund or plan, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any payment made to an individual by an unclaimed property authority, if an amount in respect of the payment had been paid to the unclaimed property authority directly from a registered pension plan, an RRSP or a RRIF in respect of an unlocated individual;â(prestation de retraite ou de pension)

Subsection.Text: Subclause (b)(i)(B)(I) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: a tax-indifferent (as defined in subsection 18.2(1)), or

Subsection.Text: The definition dividend rental arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b.1), by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph (d).
Paragraph (j) of the definition employee ownership trust in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: all or substantially all the fair market value of the property of the trust is derived, directly or indirectly, from shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of one or more qualifying businesses that the trust controls and that carry on an active business; (fiducie collective des employÃ©s)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition qualifying business in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that deals at armâs length and is not affiliated with any person (other than a subject corporation referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying business transfer that controlled and wholly-owned the corporation immediately before the time the trust acquired control of the corporation) or partnership that owned, directly or indirectly, 50% or more of the fair market value of the shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of the corporation immediately before the time the trust acquired control of the corporation;â(entreprise admissible)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying business transfer in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before the disposition, all or substantially all the fair market value of the assets of the subject corporation is derived, directly or indirectly, from assets (other than an interest in a partnership) that are used principally in an active business (referred to in this definition as the âbusinessâ) carried on by the subject corporation or a corporation that is controlled and wholly-owned by the subject corporation,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs (a)(i) and (ii) of the definition synthetic equity arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entered into by the particular person, by a person or partnership that does not deal at armâs length with, or is affiliated with, the particular person (referred to in this definition as a âconnected personâ) or, for greater certainty, by any combination of the particular person and connected persons, with one or more persons or partnerships (referred to in this definition as a âcounterpartyâ),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: have the effect, or would have the effect, if each agreement entered into by a connected person were entered into by the particular person, of eliminating all or substantially all the particular personâs risk of loss and opportunity for gain or profit in respect of the DRA share, and, for greater certainty, opportunity for gain or profit includes rights to, benefits from and distributions on a share, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition synthetic equity arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph (b) of the definition zero-emission vehicle in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired, and becomes available for use, by the taxpayer after March 18, 2019 and before 2034,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition zero-emission vehicle in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: would be an accelerated investment incentive property or a reaccelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer if subsections 1104(4) and (4.01) of the Income Tax Regulations were read without their exclusions for property included in Class 54 or Class 55 of Schedule II to those Regulations.â(vÃ©hicule zÃ©ro Ã©mission)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(iv) of the definition fiducie collective des employÃ©s in subsection 248(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: immÃ©diatement avant le moment dâun transfert admissible dâentreprise Ã la fiducie, ne dÃ©tenait pas, directement ou indirectement, seule ou avec une personne ou sociÃ©tÃ© de personnes liÃ©e ou affiliÃ©e, des actions du capital-actions ou des dettes de lâentreprise admissible, dont la valeur est Ã©gale ou supÃ©rieure Ã 50â¯% de la juste valeur marchande des actions du capital-actions et des dettes de lâentreprise admissible,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: qualifying cooperative business, at a particular time, means a corporation, controlled by a worker cooperative,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is a Canadian-controlled private corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of which not more than 40% of the directors consist of individuals that, immediately before the time that the worker cooperative acquired control of the corporation, owned, directly or indirectly, together with any person or partnership that is related to or affiliated with the director, 50% or more of the fair market value of the shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of the corporation, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that deals at armâs length and is not affiliated with any person (other than a subject corporation referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition âqualifying cooperative conversionâ that controlled and wholly-owned the corporation immediately before the time the worker cooperative acquired control of the corporation) or partnership that owned, directly or indirectly, 50% or more of the fair market value of the shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of the corporation immediately before the time the worker cooperative acquired control of the corporation;â(entreprise coopÃ©rative admissible)

Definition.Text: qualifying cooperative conversionâmeans a disposition by a taxpayer of shares of the capital stock of a corporation (in this definition referred to as the âsubject corporationâ) to another corporation (in this definition referred to as the âpurchaser corporationâ), if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before the disposition, all or substantially all the fair market value of the assets of the subject corporation is derived, directly or indirectly, from assets (other than an interest in a partnership) that are used principally in an active business carried on by the subject corporation or a corporation that is controlled and wholly-owned by the subject corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at the time of the disposition,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer deals at armâs length with the purchaser corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation acquires control of the subject corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation is a worker cooperative, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at all times after the disposition,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer deals at armâs length with the purchaser corporation and subject corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer does not retain any right or influence that, if exercised, would allow the taxpayer (whether alone or together with any person or partnership that is related to or affiliated with the taxpayer) to control, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, the purchaser corporation or subject corporation; (conversion admissible de coopÃ©rative)

Definition.Text: qualifying cooperative workerâmeans an individual who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: holds a membership share of a corporation that was incorporated or continued by or under the provisions of a law, of Canada or of a province, that provide for the establishment of the corporation as a cooperative corporation or that provide for the establishment of cooperative corporations,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is an employee of the corporation or a qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not represent, together with any person or partnership that is related to or affiliated with the individual, more than 50% of the members of the worker cooperative,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before the time of a qualifying cooperative conversion that involved the corporation, did not own, directly or indirectly, together with any person or partnership that is related to or affiliated with the individual, shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of the corporation or a qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation, the value of which is equal to or greater than 50% of the fair market value of the shares of the capital stock and indebtedness of the corporation or the qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: has not claimed, and is not related to an individual who claimed, a deduction under subsection 110.62(2) in respect of a disposition of shares of the corporation or a qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation;â(travailleur admissible de coopÃ©rative)

Definition.Text: worker cooperativeâmeans a corporation that, at all relevant times, satisfies the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation is resident in Canada,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation was incorporated or continued by or under the provisions of a law, of Canada or of a province, that provide for the establishment of the corporation as a cooperative corporation or that provide for the establishment of cooperative corporations,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation is established for the purpose of providing employment to its members,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation would be controlled by a particular person if each membership share of the capital stock of the corporation that is owned by a qualifying cooperative worker were owned by the particular person,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: at least 75% of all individuals employed by the corporation and all qualifying cooperative businesses controlled by the corporation (other than an employee who has not completed an applicable probationary period, which may not exceed 12 months) are holders of a membership share of the corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: each initial membership share provided to an employee of the corporation and any qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: issued in exchange for a payment of a nominal amount determined in the same manner for all members described in the definition qualifying cooperative worker, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: offered to each employee following their completion of an applicable probationary period, which may not exceed 12 months,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: at least one-third of the directors of the corporation are qualifying cooperative workers of the corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 40% of the directors of the corporation consist of individuals each of whom, immediately before the time of a qualifying cooperative conversion that involved the corporation, owned, directly or indirectly, together with any person or partnership that is related to or affiliated with the director, 50% or more of the fair market value of the shares of the capital stock or indebtedness of the corporation or a qualifying cooperative business controlled by the corporation, and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the by-laws of the corporation provide a procedure for allocating, crediting or distributing any surplus earnings of the corporation, including that not less than 50% of those earnings must be paid on the basis of the remuneration earned by the qualifying cooperative workers from the corporation or the labour contributed by those members to the corporation;â(coopÃ©rative de travailleurs)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: unclaimed property authorityâmeans an entity that receives and holds property on behalf of individuals that cannot be located, as authorized under the provisions of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Unclaimed Property Act, CQLR, c. B-5.1,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Unclaimed Property Act, S.B.C. 1999, c. 48, or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed law or a law designated by the Minister of Finance for the purpose of this definition that is published in such a manner as the Minister of Finance deems appropriate;â(autoritÃ© des biens non rÃ©clamÃ©s)

Definition.Text: unlocated individualâmeans an individual in respect of whom property held under a registered pension plan, RRIF or RRSP can be paid or transferred to an unclaimed property authority in accordance with the laws of Canada or a province;â(particulier introuvable)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(42) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the definition synthetic equity arrangement in subsection (1), paragraph (c) of the definition dividend rental arrangement in subsection (1) and subsection 112(10), an arrangement that reflects the fair market value of more than one type of identical share (within the meaning of subsection 112(10)) is considered to be a separate arrangement with respect to each type of identical share the value of which the arrangement reflects.
Subsections (1), (3), (4), (8) to (11) and (15) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.

Subsection (2) applies in respect of amounts paid to an individual by an unclaimed property authority if an amount in respect of the payment was paid to the unclaimed property authority after December 31, 2026.
Subsections (5) to (7) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsection (13) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsection (14) comes into force on January 1, 2027.
Paragraph 251(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a taxpayer and a personal trust (other than a trust described in any of paragraphs (a) to (e.1) and (h) of the definition trust in subsection 108(1)) are deemed not to deal with each other at armâs length if the taxpayer, or any person not dealing at armâs length with the taxpayer, would be beneficially interested in the trust if subsection 248(25) were read without reference to subclauses 248(25)(b)(iii)(A)(II) to (IV); and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.

Definition.Text: specified provisionâmeans any of subsections 10(10) and 13(24), paragraph 37(1)(h), subsections 66(11.4) and (11.5), 66.7(10) and (11), 69(11) and 111(4), (5), (5.01), (5.1) and (5.3), paragraphs (j) and (k) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection 127(9), subsections 181.1(7) and 190.1(6), section 251.2 and any provision of similar effect.â(dispositions dÃ©terminÃ©es)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 1100(1)(c)(i) of the Regulations is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of clause (A) and by adding the following after clause (B):

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1100 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.01):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Straddle years â reaccelerated investment incentive property
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxation year begins in 2029 and ends in 2030, the factor determined for A.1 in subsection (2) (other than for the purposes of paragraphs (c), (d) and (e) of the description of A.1 in subsection (2)) is to be replaced by the factor determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2029,

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2029,
is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2030, and

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2030;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraphs (c), (d) and (e) of the description of A.1 in subsection (2), if a taxation year begins in 2026 and ends in 2027, the factor determined for A.1 in subsection (2) is to be replaced by the factor determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2026,

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2026,
is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2027, and

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2027; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxation year begins in 2031 and ends in 2032, the factor determined for A.1 in subsection (2) is to be replaced by the factor determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2031,

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2031,
is the factor otherwise determined for A.1 in subsection (2) for 2032, and

is the amount that would be determined for D.1 in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2032.

Subsection.Text: Section 1100 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.02):
For the purposes of subsection (2), in respect of property of a class in Schedule II that is reaccelerated investment incentive property of a taxpayer solely because of subparagraph 1104(4.01)(b)(i),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amounts incurred by any person or partnership in respect of the property are not to be included in determining the amount for D.1 in subsection (2) in respect of the class

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the amounts are incurred before 2025, unless

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the property was acquired after 2024 by a person or partnership from another person or partnership (referred to in this subparagraph as the âtransfereeâ and the âtransferorâ, respectively),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the transferee was either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the taxpayer, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: a person or partnership that does not deal at armâs length with the taxpayer, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the transferor

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: dealt at armâs length with the transferee, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: held the property as inventory, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the amounts are incurred after 2024 and amounts are deemed to have been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act, in respect of those amounts incurred, under paragraph 1104(4.11)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount excluded from the amount determined for D.1 in subsection (2) in respect of the class because of paragraph (a) is to be included in determining the amount for F in subsection (2) in respect of the class, unless no amount in respect of the property would be so included if the property were not reaccelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsections (2) to (8) and (12) to (17) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.
Subsection (9) applies to property acquired after 2021.
Subsections (10) and (11) apply to property that is acquired and becomes available for use after April 15, 2024.
Section 1101 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1ac):

Subsection.Label: (1ac.1)
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part, each property of a taxpayer that is a new purpose-built residential rental is prescribed to be a separate class of property.
Section 1101 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2c):

Subsection.Label: (2d)
Subsection.Text: A separate class is prescribed for each vessel of a taxpayer described in Class 7 in Schedule II, including the furniture, fittings, radiocommunication equipment and other equipment attached to the vessel, that has been used by the taxpayer to earn income that would not be included in computing the income of the taxpayer because of paragraph 81(1)(c.1) of the Act.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on December 31, 2023.
Subsection 1102(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (j), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (k) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: referred to in paragraph 81(1)(c.3) of the Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1102(20.1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 1100(0.3), (2.02) and (2.021) and 1104(3.1), (4) and (4.01), a particular person or partnership and another person or partnership shall be considered not to be dealing at armâs length with each other in respect of the acquisition or ownership of a property if, in the absence of this subsection, they would be considered to be dealing at armâs length with each other and it may reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of any transaction or event, or a series of transactions or events, is to cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property to qualify as accelerated investment incentive property, reaccelerated investment incentive property or immediate expensing property; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person or partnership and the other person or partnership to satisfy the condition in subparagraph 1100(0.3)(c)(i) or subclause 1100(2.02)(a)(i)(C)(I) or (2.021)(a)(i)(C)(I).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 31, 2023.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.
Subsection 1104(2) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: new purpose-built residential rentalâmeans a purpose-built residential rental that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: built for use as a purpose-built residential rental if construction began after April 15, 2024 and before 2031, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: previously a building, or part of a building, used as a commercial property that was substantially renovated for use as a purpose-built residential rental if the renovations began after April 15, 2024 and before 2031, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: becomes available for use before 2036;â(nouvel ensemble rÃ©sidentiel construit spÃ©cialement pour la location)

Definition.Text: purpose-built residential rentalâmeans a building or a part of a building situated in Canada

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that contains

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: four or more residential rental units at least four of which contain private kitchen facilities, a private bath and a private living area, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 10 or more residential rental units, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in which all or substantially all the residential rental units are rented or offered for rent for continuous periods of not less than 28 consecutive days;â(ensemble rÃ©sidentiel construit spÃ©cialement pour la location)

Definition.Text: residential rental unitâmeans a housing unit used or intended for use as a rented residential premises that is not provided to the travelling or vacationing public;â(logement locatif)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 1104(4)(a) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired by the taxpayer after November 20, 2018 and before 2025 and becomes available for use before 2028; and

Subsection.Text: Section 1104 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of reaccelerated investment incentive property
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part and Schedules II to VI, reaccelerated investment incentive property means property of a taxpayer (other than property included in any of Classes 54 to 56) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired by the taxpayer after 2024 and becomes available for use before 2034; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: meets either of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property is not a property in respect of which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act by any person or partnership for a taxation year ending before the time the property was acquired by the taxpayer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the property was not

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: acquired in circumstances where

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the taxpayer was deemed to have been allowed or deducted an amount under paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act in respect of the property in computing income for previous taxation years, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the undepreciated capital cost of depreciable property of a prescribed class of the taxpayer was reduced by an amount determined by reference to the amount by which the capital cost of the property to the taxpayer exceeds its cost amount, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: previously owned or acquired by the taxpayer or by a person or partnership with which the taxpayer did not deal at armâs length at any time when the property was owned or acquired by the person or partnership.

Subsection.Text: Section 1104 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.1):

For the purpose of subparagraph (4.01)(b)(i), if the capital cost to a taxpayer of a depreciable property (referred to in this subsection as the âsingle propertyâ) includes amounts incurred at different times, then amounts deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act in respect of the single property are deemed to have been deducted in respect of a separate property that is not part of the single property to the extent the deducted amounts can reasonably be considered to be in respect of amounts

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: incurred before 2025; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: incurred after 2024, if any portion of the single property is considered to have become available for use before the time the single property is first used for the purpose of earning income.

Subsection.Text: The definition transmission equipment in subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: transmission equipmentâmeans equipment used to transmit electrical energy.â(matÃ©riel de transmission)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: eligible electrical generation equipmentâmeans property that is electrical generating equipment described in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph (d)(iii.1), (v), (vi), (vii), (xiv) or (xix) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph (e)(i) or (iii) of the definition clean electricity property in subsection 127.491(1) of the Act.â(matÃ©riel gÃ©nÃ©rateur dâÃ©lectricitÃ© admissible)

Definition.Text: eligible transmission equipmentâmeans property (other than a building) that is transmission equipment of a taxpayer where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission equipment is used in connection with eligible electrical generation equipment of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on annual basis

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 75% of the electrical energy generated by the eligible electrical generation equipment is transmitted by the transmission equipment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 75% of the electrical energy transmitted by the transmission equipment is generated by the eligible electrical generation equipment.â(matÃ©riel de transmission admissible)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 1104(15)(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property utilizes heat obtained from the taxpayerâs system;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1104(17) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

A property that would otherwise be eligible for inclusion in Class 43.1 or Class 43.2 in Schedule II by a taxpayer is deemed not to be eligible for inclusion in either of those classes if, at the time the property becomes available for use, there is substantial non-compliance by the taxpayer with the requirements of any environmental law, by-law or regulation of Canada, a province, a municipality, or a municipal or public body performing a function of government in Canada that is applicable in respect of the property.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 16, 2024.
Subsections (2) to (4) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2025.
Subsections (5) and (6) are deemed to have come into force on November 17, 2025.
Subsections (7) and (8) are deemed to have come into force on November 21, 2023.
The formula in subsection 1400(3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

The formula in subsection 1400(3) of the Regulations is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of the description of H, by adding âandâ at the end of the description of I and by adding the following after the description of I:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the reinsurance contract held amount in respect of a group of reinsurance contracts that is included in the description of H and that is in respect of a liability for incurred claims in respect of a group of insurance contracts that is included in the description of C.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Paragraph 2902(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an expenditure of a capital nature incurred by a taxpayer in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the acquisition of property, except any such expenditure that at the time it was incurred

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was for first term shared-use-equipment or second term shared-use-equipment (as those terms are defined in subsection 127(9) of the Act), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: was for the provision of premises, facilities or equipment if, at the time of the acquisition of the premises, facilities or equipment, it was intended

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: that the premises, facilities or equipment would be used during all or substantially all of the operating time of the premises, facilities or equipment in the expected useful life of the premises, facilities or equipment for the prosecution of scientific research and experimental development in Canada, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: that all or substantially all of the value of the premises, facilities or equipment would be consumed in the prosecution of scientific research and experimental development in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the acquisition of property that is qualified property as defined in subsection 127(9) of the Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the acquisition of property that has been used, or acquired for use or lease, for any purpose whatever before it was acquired by the taxpayer;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of property acquired on or after December 16, 2024 and, in the case of lease costs, to amounts that first become payable on or after December 16, 2024.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after the heading âSpecial-Purpose Buildingsâ after section 2902:

Section.Text: For the purposes of this Part and paragraph 37(8)(e) of the Act, a special-purpose building is a building the working areas of which are designed and constructed to have a displacement in any direction of not more than 0.02 Âµm (micrometres) and to have, per 0.028 cubic metre of interior airspace,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 350 airborne particles of a size less than or equal to 0.1 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter and no airborne particles of a size greater than 0.1 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 75 airborne particles of a size less than or equal to 0.2 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter and no airborne particles of a size greater than 0.2 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 30 airborne particles of a size less than or equal to 0.3 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter and no airborne particles of a size greater than 0.3 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter, or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: not more than 10 airborne particles of a size less than or equal to 0.5 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter and no airborne particles of a size greater than 0.5 Âµm (micrometres) in diameter.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of property acquired on or after December 16, 2024 and, in the case of lease costs, to amounts that first become payable on or after December 16, 2024.
Subparagraph 3100(1)(b)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as a form of assistance from a government, municipality or other public authority, whether as a grant, a subsidy, a forgivable loan, a deduction from tax (other than an amount described in clause (b)(i)(B) of the definition tax shelter in subsection 237.1(1) of the Act) or an investment allowance, or as any other form of assistance, other than an excluded loan as defined in subsection 12(11) of the Act, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3100(3) of the Regulations is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the amount is an excluded loan as defined in subsection 12(11) of the Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.

Section 5700 of the Regulations is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (z.3), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (z.4) and by adding the following after paragraph (z.4):

Paragraph.Label: (z.5)
Paragraph.Text: navigation device for low vision for an individual who has a vision impairment.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2024 and subsequent taxation years.
Subparagraph (iii) of the description of A in the definition exempt surplus in subsection 5907(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Heading.TitleText: Payment out of Consolidated Revenue Fund

Section.MarginalNote: Payment out of Consolidated Revenue Fund
Section.Text: Any amount payable by the Minister of National Revenue in relation to the application of subsection 127.491(2) of the Income Tax Act is to be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: Digital Services Tax (Repeals and Other Measures)

Subsection.Text: The Digital Services Tax Act, section 96 of chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 2024, is repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on June 20, 2024.
The Digital Services Tax Regulations, section 97 of chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 2024, are repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on June 20, 2024.

If a person has, before the day on which this Act receives royal assent, paid an amount to His Majesty in right of Canada and the amount, in the absence of section 126, would have been taken into account by His Majesty in right of Canada as tax, a penalty, interest or other amount under the Digital Services Tax Act, the Minister of National Revenue must refund to the person the amount, together with interest on the amount at the rate determined under paragraph 2(1)(a) of the Interest Rates (Excise Act, 2001) Regulations, for the period beginning on the day on which the amount was received by the Receiver General for Canada and ending on the day on which the refund is paid.
Any refund payable by the Minister of National Revenue under subsection (1) is to be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine references and appeals to the Court on matters arising under the Canada Pension Plan, the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the Old Age Security Act, the Petroleum and Gas Revenue Tax Act, Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the Income Tax Act, the Employment Insurance Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, the Disability Tax Credit Promoters Restrictions Act, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act when references or appeals to the Court are provided for in those Acts.
Subsections 12(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine questions referred to it under section 310 or 311 of the Excise Tax Act, section 97.58 of the Customs Act, section 173 or 174 of the Income Tax Act, section 51 or 52 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, section 204 or 205 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 62 or 63 of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, section 121 or 122 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, section 45 or 46 of the Underused Housing Tax Act, section 105 or 106 of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act or section 95 or 96 of the Global Minimum Tax Act.

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine applications for extensions of time under subsection 28(1) of the Canada Pension Plan, section 33.2 of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, section 304 or 305 of the Excise Tax Act, section 97.51 or 97.52 of the Customs Act, section 166.2 or 167 of the Income Tax Act, subsection 103(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, section 45 or 47 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, section 197 or 199 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 115 or 117 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, section 39 or 41 of the Underused Housing Tax Act, section 99 or 101 of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act or section 89 or 91 of the Global Minimum Tax Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 18.29(3)(a) of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of subparagraph (ix), by replacing âorâ with âandâ at the end of subparagraph (x) and by repealing subparagraph (xi).
Subsection 18.31(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If it is agreed under section 310 of the Excise Tax Act, section 97.58 of the Customs Act, section 51 of the Air Travellers Security Act, section 204 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 62 of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Act, 2006, section 121 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, section 45 of the Underused Housing Tax Act, section 105 of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act or section 95 of the Global Minimum Tax Act that a question should be determined by the Court, sections 17.1, 17.2 and 17.4 to 17.8 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of the determination of the question.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.32(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provisions applicable to determination of a question
Subsection.Text: If an application has been made under section 311 of the Excise Tax Act, section 52 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, section 205 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 63 of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006, section 122 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, section 46 of the Underused Housing Tax Act, section 106 of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act or section 96 of the Global Minimum Tax Act for the determination of a question, the application or determination of the question must, subject to section 18.33, be determined in accordance with sections 17.1, 17.2 and 17.4 to 17.8, with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Section.Text: Paragraph 18(1)(t) of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of subparagraph (v) and by repealing subparagraph (vi).
Subsection 164(2.01) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall not, in respect of a taxpayer, refund, repay, apply to other debts or set-off amounts under this Act at any time unless all returns of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed by the taxpayer at or before that time under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act have been filed with the Minister.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 221.2(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a particular amount was appropriated to an amount (in this section referred to as the âdebtâ) that is or may become payable by a person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act or the Global Minimum Tax Act, the Minister may, on application by the person, appropriate the particular amount, or a part of it, to another amount that is or may become payable under any of those Acts and, for the purposes of any of those Acts,

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition program legislation in section 2 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of subparagraph (x) and by repealing subparagraph (xi).
Subsection 40(4) of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A refund shall not be paid until the person has filed with the Minister all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge that are required to be filed under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act.
Paragraph 188(6)(a) of the Excise Act, 2001 is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister under this Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act; or

Subsection.Text: Clause 188(7)(b)(ii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the Minister under this Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act, or

Section.Text: Subsection 189(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A refund shall not be paid until the person has filed with the Minister or the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed under this Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Customs Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act.

Section.Text: Section 34 of the Underused Housing Tax Act is replaced by the following:

An amount under section 33 is not to be paid to a person by the Minister at any time, unless all returns of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed at or before that time by the person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act have been filed with the Minister.
Section 45 of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act is replaced by the following:

A rebate under this Subdivision is not to be paid to a person at any time unless all returns of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed at or before that time by the person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act have been filed with the Minister.
Section 48 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a trustee is appointed under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act to act in the administration of the estate or succession of a bankrupt, a rebate under this Division that the bankrupt was entitled to claim before the appointment must not be paid after the appointment unless all returns required to be filed in respect of the bankrupt under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act in respect of periods ending before the appointment have been filed and all amounts required to be paid under that Part and those Acts by the bankrupt in respect of those periods have been paid.
The portion of subsection 53(3) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, at any time, a person referred to in subsection (1) or (2) fails to give or maintain security in an amount satisfactory to the Minister, the Minister may retain as security, out of any amount that may be or may become payable to the person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act or the Global Minimum Tax Act, an amount not exceeding the amount determined by the formula

Section.Text: Subsection 57(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Restriction â rebate of net tax
Subsection.Text: A rebate under subsection (4) is not to be paid to a person at any time unless all returns of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed at or before that time by the person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act have been filed with the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An amount under section 92 or 93 is not to be paid to a person by the Minister at any time, unless all returns of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed at or before that time by the person under this Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part 1 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Global Minimum Tax Act have been filed with the Minister.
The description of B in paragraph 67(2)(a) of the Global Minimum Tax Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, if any, the transferee was assessed under paragraph 97.44(1)(b) of the Customs Act, subsection 325(2) of the Excise Tax Act, subsection 160(2) of the Income Tax Act, subsection 297(3) of the Excise Act, 2001, subsection 161(1) of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, subsection 80(3) of the Underused Housing Tax Act or subsection 150(4) of the Select Luxury Items Tax Act in respect of the property, and

Section.Text: Section 77 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister must not, in respect of a person, refund, repay, apply to other debts or set off amounts under this Act until the person has filed with the Minister all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge that are required to be filed under this Act, the Income Tax Act, the Excise Tax Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act, the Underused Housing Tax Act and the Select Luxury Items Tax Act.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Excise Tax Act (GST/HST), the Underused Housing Tax Act, the Select Luxury Items Tax Act and Other Related Texts

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 181(5) of the Excise Tax Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, if, in full or partial consideration for a taxable supply of property or a service, a supplier that is a registrant accepts a coupon that may be exchanged for the property or service or that entitles the recipient of the supply to a reduction of, or a discount on, the price of the property or service and a particular person at any time pays, exclusively in the course of commercial activities of the particular person, an amount to the supplier for the redemption of the coupon, the following rules apply:
Section 181 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
For the purposes of subsection (5), a payment is made by a person exclusively in the course of commercial activities of the person only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is not a financial institution and all or substantially all of the activities in the course of which the payment is made are commercial activities; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a financial institution and all of the activities in the course of which the payment is made are commercial activities.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on August 16, 2025. They also apply in respect of any payment made by a person before August 16, 2025 to a supplier for the redemption of a coupon if the person has not claimed an input tax credit in respect of the payment in a return under Division V of Part IX of the Act that is filed before August 16, 2025.
Subsection 191(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, subsection 256.2(3.1) and the Real Property (GST/HST) Regulations, the construction or substantial renovation of a multiple unit residential complex or a condominium complex, or the construction of an addition to a multiple unit residential complex, shall be deemed to be substantially completed not later than the day all or substantially all of the residential units in the complex or addition are occupied after the construction or substantial renovation is begun.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on September 14, 2023.
Section 256.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Purpose-built rental housing â student residence
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of applying this section and the Real Property (GST/HST) Regulations (other than prescribed provisions) in respect of a taxable supply of property that is a residential complex or an addition to a residential complex, if the taxable supply and the property meet the conditions described in paragraph (3.1)(a) or (b), clause (a)(iii)(A) of the definition qualifying residential unit in subsection (1) is to be read as follows:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: as the primary place of residence of the person or a relation of the person, or of a lessor of the complex or a relation of that lessor, for a period of at least one year or for a shorter period where the next use of the unit after that shorter period is as described in clause (B),

Clause.Label: (A.1)
Clause.Text: if the person is a university that is established and operated otherwise than for profit, a public college that is established and operated otherwise than for profit or a school authority that is established and operated otherwise than for profit, as a place of residence for students attending the university, the public college or a school of the school authority, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 256.2(3) of the Act after paragraph (d) and before the first formula in that subsection is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsections (3.1) to (3.4), (7) and (8), pay a rebate to the person equal to the total of all amounts each of which is an amount, in respect of a residential unit that forms part of the residential complex or addition, as the case may be, and is a qualifying residential unit of the person at the particular time, determined by the formula

Subsection.Text: The description of B in subsection 256.2(3.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the unitâs percentage of total floor space.

Subsection.Text: Section 256.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3.2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Purpose-built rental housing â student residence
Subsection.Text: The rules set out in subsection (3.4) apply in respect of the construction or substantial renovation of a residential complex or an addition to a multiple unit residential complex if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the builder of the residential complex or addition is a university, a public college or a school authority;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the construction or substantial renovation of the residential complex or addition is carried out primarily for the purpose of providing a place of residence for students attending the university, the public college or a school of the school authority;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder would, in the absence of subsection 191(6), be deemed under section 191 to have made and received, at a particular time, a taxable supply by way of sale of the residential complex or addition; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the taxable supply referred to in paragraph (c) that would, in the absence of subsection 191(6), be deemed under section 191 to have been made and received by the builder, it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the residential complex or addition is property prescribed for the purposes of subsection (3.1), determined as if the deemed purchase referred to in subparagraph (3)(a)(ii) were the taxable supply and the particular time referred to in paragraph (3)(b) were the particular time referred to in paragraph (c), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxable supply and the residential complex or addition meet the conditions prescribed for the purposes of subsection (3.1) and the conditions described in paragraph (3.1)(a) or (b).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amount of rebate â student residence
Subsection.Text: If the conditions set out in subsection (3.3) are met in respect of the construction or substantial renovation of a residential complex or an addition to a multiple unit residential complex, for the purposes of applying subsection (3) in respect of the construction or substantial renovation of the residential complex or addition, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions in subparagraph (3)(a)(ii) and paragraphs (3)(b) to (d) are deemed to be met at the particular time referred to in paragraph (3.3)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the deemed purchase referred to in subparagraph (3)(a)(ii) is deemed to be the taxable supply referred to in paragraph (3.3)(c) that would, in the absence of subsection 191(6), be deemed under section 191 to have been made and received by the builder of the residential complex or addition;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the particular time referred to in paragraph (3)(b) is deemed to be the particular time referred to in paragraph (3.3)(c); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: despite subsections (3.1) and (3.2), the amount of the rebate under subsection (3) in respect of the construction or substantial renovation of the residential complex or addition is equal to the amount determined as if the first formula in subsection (3) and the descriptions for that formula were read as follows:
