The court may make the order and any other order that it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Court enforcement â accredited third-party service provider
Subsection.Text: If an accredited third-party service provider, or another entity or individual in conducting the business or affairs of the accredited third-party service provider, fails to comply with a compliance agreement or a direction made under section 148, contravenes a provision of this Act or the regulations or omits to do any thing that is required to be done under this Act, the Bank may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order requiring the accredited third-party service provider, entity or individual to comply with the compliance agreement or the direction, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done. The court may make the order and any other order that it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Court enforcement â external complaints body
Subsection.Text: If the external complaints body fails to comply with a compliance agreement, any of paragraphs 115(b) to (u) or a direction made under section 148, the Bank may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order requiring the external complaints body to comply with the compliance agreement, the paragraph or the direction. The court may make the order and any other order that it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Court enforcement â technical standards body
Subsection.Text: If the technical standards body fails to comply with a compliance agreement or a direction made under section 148, the Bank may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order requiring the technical standards body to comply with the compliance agreement or the direction. The court may make the order and any other order that it thinks fit.
An appeal from a decision of a court under this section lies in the same manner and to the same court as an appeal from any other order of the court.

Section.Text: If, in the Bankâs opinion, an individual or entity fails to comply, or there are reasonable grounds to believe that an individual or entity will fail to comply, with an undertaking required under section 55, terms and conditions imposed under section 56 or an order made under subsection 60(1) or (3), the Bank must immediately notify the Minister.
The Minister may designate persons or classes of persons as authorized persons for the purposes of sections 152 to 154.

Subsection.Text: An authorized person may request in writing that an individual or entity, within the time and in the manner specified by the authorized person, provide the authorized person with any information that the authorized person considers necessary for a purpose related to verifying compliance with an undertaking required under section 55, terms and conditions imposed under section 56 or an order made under subsection 60(1) or (3).
The individual or entity must comply with the request.

An authorized person may, from time to time, examine the records and inquire into the business and affairs of an individual or entity for the purpose of verifying compliance with an undertaking required under section 55, terms and conditions imposed under section 56 or an order made under subsection 60(1) or (3) and for that purpose may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enter any place in which the authorized person has reasonable grounds to believe that there are records relevant to verifying compliance with the undertaking, terms and conditions or order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use any computer system in the place, or cause it to be used, to examine data contained in or available to it;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reproduce any record or cause any record to be reproduced from the data, in the form of a printout or other intelligible output and remove the printout or other output for examination or copying; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: use any copying equipment in the place or cause it to be used.

Subsection.Text: The owner or person in charge of the place and every person in the place must give all assistance that is reasonably required to enable the authorized person to perform their functions under this section and must provide any documents or information, and access to any data, that are specified by the authorized person.
If the place is a dwelling-house, an authorized person may enter it without the occupantâs consent only under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (2).

On ex parte application, a justice of the peace may issue a warrant authorizing an authorized person to enter a dwelling-house, subject to any conditions specified in the warrant, if the justice is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the dwelling-house is a place referred to in paragraph 153(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling-house is necessary for a purpose related to verifying compliance with an undertaking required under section 55, terms and conditions imposed under section 56 or an order made under subsection 60(1) or (3); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry was refused by the occupant or there are reasonable grounds to believe that entry will be refused by, or that consent to entry cannot be obtained from, the occupant.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designating as a violation the contravention of a specified provision of this Act or of the regulations or the non-compliance with orders made, undertakings required, compliance agreements entered into or directions made under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing, having regard to subsection (2), a penalty or a range of penalties in respect of a violation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the service of documents under sections 156 to 168, including the manner and proof of service and the circumstances under which documents are considered to be served;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the circumstances in which the Bank must not make public, under subsection 168(1), the name of the individual or entity that committed the violation; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: generally, for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this section and sections 156 to 168.

Subsection.Text: The maximum penalty for a violation is $1,000,000 if the violation is committed by an individual and $10,000,000 if the violation is committed by a participating entity or accredited third-party service provider.

Section.Text: Except if a penalty is fixed under paragraph 155(1)(b), the amount of a penalty must, in each case, be determined taking into account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the degree of intention or negligence on the part of the individual or entity that committed the violation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the harm done by the violation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the duration of the violation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the ability of the individual or entity that committed the violation to pay the penalty;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the history of the individual or entity that committed the violation with respect to any prior violation or conviction under this Act within the five-year period immediately before the violation; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other criteria that may be provided for in the regulations.

Section.Text: The purpose of a penalty is to promote compliance with this Act and not to punish.

If a contravention or non-compliance that is designated under paragraph 155(1)(a) can be proceeded with either as a violation or as an offence, proceeding in one manner precludes proceeding in the other.

Subsection.Text: Every contravention or non-compliance that is designated under paragraph 155(1)(a) constitutes a violation and the individual or entity that commits the violation is liable to a penalty determined in accordance with sections 155 and 156.

If the Bank believes on reasonable grounds that an individual or entity has committed a violation, the Bank may issue a notice of violation and, in that case, must cause it to be served on the individual or entity.
A notice of violation must name the individual or entity believed to have committed a violation, identify the violation and set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the penalty that the Bank proposes to impose;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the right of the individual or entity, within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served or within any longer period that the Bank specifies, to pay the penalty or to make representations to the Governor with respect to the violation and the proposed penalty, and the manner for doing so; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that, if the individual or entity does not pay the penalty or make representations in accordance with the notice, the individual or entity will be deemed to have committed the violation and the Bank may impose a penalty in respect of it.

Subsection.Text: If the individual or entity pays the penalty proposed in the notice of violation, the individual or entity is deemed to have committed the violation and proceedings in respect of it are ended.

If the individual or entity makes representations in accordance with the notice, the Governor must decide, on a balance of probabilities, whether the individual or entity committed the violation and, if so, may, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 155(1)(b), impose the penalty proposed in the notice, a lesser penalty or no penalty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to pay or make representations
Subsection.Text: An individual or entity that neither pays the penalty nor makes representations in accordance with the notice is deemed to have committed the violation and the Bank may, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 155(1)(b), impose the penalty proposed in the notice, a lesser penalty or no penalty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice â decision and right of appeal
Subsection.Text: The Bank must cause notice of any decision made under subsection (2) or (3) to be issued and served on the individual or entity together with notice of the right of appeal under section 161.

An individual or entity on which a notice under subsection 160(4) is served may, within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served or within any longer period that the Court allows, appeal the decision to the Federal Court.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Court to take precautions against disclosing
Subsection.Text: In an appeal, the Federal Court must take every reasonable precaution, including, if appropriate, conducting hearings in private, to avoid the disclosure by the Court or any person of confidential information referred to in section 131.
On an appeal, the Federal Court may confirm, set aside or, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 155(1)(b), vary the decision.
A penalty constitutes a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in the Federal Court.
No proceedings to recover a debt referred to in subsection (1) may be commenced later than five years after the day on which the debt becomes payable.
A debt referred to in subsection (1) is payable to and must be remitted to the Receiver General.
The unpaid amount of any debt referred to in subsection 162(1) may be certified by the Governor.
Registration in the Federal Court of a certificate has the same effect as a judgment of that Court for a debt of the amount specified in the certificate and all related registration costs.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, a violation is not an offence and, accordingly, section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply in respect of a violation.

Subsection.Text: Due diligence is a defence in a proceeding in relation to a violation.

Every rule and principle of the common law that makes any circumstance a justification or excuse in relation to a charge for an offence applies in respect of a violation to the extent that it is not inconsistent with this Act.

Section.Text: In a proceeding in respect of a violation or a prosecution for an offence, a notice purporting to be issued under subsection 159(2) or 160(4) or a certificate purporting to be made under subsection 163(1) is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it.

Subsection.Text: No proceedings in respect of a violation may be commenced later than two years after the day on which the subject matter of the proceedings became known to the Bank.

A document appearing to have been issued by the Bank, certifying the day on which the subject matter of any proceedings became known to the Bank, is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed the document and is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the matter asserted in it.
Subject to any regulations, the Bank must make public the nature of a violation, the name of the individual or entity that committed it and the amount of the penalty imposed.
In making public the nature of a violation, the Bank may include the reasons for the decision, including the relevant facts, analysis and considerations that formed part of the decision.

Section.Text: A participating entity, an accredited third-party service provider and the technical standards body are authorized to use the phrase âconsumer-driven bankingâ to indicate or describe the activities that they perform under this Act.
An individual or entity, other than a participating entity, must not

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use the term âparticipating entityâ or a variation, abbreviation or equivalent of that term, or any words, name or designation â in any language â in a manner that leads to a reasonable belief that the individual or entity is a participating entity for the purposes of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: represent themselves, in any way or by any means, to be a participating entity for the purposes of this Act.

Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, an individual or entity must not, for the purposes of providing a consumer in Canada with a product or service, use an interface or application to gain direct access to the consumerâs data using the consumerâs authentication information.
An individual or entity must not knowingly provide false or misleading information in relation to their participation under this Act.
An individual or entity must not provide false or misleading information to the Bank, the Minister or a designated person or government authority.

Subsection.Text: Every individual or entity commits an offence that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes a provision of this Act, other than section 172, or of the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with an order made under section 55 or an undertaking required under that section;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with an order made under section 56 or subsection 60(1) or (3) or 61(1) or (3);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with a compliance agreement entered into under section 147; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with a direction made under section 148.

Subsection.Text: Every individual or entity that contravenes section 172 commits an offence.
Every individual or entity that commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity, to a fine of not more than $5,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than one year, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity, to a fine of not more than $500,000.

Subsection.Text: An individual or entity is not to be found guilty of an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

If an individual or entity is convicted of an offence under this Act, the court may, in addition to any punishment that it may otherwise impose, order the individual or entity to comply with the provisions of this Act or of the regulations in respect of which the individual or entity was convicted.

If an individual or entity is convicted of an offence under this Act, the court may, if it is satisfied that as a result of the commission of the offence the convicted individual or entity acquired any monetary benefits or that monetary benefits accrued to the convicted individual or entity or the individualâs spouse, common-law partner or other dependant, order the convicted individual or entity to pay, despite the maximum amount of any fine that may otherwise be imposed under this Act, an additional fine in an amount equal to three times the courtâs estimation of the amount of those monetary benefits.

Section.Text: If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any director, any officer, any agent or mandatary or any principal officer of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and is liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided for an individual in respect of the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

Subsection.Text: Proceedings by way of summary conviction in respect of an offence under this Act may be commenced at any time within, but not later than, two years after the day on which the subject matter of the proceedings became known to the Bank.

A document appearing to have been issued by the Bank, certifying the day on which the subject matter of any proceedings became known to the Bank, is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed the document and is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the matter asserted in it.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act and, in particular, may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the data that is excluded from the definition derived data in section 2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting accreditation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting fees for accreditation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting notifications and the provision of information in the case of the revocation of a participating entityâs or accredited third-party service providerâs accreditation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registry referred to in section 44;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sharing of data under section 76 and providing for exceptions to the duty to share and to the prohibition on imposing conditions set out in that section;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting security safeguards to be implemented for the purposes of section 79;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting designations under section 80;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting reports made and notices given under section 82;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duty to investigate and report under section 83;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the express consent that must be obtained under section 85;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information to be provided under subsection 85(4);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: respecting exceptions to the duty set out in subsection 85(6);

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the record that must be kept under subsection 85(8);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the renewal of consent under subsection 87(1), providing for the circumstances referred to in that subsection and respecting the information to be provided under paragraph 87(4)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: exempting participating entities, for the purposes of subsection 87(2), from any requirements under subsection 85(4);

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: respecting exceptions to the duty set out in subsection 87(5);

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the notice of withdrawal of consent referred to in subsection 90(1) and the information to be provided under paragraph 90(2)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: respecting exceptions to the duty set out in subsection 90(4);

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duty set out in subsection 92(1);

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the display of a sign under section 94;

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duties set out in section 96;

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duty to keep records under section 102;

Paragraph.Label: (x)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the liability of consumers and participating entities under subsection 103(1);

Paragraph.Label: (y)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duties set out in subsection 105(4) or section 111;

Paragraph.Label: (z)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duty to keep records under section 122;

Paragraph.Label: (z.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the annual report referred to in section 128, including the information that is to be included in the report and the form and manner in which, and the time within which, it is to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (z.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which information referred to in subsection 133(1) may be used as evidence;

Paragraph.Label: (z.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting exceptions to the prohibition set out in section 171; and

Paragraph.Label: (z.4)
Paragraph.Text: providing for anything that by this Act is to be provided for in the regulations.

Section.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under any of subsections 13(1) to (3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a notice given under section 25;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a notice given under section 38;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a directive issued under section 51;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under any of sections 55 to 57;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under subsection 60(1) or (3) or 61(1) or (3);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a notice issued under subsection 66(1);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a directive issued under subsection 67(2) or (7) or section 68;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a designation made under subsection 114(1);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under subsection 119(1);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under subsection 124(1);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under subsection 125(1) or 127(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: a direction made under section 148.

Section.Text: No later than the third anniversary of the day on which this section comes into force, and every five years after that, the Minister must cause a review of this Act and its operation to be commenced.

The provisions of this Act, other than sections 1 to 9, 13, 14, 74 and 113, subsections 114(1), (2), (4) and (5), paragraph 115(a) and sections 117 to 119, 123 to 132, 136 to 139, 143, 147 to 149, 169, 170 and 172 to 179, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les services bancaires axÃ©s sur les consommateurs

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference to âsections 131 and 132â.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act

Section.Text: The definitions participating entity, Senior Deputy Commissioner and technical standards body in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act are repealed.
Section 2.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The purpose of this Act is to ensure that financial institutions, the external complaints body and payment card network operators are supervised by an agency of the Government of Canada so as to contribute to the protection of consumers of financial products and services and the public, including by strengthening the financial literacy of Canadians.
Subsection 3(4) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 5.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may give a written direction to the Agency if the Minister is of the opinion that it can strengthen consumer protection and the publicâs confidence in that protection or enhance the financial literacy of Canadians.

Section.Text: The heading before section 7.2 and sections 7.2 to 7.4 of the Act are repealed.
Section 9.1 of the Act is repealed.
Section 10 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The employees that are necessary to enable the Commissioner to perform the Commissionerâs duties are to be appointed in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.
Subsection 11(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In respect of persons appointed under sections 8 and 10, the Commissioner is authorized to exercise the powers and perform the functions of the Treasury Board that relate to human resources management within the meaning of paragraphs 7(1)(b) and (e) and section 11.1 of the Financial Administration Act, and those of deputy heads under subsection 12(2) of that Act, as that subsection reads without regard to any terms and conditions that the Governor in Council may direct, including the determination of terms and conditions of employment and the responsibility for employer and employee relations.

Section.Text: Section 12.1 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsection 13(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Agency carries on any activity in furtherance of an object described in paragraph 3(2)(d) or (e) on the Ministerâs recommendation, the Minister may on terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, in any fiscal year, make a payment out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the Agency for the purposes of the activity.
Subsection 14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4) or a Deputy Commissioner must not hold, directly or indirectly, any interest or right in any shares of any financial institution, any bank holding company, any insurance holding company, the external complaints body or any other body corporate, however created, carrying on any business in Canada that is substantially similar to any business carried on by any financial institution or the external complaints body.
The portion of subsection 14(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4) or a Deputy Commissioner must not

Section.Text: Sections 14.1 and 15 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Ownership â payment card network operators
Section.Text: The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4) or a Deputy Commissioner must not hold, directly or indirectly, any interest or right in any shares of a payment card network operator.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 16(1) to (1.2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: No grant or gratuity to be made

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4), a Deputy Commissioner and any person appointed under section 10 must not accept or receive, directly or indirectly, any grant or gratuity from a financial institution, a bank holding company, an insurance holding company or the external complaints body or from a director, officer or employee of any of them and a financial institution, a bank holding company, an insurance holding company and the external complaints body, and any director, officer or employee of any of them, must not make or give any such grant or gratuity.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No grant or gratuity â payment card network operators
Subsection.Text: The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4), a Deputy Commissioner and any person appointed under section 10 must not accept or receive, directly or indirectly, any grant or gratuity from a payment card network operator or any of its directors, officers or employees, and a payment card network operator or any of its directors, officers or employees must not make or give any such grant or gratuity.
The portion of subsection 16(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person, financial institution, bank holding company, insurance holding company or payment card network operator that contravenes subsection (1) or (1.1) is guilty of an offence and liable

Section.Text: Subsections 17(5) and (6) of the Act are repealed.
Section 33 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No action lies against His Majesty, the Minister, the Commissioner, any Deputy Commissioner, any officer or employee of the Agency or any person acting under the direction of the Commissioner for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under any Act of Parliament are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.
Section 33.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner, any Deputy Commissioner, any officer or employee of the Agency or any person acting under the instructions of the Commissioner is not a compellable witness in any civil proceedings in respect of any matter coming to their knowledge as a result of exercising any of their powers or performing any of their duties or functions under this Act or an Act listed in Schedule 1.
Schedule 1 to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading âSCHEDULE 1â with the following:

AmendedText.OriginatingRef: (Subparagraph 3(2)(a)(ii), subsection 5(1), subparagraph 19(1)(a)(i), paragraph 20(e) and sections 20.1 and 33.1)

Section.Text: Schedule 1 to the Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les services bancaires axÃ©s sur les consommateurs

Section.Text: Sections 213 to 221 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2024, No. 1 are repealed.
Section 224 of the Act is repealed.
The Consumer-Driven Banking Act, section 198 of chapter 17 of the Statutes of Canada, 2024, is repealed.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 10
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Sunset Provisions)

Section.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (4), companies shall not carry on business after June 30, 2033.

Section.Text: Subsection 21(1) of the Bank Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (4), banks shall not carry on business, and authorized foreign banks shall not carry on business in Canada, after June 30, 2033.

Section.Text: Subsection 670(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (4), bank holding companies shall not carry on business after June 30, 2033.

Section.Text: Subsection 21(1) of the Insurance Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (4), companies and societies shall not carry on business, and foreign companies shall not carry on business in Canada, after June 30, 2033.

Section.Text: Subsection 707(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (4), insurance holding companies shall not carry on business after June 30, 2033.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 11
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Modernizing Limits on Borrowing, Loans and Investments)

Section.Text: The definition commercial loan in subsection 449(1) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is repealed.
The heading before section 460 and sections 460 to 466 of the Act are repealed.
Section 467 of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (c).
Section 468 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Divestment order â portfolios of loans and interests
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.2), the Superintendent may, by order, direct a company to reduce, within any period that the Superintendent considers reasonable, the aggregate value of one or more of the following categories of loans or interests by way of a disposition of a portion of that value:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the commercial loans held by the company and its subsidiaries;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the interests of the company and its subsidiaries in real property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the following interests of the company and its subsidiaries:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the participating shares of a body corporate that are beneficially owned by the company and its subsidiaries, other than participating shares of a permitted entity in which the company has a substantial investment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the ownership interests in an unincorporated entity that are beneficially owned by the company and its subsidiaries, other than ownership interests in a permitted entity in which the company has a substantial investment.

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may make an order under subsection (1.1) only on the basis of prudential considerations that the Superintendent considers relevant with respect to the aggregate value of the category or categories of loans or interests in question.

Section.Text: Paragraph 470(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: assets that are debt obligations that are

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: guaranteed by any financial institution other than the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: fully secured by deposits with any financial institution, including the company, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: fully secured by debt obligations that are guaranteed by any financial institution other than the company;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: assets that are debt obligations issued

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by, or by any agency of,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the Government of Canada,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the government of a province,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a municipality, or

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a prescribed international agency;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: assets that are debt obligations that are guaranteed by, or fully secured by securities issued by, a government, a municipality or an agency referred to in paragraph (a.1);

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: assets that are debt obligations that are widely distributed, as that expression is defined by the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: assets that are debt obligations of an entity controlled by the company;

Section 479 of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (c).

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may make an order under subsection (1.1) only on the basis of prudential considerations that the Superintendent considers relevant with respect to the aggregate value of the category or categories of interests in question.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Divestment order â portfolios of loans and interests
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.2), the Superintendent may, by order, direct a company to reduce, within any period that the Superintendent considers reasonable, the aggregate value of one or more of the following categories of loans or interests by way of a disposition of a portion of that value:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may make an order under subsection (1.1) only on the basis of prudential considerations that the Superintendent considers relevant with respect to the aggregate value of the category or categories of loans or interests in question.

Subsection.Text: Sections 612 and 615 do not apply in respect of assets of a foreign company held in relation to a segregated fund maintained under section 593.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exclusion of liabilities of segregated funds
Subsection.Text: A reference in section 615 to the assets in Canada or the liabilities in Canada of a foreign company does not include liabilities of the foreign company for the policies and amounts in respect of which a segregated fund is maintained under section 593.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Division come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 12
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Electronic Delivery of Governance Documents)

Section.Text: The Bank Act is amended by adding the following after section 14.11:
The Governor in Council may, by order, amend Schedule V by adding, deleting or amending a reference to a version of National Instrument 51-102 or National Instrument 54-101.
Section 992 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: NI 51-102âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V, means the version of National Instrument 51-102 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 51-102)
NI 54-101âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V, means the version of National Instrument 54-101 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 54-101)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 995(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite anything in this Part, but subject to subsections (3) and (4), a requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a notice, document or other information is not satisfied by providing an electronic document unless
Section 995 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders of a distributing bank or of a distributing bank holding company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 138(1) or 727(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available as an electronic document in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice-and-access â institutions that are not distributing
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders or members of a bank that is not a distributing bank, of a bank holding company that is not a distributing bank holding company or of a federal credit union for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 138(1) or 727(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent to the shareholders or members that contains only

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the time and place of the meeting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that the documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting are available as electronic documents on the website of the bank, bank holding company or federal credit union,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the address of the website of the bank, bank holding company or federal credit union on which the documents or other information are available,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that no paper copies of any document or other information for use in connection with the meeting will be sent unless they are requested,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: instructions on how to request the paper copies,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the matters to be voted on during the meeting, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the voting procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available to shareholders or members as an electronic document on the website of the bank, bank holding company or federal credit union.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (4)(c), a document or other information is considered to be available as an electronic document only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is available on the website on or before the day on which the notice referred to in paragraph (4)(a) is sent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it remains available on the website for at least one year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is in an accessible, printable and searchable format.

Subsection.Text: On the request of a shareholder or member of a bank that is not a distributing bank, of a bank holding company that is not a distributing bank holding company or of a federal credit union, paper copies of the following documents or other information must be sent by prepaid mail addressed to, or delivered personally to, the shareholder or member at their latest address as shown in the records of the bank, bank holding company or federal credit union, or of its transfer agent:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any document or other information made available under subsection (4) before the request is made; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all documents and other information made available under subsection (4) after the request is made.

Subsection.Text: A document or other information referred to in paragraph (6)(a) must be sent or delivered

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within three business days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received before the day of the meeting; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 10 days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received on or after the day of the meeting.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a document or other information that is required under this Act or the regulations to be sent to a specific place and that is made available under paragraph (3)(c) or (4)(c) is not required to be sent to an information system designated by the addressee.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule IV, the Schedule V set out in Schedule 3 to this Act.
Section 539.01 of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: NI 51-102âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V to the Bank Act, means the version of National Instrument 51-102 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 51-102)
NI 54-101âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V to the Bank Act, means the version of National Instrument 54-101 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 54-101)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 539.04(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite anything in this Part, but subject to subsections (3) and (4), a requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a notice, document or other information is not satisfied by providing an electronic document unless
Section 539.04 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders of a distributing company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 141(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available as an electronic document in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice-and-access â companies that are not distributing
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders of a company that is not a distributing company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 141(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent to the shareholders that contains only

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the time and place of the meeting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that the documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting are available as electronic documents on the website of the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the address of the website of the company on which the documents or other information are available,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that no paper copies of any document or other information for use in connection with the meeting will be sent unless they are requested,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: instructions on how to request the paper copies,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the matters to be voted on during the meeting, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the voting procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available to shareholders as an electronic document by making it available on the website of the company.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (4)(c), a document or other information is considered to be available as an electronic document only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is available on the website on or before the day on which the notice referred to in paragraph (4)(a) is sent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it remains available on the website for at least one year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is in an accessible, printable and searchable format.

Subsection.Text: On the request of a shareholder of a company that is not a distributing company, paper copies of the following documents or other information must be sent by prepaid mail addressed to, or delivered personally to, the shareholder at their latest address as shown in the records of the company, or of its transfer agent:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any document or other information made available under subsection (4) before the request is made; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all documents and other information made available under subsection (4) after the request is made.

Subsection.Text: A document or other information referred to in paragraph (6)(a) must be sent or delivered

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within three business days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received before the day of the meeting; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 10 days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received on or after the day of the meeting.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a document or other information that is required under this Act or the regulations to be sent to a specific place and that is made available under paragraph (3)(c) or (4)(c) is not required to be sent to an information system designated by the addressee.

Section.Text: Section 1034 of the Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: NI 51-102âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V to the Bank Act, means the version of National Instrument 51-102 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 51-102)
NI 54-101âin respect of a province set out in column 1 of Table 1 of Schedule V to the Bank Act, means the version of National Instrument 54-101 referred to in column 2.â(RÃ¨glement 54-101)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 1037(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite anything in this Part, but subject to subsections (3) to (5), a requirement under this Act or the regulations to provide a notice, document or other information is not satisfied by providing an electronic document unless
Section 1037 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders of a distributing company or of a distributing insurance holding company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 143(1) or 767(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available as an electronic document in accordance with the rules governing notice-and-access set out in NI 51-102 or NI 54-101.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to policyholders of a distributing company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 143(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent to the policyholders that contains only

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the time and place of the meeting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that the documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting are available as electronic documents on the website of the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the address of the website of the company on which the documents or other information are available,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that no paper copies of any document or other information for use in connection with the meeting will be sent unless they are requested,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: instructions on how to request the paper copies,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the matters to be voted on during the meeting, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the voting procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy that is required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available to policyholders as an electronic document on the website of the company.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice-and-access â companies that are not distributing
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the provision of a document or other information, including a proxy circular, to shareholders or policyholders of a company that is not a distributing company or of an insurance holding company that is not a distributing insurance holding company for use in connection with a meeting for which notice referred to in subsection 143(1) or 767(1) is sent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of availability of documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting is sent to the shareholders or policyholders that contains only

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the time and place of the meeting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that the documents or other information for use in connection with the meeting are available as electronic documents on the website of the company or insurance holding company,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the address of the website of the company or insurance holding company on which the documents or other information are available,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement that no paper copies of any document or other information for use in connection with the meeting will be sent unless they are requested,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: instructions on how to request the paper copies,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the matters to be voted on during the meeting, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the voting procedures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice is accompanied by any form of proxy and written request for voting instructions that are required to be sent under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the document or other information is made available to shareholders or policyholders as an electronic document on the website of the company or insurance holding company.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraphs (4)(c) and (5)(c), a document or other information is considered to be available as an electronic document only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is available on the website on or before the day on which the notice referred to in paragraph (4)(a) or (5)(a) is sent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it remains available on the website for at least one year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is in an accessible, printable and searchable format.

Subsection.Text: On the request of a policyholder of a distributing company or of a shareholder or policyholder of a company that is not a distributing company or of an insurance holding company that is not a distributing insurance holding company, paper copies of the following documents or other information must be sent by prepaid mail addressed to, or delivered personally to, the policyholder or shareholder at their latest address as shown in the records of the company or insurance holding company, or of its transfer agent:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any document or other information made available under subsection (4) or (5) before the request is made; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all documents and other information made available under subsection (4) or (5) after the request is made.

Subsection.Text: A document or other information referred to in paragraph (7)(a) must be sent or delivered

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within three business days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received before the day of the meeting; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 10 days after the day on which the request is received, if it is received on or after the day of the meeting.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a document or other information that is required under this Act or the regulations to be sent to a specific place and that is made available under paragraph (3)(c), (4)(c) or (5)(c) is not required to be sent to an information system designated by the addressee.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 13
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Equity Threshold Related to Public Holding Requirement)

Section.MarginalNote: Terminology changes â replacement of âtwoâ

Subparagraph.Label: (v)

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 416(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)

Subparagraph.Label: (v)

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)

Heading.Label: DIVISION 14
Heading.TitleText: Legislation Related to Financial Institutions (Powers of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions)

Section.Text: Paragraph 502(1)(b) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 503(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.001)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 505(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, but at least once in each calendar year, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of each company that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the company is complying with the provisions of this Act, whether the company is in a sound financial condition and whether the company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures. After the conclusion of each examination and inquiry, the Superintendent shall report on it to the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 506.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement, called a âprudential agreementâ, with a company for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to maintain or improve its safety and soundness, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect it against threats to its integrity or security or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 507(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a company, or a person with respect to a company, is committing, or is about to commit, an act that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the company, or is pursuing or is about to pursue any course of conduct that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the company, the Superintendent may direct the company or person to
Subsection 507(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a company does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the company to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Subsection 527.4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister â terms, conditions and undertakings

Subsection.Text: In addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, the Minister may, in granting an approval, impose any terms and conditions or require any undertaking that the Minister considers appropriate, including any terms, conditions or undertakings specified by the Superintendent to maintain or improve the safety and soundness of any financial institution that is regulated under an Act of Parliament and to which the approval relates, or that might be affected by it, or to ensure that such a financial institution has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 606(2) of the Bank Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 613(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, but, in the case of an authorized foreign bank that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2), at least once in each calendar year, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of each authorized foreign bank that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the authorized foreign bank is complying with the provisions of this Act and whether the authorized foreign bank has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada and adheres to those policies and procedures. After the conclusion of each examination and inquiry, the Superintendent shall report on it to the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 614.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement, called a âprudential agreementâ, with an authorized foreign bank for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to protect the interests of its depositors and creditors, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect it against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 615(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Superintendentâs directions to authorized foreign bank

Subsection.Text: If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, an authorized foreign bank, or a person with respect to an authorized foreign bank, is committing, or is about to commit, an act that is an unsafe or unsound practice in relation to the business or affairs in Canada of the authorized foreign bank, or is pursuing or is about to pursue any course of conduct that is an unsafe or unsound practice in relation to that business or those affairs, the Superintendent may direct the authorized foreign bank or person to
Subsection 615(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, an authorized foreign bank does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the authorized foreign bank to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Paragraph 635(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the bank has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 636(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.001)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 643(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, but at least once in each calendar year, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of each bank that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the bank is complying with the provisions of this Act, whether the bank is in a sound financial condition and whether the bank has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures. After the conclusion of each examination and inquiry, the Superintendent shall report on it to the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 644.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement, called a âprudential agreementâ, with a bank for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to maintain or improve its safety and soundness, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect it against threats to its integrity or security or maintaining or improving its adherence to those polices and procedures.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 645(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a bank, or a person with respect to a bank, is committing, or is about to commit, an act that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the bank, or is pursuing or is about to pursue any course of conduct that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the bank, the Superintendent may direct the bank or person to
Subsection 645(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a bank does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the bank to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Paragraph 954(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: determine whether the bank holding company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 955(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 957(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of each bank holding company that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the bank holding company is complying with the provisions of this Act and to ascertain the financial condition of the bank holding company or to determine whether the bank holding company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Section 959 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement, called a âprudential agreementâ, with a bank holding company for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to protect the interests of depositors, policyholders and creditors of any federal financial institution affiliated with it, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect the bank holding company against threats to its integrity or security or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures.
Subsection 960(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a bank holding company does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the bank holding company to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Subsection 973.02(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister â terms, conditions and undertakings

Subsection.Text: In addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, the Minister may, in granting an approval, impose any terms and conditions or require any undertaking that the Minister considers appropriate, including any terms, conditions or undertakings specified by the Superintendent to maintain or improve the safety and soundness of any financial institution that is regulated under an Act of Parliament and to which the approval relates, or that might be affected by it, or to ensure that such a financial institution has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Paragraph 671(1)(b) of the Insurance Companies Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 672(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.001)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 674(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, but at least once in each calendar year, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of each company, society, foreign company and provincial company that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the company, society, foreign company or provincial company is complying with the provisions of this Act, whether the company, society or provincial company or the insurance business in Canada of the foreign company is in a sound financial condition and whether the company, society or provincial company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures or the foreign company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada and adheres to those policies and procedures. After the conclusion of each examination and inquiry, the Superintendent shall report on it to the Minister.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 675.1(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a company, society or provincial company, for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to maintain or improve its safety and soundness, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect it against threats to its integrity or security or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign company, for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to protect the interests of its policyholders and creditors in relation to its insurance business in Canada, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect it against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 676(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a company, society, foreign company or provincial company, or a person with respect to a company, society, foreign company or provincial company, is committing, or is about to commit, an act that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the company, society, foreign company or provincial company, or is pursuing or is about to pursue any course of conduct that is an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the business or affairs of the company, society, foreign company or provincial company, the Superintendent may direct the company, society, foreign company, provincial company or person to
Paragraphs 676(1)(a) to (d) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cease or refrain from committing the act or pursuing the course of conduct; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: perform such acts as in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 676(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, a company, society, or provincial company does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security or does not adhere to those policies or procedures, or a foreign company does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security in relation to its business in Canada or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the company, society, foreign company or provincial company to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Paragraph 997(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: determine whether the insurance holding company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Subsection 998(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: Subsection 1000(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent, from time to time, shall make or cause to be made any examination and inquiry into the business and affairs of any insurance holding company that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the insurance holding company is complying with the provisions of this Act and to ascertain the financial condition of the insurance holding company or to determine whether the insurance holding company has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those policies and procedures.

Section.Text: Section 1002 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement, called a âprudential agreementâ, with an insurance holding company for the purposes of implementing any measure designed to protect the interests of depositors, policyholders and creditors of any federal financial institution affiliated with it, establishing adequate policies and procedures to protect the insurance holding company against threats to its integrity or security or maintaining or improving its adherence to those policies and procedures.
Subsection 1003(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, an insurance holding company does not have adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security or does not adhere to those policies and procedures, the Superintendent may direct the insurance holding company to take any measures that in the opinion of the Superintendent are necessary to remedy the situation.

Section.Text: Subsection 1016.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister â terms, conditions and undertakings

Subsection.Text: In addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, the Minister may, in granting an approval, impose any terms and conditions or require any undertaking that the Minister considers appropriate, including any terms, conditions or undertakings specified by the Superintendent to maintain or improve the safety and soundness of any financial institution that is regulated under an Act of Parliament and to which the approval relates, or that might be affected by it, or to ensure that such a financial institution has adequate policies and procedures to protect itself against threats to its integrity or security and adheres to those polices and procedures.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act

Section.Text: The heading before section 22 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 22(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.001)
Paragraph.Text: to any federal government agency or body, for purposes related to the Superintendentâs regulation or supervision of financial institutions, including purposes related to threats to the integrity or security of financial institutions or risks to national security,

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 22:

For greater certainty, the Superintendent may receive any information that is relevant to the exercise of the Superintendentâs powers or the performance of the Superintendentâs duties or functions.
Section 627.22 of the Bank Act is replaced by the following:
