An institution shall make the prescribed amount of all funds deposited by a cheque or other instrument into a retail deposit account available for withdrawal immediately or, if there is no prescribed amount, the first $250.
This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 627.01(1) of the Bank Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: consumer-targeted fraud, in relation to a product or service in Canada that is offered, sold or provided by an institution to a natural person other than for business purposes, includes a transaction that is unauthorized or that is authorized as a result of coercion or deception.â(fraude ciblant les consommateurs)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 627.13:

Subsection.Text: An institution shall not activate a prescribed capability for a personal deposit account in Canada without first obtaining, in accordance with the regulations, the express consent of the natural person who requested the opening of the account or in whose name it is kept.
An institution shall permit a natural person in whose name a personal deposit account in Canada is kept to deactivate a prescribed account capability.

An institution shall permit a natural person in whose name a personal deposit account in Canada is kept to adjust the following limits for each class of withdrawal or transfer of funds that can be made from the account:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum amount of a withdrawal or transfer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of withdrawals or transfers that can be made in a given period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum amount of all withdrawals or transfers that can be made in a given period; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed limit.

Subsection.Text: An adjusted limit may not, however, exceed any limit set by the institution.
The institution shall ensure that an adjusted limit takes effect within the prescribed period.
An institution shall, without delay and by electronic means, notify a natural person in whose name a personal deposit account in Canada is kept if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an account capability referred to in subsection 627.131(1) is activated;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an account capability referred to in subsection 627.131(2) is deactivated; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a limit referred to in subsection 627.132(1) is adjusted.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply if the natural person has opted out, in writing, of receiving the notice or does not provide the institution with the contact information required to receive it.
An institution shall establish and adhere to policies and procedures to detect and prevent consumer-targeted fraud and to mitigate its impacts.
The policies and procedures shall, together, set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the criteria that the institution uses to decide whether a transaction is suspicious;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the criteria that the institution uses to decide whether to suspend or cancel, or take other measures in relation to, a suspicious transaction;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: how the institution communicates a decision referred to in paragraph (b) to persons affected by a suspension, cancellation or other measure;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the criteria that the institution uses to decide whether a natural person is a victim of consumer-targeted fraud and, if so, whether a remedy is available to them;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the criteria that the institution uses to decide what types of remedies are available to a natural person who is a victim of consumer-targeted fraud;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: how the institution communicates a decision referred to in paragraph (e) to a natural person who is a victim of consumer-targeted fraud; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed criteria.

Subsection.Text: The institution shall provide its employees, representatives, agents or mandataries and other intermediaries who deal with customers who are in Canada with initial and recurrent training on the detection and prevention of consumer-targeted fraud and on the institutionâs policies and procedures.
The institution shall, in accordance with the regulations, prepare an annual report on consumer-targeted fraud in Canada and provide it to the Commissioner.
The Commissioner shall, in accordance with the regulations, prepare an annual report on the reports provided under subsection 627.134(4) and provide it to the Minister.

Any information in a report referred to in subsection 627.134(4) that could reasonably be expected to reveal the identity of an institution or of a victim of consumer-targeted fraud is confidential and shall be treated accordingly.

Section.Text: Section 627.998 of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (n) and by adding the following after paragraph (o):

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing account capabilities for the purposes of subsection 627.131(1) or (2);

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner in which express consent is to be obtained for the purposes of subsection 627.131(1);

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing limits for the purposes of subsection 627.132(1);

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing periods for the purposes of subsection 627.132(3);

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing criteria that are to be set out in the policies and procedures referred to in subsection 627.134(2);

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reports that are to be prepared under subsection 627.134(4), including their contents and the time limit for providing them to the Commissioner; and

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reports that are to be prepared under subsection 627.135(1), including their contents and the time limit for providing them to the Minister.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12.1(2) of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to insure certain pre-existing deposits
Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph 12(c) and subsection 4(2) of the schedule, but subject to the other provisions of this Act, if a pre-existing deposit is held by a federal credit union that is a member institution and all or a portion of the pre-existing deposit would have been guaranteed or insured under the law of a province if the local cooperative credit society had not been continued as a federal credit union, the Corporation must insure the pre-existing deposit during the transition period up to the amount that would have been guaranteed or insured under the law of that province.
Subsection 12.1(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12.1:

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: pre-existing depositâmeans a deposit that was made with a local cooperative credit society before the transaction day and that remains outstanding on that day.â(dÃ©pÃ´t prÃ©existant)

transaction day, in respect of the assets of a local cooperative credit society acquired by a federal credit union under a purchase agreement referred to in section 236.1 of the Bank Act, means the day on which the acquisition is made.â(date de lâopÃ©ration)
transition period, in respect of a pre-existing deposit, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a pre-existing deposit that is to be repaid on a fixed day, the period that begins on the transaction day and ends on that fixed day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other pre-existing deposit, the period that begins on the transaction day and ends on the day that is 180 days after that day.â(pÃ©riode transitoire)

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to insure certain pre-existing deposits
Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph 12(c) and subsection 4(2) of the schedule, but subject to the other provisions of this Act, if a pre-existing deposit is held by a federal credit union that is a member institution and all or a portion of the pre-existing deposit would have been guaranteed or insured under the law of a province if the assets of the local cooperative credit society had not been acquired by the federal credit union, the Corporation must insure the pre-existing deposit during the transition period up to the amount that would have been guaranteed or insured under the law of that province.

During the transition period, a pre-existing deposit, less any withdrawals from the pre-existing deposit, is deemed, for the purposes of deposit insurance with the Corporation, to be a deposit separate from any deposit made on or after the transaction day.

During the transition period, any withdrawal â up to the amount of the pre-existing deposit â is deemed, for the purposes of deposit insurance with the Corporation, to be withdrawn from the pre-existing deposit.
Subsection 13(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person has deposits with two or more member institutions â including member institutions insured under section 12.1 â that amalgamate and continue in operation as one member institution, in this section referred to as the âamalgamated institutionâ, a deposit of that person with an amalgamating institution on the day on which the amalgamated institution is formed, less any withdrawals from the deposit, shall, for the purposes of deposit insurance with the Corporation, be deemed to be and continue to be separate from any deposit of that person on that day with the other amalgamating institution or institutions that become part of the amalgamated institution for a period of two years or, in the case of a term deposit with a remaining term exceeding two years, to the maturity of the term deposit.
Section 13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

When a federal credit union acquires all or substantially all of the assets of a local cooperative credit society, the transaction is deemed to be an amalgamation of two member institutions for the purposes of this section.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 17:

Despite section 17, the Corporation must, in the manner and to the extent provided in this Act and the by-laws, insure the deposits held by a federal credit union that was a local cooperative credit society prior to the issuance of the letters patent in respect of its continuation for the purposes of amalgamation.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 23:

Section.MarginalNote: Calculation of first premium upon acquisition
Section.Text: When a federal credit union acquires all or substantially all of the assets of a local cooperative credit society, the premium payable by the federal credit union in respect of those assets is calculated in accordance with section 23, which applies, with any necessary modifications, to those assets as if they belonged to a member institution on the day on which the acquisition is made.
The schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading âSCHEDULEâ with the following:

AmendedText.OriginatingRef: (Section 2, subsections 11(2.1), 12.1(2), 12.2(2) and 14(1.01), sections 25.4 and 26.01 and subsection 45.2(3))

Section.Text: The definition consumer provision in section 2 of the Bank Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: consumer provisionâmeans a provision referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (a.2) of the definition consumer provision in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act;â(disposition visant les consommateurs)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 39.01:

Subsection.Text: When the Minister issues letters patent continuing a local cooperative credit society as a federal credit union under subsection 35(1), subject to the regulations, the Minister may, on application and by order, subject to any terms and conditions that the Minister considers appropriate, exempt the resulting federal credit union from the application of one or more provisions of Part XII.2 for a period of up to three years from the day on which its letters patent are issued, if the Minister is of the opinion that the resulting federal credit union has an acceptable plan to bring itself into compliance with Part XII.2 within that period.
The plan referred to in subsection (1) must be approved by the Minister and must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: how the resulting federal credit union would, as soon as reasonably feasible, meet the requirements of any of the provisions referred to in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any alternative requirements that the resulting federal credit union would meet during the period when the plan is in effect.

Subsection.Text: During any period in which a provision of Part XII.2 does not apply to a resulting federal credit union, the resulting federal credit union must comply with the plan approved by the Minister under subsection (2).
The resulting federal credit union may, at any time, seek the Ministerâs approval to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amend the plan; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: extend the period of the exemption to end no later than the third anniversary of the day on which its letters patent are issued.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting plans under this section, including regulations limiting the provisions of Part XII.2 that may be the subject of an order under subsection (1), and the Commissioner may make guidelines respecting such plans.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of resulting federal credit union
Subsection.Text: In this section, resulting federal credit union means the federal credit union resulting from the continuance, or the continuance and amalgamation, as the case may be, in respect of an application made under subsection 33(2), (3) or (4).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 227:

Section.MarginalNote: Simplified amalgamation into federal credit union

Subsection.Text: A federal credit union may, without complying with sections 224 to 226, amalgamate with one or more local cooperative credit societies that have applied, under subsection 33(3), to be continued as a federal credit union if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total assets of the local cooperative credit societies do not exceed 25% of the assets of the federal credit union;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amalgamation is approved by a resolution of the directors of the federal credit union and by separate special resolutions of the members and shareholders, if any, of each local cooperative credit society;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the resolutions provide that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the by-laws of the resulting federal credit union, including any by-laws respecting shares, will be the same as those of the federal credit union before the amalgamation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the head office of the resulting federal credit union will be in the same province as the head office of the federal credit union before the amalgamation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amalgamation does not add, change or remove any rights or privileges of any members or shareholders of the federal credit union prior to amalgamation; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a request has not been made under subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: The federal credit union must, in accordance with the regulations, disclose to all its members

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the federal credit union has entered into an amalgamation agreement and identify the local cooperative credit societies that are parties to that agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the amalgamation is subject to this section;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the amalgamation is subject to the approval of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the members and shareholders, if any, if a request is made under subsection (3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the members and shareholders, if any, of each local cooperative credit society,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the applicable provincial regulatory authorities; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other prescribed information.

Subsection.Text: The amalgamation is not required to be approved by the members of the federal credit union or its shareholders, if any, unless a request that a meeting be held to vote on the amalgamation is made, within the prescribed time, by at least two members entitled to vote at such a meeting, or at least one per cent of the total number of members entitled to vote at such a meeting, whichever is greater.
A meeting requested under subsection (3) must be called as nearly as possible in the manner in which meetings are to be called under the by-laws and this Act.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the timing, form and content of the disclosure to be provided to members under subsection (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the timing of a meeting request under subsection (3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of resulting federal credit union
Subsection.Text: In this section, resulting federal credit union means the federal credit union resulting from the issuance of letters patent under subsection 223(1.2).

Section.Text: Subsection 228(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), unless an amalgamation agreement is terminated in accordance with subsection 226(5), the applicants must, within three months after the approval of the agreement in accordance with subsection 226(4), the approval of the directors in accordance with subsection 227(1) or (2) or the latest of the approvals referred to in paragraph 227.1(1)(b), jointly apply to the Minister for letters patent of amalgamation continuing the applicants as one bank.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 236:

Section.MarginalNote: Asset purchase by federal credit union

Subsection.Text: A federal credit union may acquire all or substantially all of the assets of a local cooperative credit society only if the federal credit union also assumes all or substantially all of the liabilities of the local cooperative credit society.

An agreement of purchase and sale (referred to in this section as a âpurchase agreementâ) must set out the terms of, and the means of effecting, the acquisition of assets referred to in subsection (1).

Despite anything in this Act, the consideration for the acquisition of assets referred to in subsection (1) may be cash or fully paid securities of the federal credit union or in part cash and in part fully paid securities of the federal credit union or any other consideration that is provided for in the purchase agreement.

A purchase agreement must be sent to the Superintendent before it is submitted to the members or shareholders, as applicable, of the local cooperative credit society for their approval under the laws of the relevant province.

An application for the Ministerâs approval of a purchase agreement must be filed with the Superintendent by the federal credit union or the local cooperative credit society and must contain the information, material and evidence that the Superintendent may require.
If the Superintendent is of the opinion that the application includes all the required information, material and evidence, the Superintendent must certify receipt of the application.
The Minister may approve a purchase agreement if the Minister is of the opinion that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the local cooperative credit society has complied with the applicable requirements set out in regulations made under subsection (12);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase agreement has been approved by separate special resolutions of the members and shareholders, if any, of the local cooperative credit society; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the local cooperative credit society has obtained all of the approvals required for the sale under the laws of the relevant province.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must make a decision in respect of the application within 45 days of the day on which the Superintendent certifies receipt of it, but the Minister may extend this period for an additional 45 days if the Minister considers it appropriate to do so.
A purchase agreement has no force or effect until it has been approved by the Minister.
The Minister may, by order, grant to the federal credit union the transitional relief set out under section 39.011 or 231, subject to the same conditions set out in those sections.

When a federal credit union enters into a purchase agreement with a local cooperative credit society, the federal credit union may appoint one or more directors of the local cooperative credit society to serve as additional directors for a term expiring not later than the close of the federal credit unionâs next annual meeting.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting notice and disclosure requirements in respect of a purchase agreement under this section, including regulations that authorize the Minister to grant exemptions from those requirements.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 417:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 417, if, on the day before the day on which a local cooperative credit society is continued as a federal credit union under this Act, the local cooperative credit society or its subsidiary is engaging in any of the following activities, the federal credit union may, with the Ministerâs approval, engage in that activity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the leasing of motor vehicles in Canada for the purpose of extending credit to a customer or financing a customerâs acquisition of a motor vehicle;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: providing temporary possession of motor vehicles to customers in Canada for a purpose other than to finance a customerâs acquisition of a motor vehicle.

Subsection.Text: Despite section 417, if, on the day before the day on which it amalgamates with another federal credit union under this Act, a federal credit union or its subsidiary is engaging in an activity referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (b), the federal credit union resulting from that amalgamation may, with the Ministerâs approval, engage in that activity.
A federal credit union may, with the Ministerâs approval, engage in an activity referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the federal credit union was continued from a local cooperative credit society before the day on which this subsection comes into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the local cooperative credit society, or its subsidiary, was engaging in the activity on the day before it was continued.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in granting an approval under this section, impose any terms and conditions, including limits on where the activity may be engaged in and what types of motor vehicles and how many of those vehicles may be leased, or require any undertaking that the Minister considers appropriate.

Section.Text: Paragraph 468(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in any financial service activity that a bank is permitted to engage in under any of paragraphs 409(2)(a) to (d) or any other activity that a bank is permitted to engage in under section 410 or 411 or that a federal credit union is permitted to engage in under section 417.1;

Section.Text: Paragraph 482(2)(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: assets purchased or sold under a sale agreement that is approved by the Minister under section 236 or a purchase agreement that is approved by the Minister under section 236.1;

TitleText.XRefExternal: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act

Section.Text: The definition consumer provision in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions contained in a plan approved by the Minister under subsection 39.011(2) of the Bank Act;

Section.Text: The provisions of this Division, other than section 337, subsection 339(1) and sections 340, 343, 344, 348, 349 and 351, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Special Economic Measures Act is amended by adding the following after section 3.1:

Heading.Label: PART 1
Heading.TitleText: Measures in Relation to Foreign States

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance must be consulted before an order or regulation is made under subsection (1) if any of the following persons would be identified in the order or regulation:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign entity that is identified by the Financial Stability Board as a global systemically important bank;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign institution, as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, that carries on business in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign payment service provider, as defined in section 2 of the Retail Payment Activities Act, that directs retail payment activities, as defined in that section, at persons that are in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a central bank of a foreign state;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign entity that operates a stock exchange or a clearing and settlement system.

Section.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the Minister of Foreign Affairs is responsible for the administration and enforcement of this Act other than Part 2.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: federal financial institutionâmeans a financial institution, as defined in section 3 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act.â(institution financiÃ¨re fÃ©dÃ©rale)

foreign propertyâmeans any property that is situated in Canada and that is owned â or is held or controlled, directly or indirectly â by a person, including a foreign state, that is identified in an order or regulation made under subsection 4(1).â(bien Ã©tranger)

Subsection.Text: On the recommendation of the Minister of Finance made after that Minister has consulted with the Minister of Foreign Affairs, the Governor in Council may make regulations requiring that a federal financial institution provide the Minister of Finance with information on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any foreign property that is in the federal financial institutionâs possession or control; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any profits that the federal financial institution realizes from such foreign property.

Subsection.Text: The regulations may include rules respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the manner of determining the amount of any profits;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the non-application of the regulations to any foreign property or profits;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the time within which and the manner in which information must be provided; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter related to the administration of the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance may make an order directing a specified federal financial institution to pay to the Receiver General any profits realized from foreign property that is in the federal financial institutionâs possession or control, in the amount that is specified in the order or determined in the manner set out in the order.
The order may include rules respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time within which and the manner in which the profits must be paid; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter related to the administration of the order.

Subsection.Text: An amount payable under the order and any costs incurred by or on behalf of His Majesty in right of Canada in relation to recovering the amount are the liability of the federal financial institution that is required to pay the amount and constitute a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in any court of competent jurisdiction.
The order is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: The Minister of Finance is responsible for the administration and enforcement of this Part.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs, any Minister designated under subsection 6(2) or (3), the Superintendent of Financial Institutions and the Director of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service may assist the Minister of Finance in matters relating to the making, administration or enforcement of regulations referred to in subsection 14(1) or an order referred to in subsection 15(1) and, for that purpose, those persons and the Minister of Finance may collect information from and disclose information to each other.

The Minister of Finance may disclose to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police or the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada any information that is relevant to the making, administration or enforcement of regulations referred to in subsection 14(1) or an order referred to in subsection 15(1).

At the request of the Minister of Finance, the Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police may, for the purposes of this Part, disclose to the Minister of Finance any information received from a federal financial institution under an order or regulation made under Part 1.
The Act is amended by replacing âthis Actâ and âthe Actâ with âthis Partâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 4(4)(a) and (b) and subsection 4(5);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 6(2) and (3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(9);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 9(1) and paragraphs 9(2)(a) to (c);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 10(1);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 11(1) and (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 12.

Section.MarginalNote: Profits realized before coming into force
Section.Text: A regulation or order may be made under Part 2 of the Special Economic Measures Act with respect to any profits referred to in that Part that are realized before the day on which this section comes into force only if the profits are realized from property that is owned â or held or controlled, directly or indirectly â by Russia, as defined in section 1 of the Special Economic Measures (Russia) Regulations, or by a person who is identified in those Regulations.

Heading.TitleText: Related and Consequential Amendments to the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Section.Text: The definition sanctions evasion offence in subsection 2(1) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: sanctions evasion offenceâmeans an offence arising from the contravention of a restriction or prohibition established by an order or regulation made under the United Nations Act, under Part 1 of the Special Economic Measures Act or under the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act (Sergei Magnitsky Law).â(infraction de contournement de sanctions)

Section.Text: Paragraph 7.1(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order or regulation made under Part 1 of the Special Economic Measures Act; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 11.11(1)(b.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person or entity that is subject to a prohibition on financial or related services under Part 1 of the Special Economic Measures Act;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 53.6:

At the request of the Minister or an officer of the Department of Finance, the Director shall, for the purposes of Part 2 of the Special Economic Measures Act, disclose to the Minister or the officer any information received by the Centre under paragraph 7.1(1)(c) from a financial institution, as defined in section 3 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 19
Heading.TitleText: Basic Pension and Accommodation and Meals Charge

Section.Text: Section 74 of the Pension Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: covered periodâmeans the period beginning on April 1, 1985 and ending on December 31, 2026.â(pÃ©riode visÃ©e)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 74:
For the covered period, the basic pension payable as of a date set out in column 1 of Schedule IV is deemed to have been the basic pension set out in column 2 for that date.
For the covered period, the following amounts are deemed to have been calculated on the basis of the adjustments to the basic pension referred to in section 74.1:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that, under this Act, as it read before the coming into force of this section, or under any other Act of Parliament, was to have been adjusted at the same times and by the same percentage as the basic pension; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that, under this Act, as it read before the coming into force of this section, or under any other Act of Parliament, was to have been determined on the basis of the basic pension or an amount referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: In the event of a conflict or inconsistency between sections 74.1 and 74.2 and any other provision of this Act or a provision of any other Act of Parliament, or of any regulation made under this or any other Act of Parliament, sections 74.1 and 74.2 prevail.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 75(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Beginning on January 1, 2027, the basic pension shall be adjusted annually in the manner prescribed by regulation of the Governor in Council, so that the basic pension payable for a month in the following calendar year is the greater of
Section 75 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
In paragraph (1)(b), province does not include Yukon, the Northwest Territories or Nunavut and is deemed never to have included them.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 91:
The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of paragraph 75(1)(b), including regulations respecting the determination of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the average annual gross composite wage of categories of unskilled members of the federal public administration designated by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: income tax for a single person calculated in the province with the lowest combined provincial and federal income tax rate.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by regulation, amend Schedule IV by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adding âJanuary 1, 2026â to column 1 and the basic pension for that date in column 2; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: varying any basic pension set out in column 2.

Section.Text: Regulations made under section 91 for the purposes of section 75 and regulations made under section 91.1 or 91.2 may, if they so provide, have retroactive effect.
Schedule I to the Act is amended by replacing the reference after the heading âSCHEDULE Iâ with the following:

AmendedText.OriginatingRef: (Subsection 3(1), paragraphs 21(1)(a) and (2)(a), subsections 21(5), 34(6) and (7), paragraph 34(12)(a), section 41, subsections 42(3) and (5), 45(2), (2.1), (3), (3.01) and (3.1), 48(5), 49(1) and (2), 50(1) and 55(2), sections 70 and 71, subsections 71.1(1) and 71.2(2), paragraph 72(1)(a), section 74, subsection 75(3) and section 78)

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading âSCHEDULE IIâ with the following:

AmendedText.OriginatingRef: (Paragraphs 21(1)(b) and (i) and (2)(b) and (d), subsections 34(6) and (11), subparagraph 34(12)(a)(i), subsections 45(2), (2.1) and (3.3), section 46, subsections 47(3) and 52(1), paragraph 52(2)(a), subsections 52(4), 53(2), (5) and (6) and 55(2), sections 70 and 71, subsection 75(3) and section 78)

Section.Text: Schedule III to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading âSCHEDULE IIIâ with the following:
The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule III, the Schedule IV set out in Schedule 4 to this Act.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act

Section.Text: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act is amended by adding the following after section 34:
Beginning on January 1, 2027, the following benefits are to be adjusted annually only on the basis of the Consumer Price Index:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an award granted under section 32 or 32.1; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any compensation granted under section 5 of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Pension Continuation Act that is determined on the basis of rates provided for under the Pension Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the annual adjustment of the benefits referred to in section 34.1.
Regulations made under subsection (1) may, if they so provide, have retroactive effect.

Section.Text: The Department of Veterans Affairs Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations defining âprovinceâ for the purposes of any provision of the Veterans Health Care Regulations.
Regulations made under subsection (1) may, if they so provide, have retroactive effect.

Section.Text: For the period beginning on April 1, 1993 and ending on July 15, 1998, the term âprovinceâ in paragraph 20(5)(a), subparagraph 20(6)(b)(i) and paragraphs 23(5)(a) and (6)(a) of the Veterans Health Care Regulations is deemed to have referred only to Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Manitoba, British Columbia, Prince Edward Island, Saskatchewan, Alberta or Newfoundland and Labrador.
Section 33.1 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: In paragraph (4)(a), province means Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Manitoba, British Columbia, Prince Edward Island, Saskatchewan, Alberta or Newfoundland and Labrador.
Sections 363 to 370 come into force on the later of January 2, 2026 and the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Section 374 is deemed to have come into force on July 15, 1998.
Section 21 of the Veterans Well-being Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

For the purposes of subsection (1), the first annual adjustment to the monthly military salary shall be prorated to the number of days remaining in the calendar year as of the day on which the adjustment begins.

For the purposes of subsection (1.1), the day on which the adjustment begins is the day after the day on which the member or veteran is released from the regular force or reserve force, on which they complete their Class C Reserve Service or on which they die, as the case may be.
Subsection 21(1.2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (1.1), the day on which the adjustment begins is the day after the day on which the member or veteran is released from the regular force, on which they complete their Class B Reserve Service of more than 180 days or Class C Reserve Service or on which they die, as the case may be.
Subsection 21(1.2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (1.1), the day on which the adjustment begins is the day after the day on which the member or veteran is released from the regular force, on which they complete their Class A Reserve Service, Class B Reserve Service or Class C Reserve Service or on which they die, as the case may be.
Subsections 21(1.1) and (1.2) of the Regulations are repealed.
Section 27 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

For the purposes of subsection (1), the first annual adjustment to the value of variable A and variable B described in subsection 19(1) of the Act shall be prorated to the number of days remaining in the calendar year as of the day on which the earnings loss benefit begins to be payable.
Subsection 27(1.1) of the Regulations is repealed.
Section 27 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

For the purposes of subsection (2), the first annual adjustment to the value of the benefit referred to in subsection 23(1) of the Act and to the amount determined under subsection 23(3) of the Act shall be prorated to the number of days remaining in the calendar year as of the day on which the earnings loss benefit begins to be payable.
Subsection 27(2.1) of the Regulations is repealed.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the earnings loss benefit under the Veterans Well-being Act, as it read from time to time before April 1, 2019.
Regulations made under subsection (1) may, if they so provide, have retroactive effect.
Subsections 376(1) and 377(1) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2006.
Subsection 376(2) is deemed to have come into force on October 3, 2011.
Subsection 376(3) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2015.
Subsections 376(4) and 377(2) and (4) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2019.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 32.12(1)(c) of the English version of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness is of the opinion that that deployment has exposed or may expose those members to conditions of elevated risk.

Section.Text: Section 32.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

All claims for awards under this Part shall be dealt with and determined by the Minister as defined in subsection 3(1) of the Pension Act in the same manner as claims under that Act. All provisions of that Act that are not inconsistent with this Part apply, with any necessary modifications, to any claim under this Part.
The following definitions apply in sections 383 and 384.

Definition.Text: Actâmeans the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act.â(Loi)
coming-into-force dayâmeans the day on which this Act receives royal assent.â(date dâentrÃ©e en vigueur)

Section.Text: The Minister of Veterans Affairs is retroactively authorized to deal with and determine all claims for an award that were made under Part II of the Act before the coming-into-force day.

Any disclosure of information that occurred before the coming-into-force day and that would be authorized under section 32.3 of the Act if it occurred on or after that day is retroactively authorized.

For greater certainty, on the first day on which both this Division and section 112 of the Public Complaints and Review Commission Act are in force, that section 112 operates so as to replace every reference to âForceâ with a reference to âRCMPâ in the English version of the provisions enacted by this Division.
The Canada Development Investment Corporation Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to continue the Canada Development Investment Corporation

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Canada Development Investment Corporation Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: agent corporationâhas the same meaning as in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act.â(sociÃ©tÃ© mandataire)
Boardâmeans the board of directors of the Corporation.â(conseil)
Corporationâmeans the Canada Development Investment Corporation continued under section 5.â(Corporation)
directorâmeans a member of the Board.â(administrateur)
designated Ministerâmeans the Minister of Finance or, if another federal minister is designated under section 4, that minister.â(ministre dÃ©signÃ©)
wholly-owned subsidiaryâhas the same meaning as in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act.â(filiale Ã cent pour cent)

Section.Text: In the event of any inconsistency between this Act and Part X of the Financial Administration Act, this Act prevails to the extent of the inconsistency.
The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any federal minister to be the designated Minister referred to in this Act.
The Canada Development Investment Corporation, incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act, is continued as a corporation under this Act.
The head office of the Corporation is to be in Toronto unless the Governor in Council by order designates another place in Canada.
The Corporation is for all purposes an agent of His Majesty in right of Canada.
The Corporation, or any of its subsidiaries that are an agent of His Majesty in right of Canada, may enter into contracts with His Majesty as though they were not an agent of His Majesty.
In carrying out its purpose, the Corporation has the capacity, rights, powers and privileges of a natural person.

Subsection.Text: The mandate of the Corporation is to assist in the creation and development of businesses, resources, property and industries of Canada.
In carrying out its mandate, the Corporation must conduct all of its activities in the best interests of Canada and must do so in a commercial manner.

Section.Text: In carrying out its mandate, the Corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may provide advice and support to the Government of Canada, including to ministers of His Majesty in right of Canada, to departments, commissions, boards and agencies of the Government of Canada and to Crown corporations as defined in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act, respecting financial, commercial, economic and strategic matters arising in Canada or relating to Canadaâs interests;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may invest in entities owning property or carrying on business related to the economic interests of Canada, including by acquiring their shares or securities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may invest in ventures or enterprises, including through the acquisition of property, likely to benefit Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: may do all things that are necessary for the management, control or disposal of its assets or those assigned to it by the Government of Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is to perform any duties or functions required to be performed by the Corporation under any other Act.

Heading.TitleText: Board, Chief Executive Officer and Employees

Section.Text: The Board consists of a chairperson, the chief executive officer and at least 2 but not more than 10 other directors.

Subsection.Text: Each director, other than the chairperson and the chief executive officer, is to be appointed by the designated Minister, with the approval of the Governor in Council, to hold office during pleasure for a term of not more than four years such that, to the extent possible, the terms of office of not more than one half of the directors end in any one year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Appointment â chairperson and chief executive officer
Subsection.Text: The chairperson and chief executive officer are to be appointed by the Governor in Council, after consultation by the designated Minister with the Board, to hold office during pleasure for terms that the Governor in Council considers appropriate.

If the chairperson is absent or unable to act or if the office of chairperson is vacant, the Board may authorize one of the other directors to act as chairperson, but that person is not authorized to act as chairperson for a period of more than 90 days without the approval of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Absence or incapacity â chief executive officer
Subsection.Text: If the chief executive officer is absent or unable to act or if the office of chief executive officer is vacant, the Board may authorize an officer or employee of the Corporation to act as chief executive officer, but that person is not authorized to act as chief executive officer for a period of more than 90 days without the approval of the Governor in Council.
The Governor in Council may terminate the appointment of, or remove or suspend, any director.
A person is not entitled to hold the offices of chairperson and chief executive officer at the same time.
The chairperson and chief executive officer are eligible to be reappointed.

Despite subsection (1), if a director, other than the chairperson and chief executive officer, is not appointed to take office when the term of an incumbent director ends, the incumbent director continues in office until their successor is appointed.

Section.Text: The directors and employees of the Corporation, or of any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries that are agent corporations, are deemed to be employees for the purposes of the Government Employees Compensation Act and to be employed in the federal public administration for the purposes of any regulations made under section 9 of the Aeronautics Act.
Section 91 of the Financial Administration Act does not apply in respect of the Corporation or any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries.

The Corporation and any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries must not procure the incorporation, dissolution or amalgamation of their subsidiaries, or acquire or dispose of any shares in their subsidiaries, without the concurrence of the designated Minister, unless they are already exempted under another Act of Parliament from the application of section 91 of the Financial Administration Act in respect of that transaction.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation and any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries that are agent corporations are authorized to give guarantees in relation to any person in accordance with any terms or conditions specified by the Minister of Finance.

In calculating any applicable limit on the total value of the guarantees that the Corporation or any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries that are agent corporations are authorized to give under subsection (1) or under any other Act of Parliament, the value of any guarantees that the Corporation or subsidiary has insured or reinsured or with respect to which the Corporation or subsidiary has a right, by agreement, to be indemnified must not be taken into account.

Section.Text: The Corporation, or any of its wholly-owned subsidiaries that are agent corporations, may sell or otherwise dispose of or lease any property they hold and may retain and use the proceeds of the disposal or lease.

If a subsidiary of the Corporation is not expressly declared by or under any other Act of Parliament to be an agent of the Crown, the Governor in Council may, by order, declare that it is not an agent of His Majesty in right of Canada.

Despite the definition wholly-owned subsidiary in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act, any entity that becomes a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Corporation â through the realization of a security interest or through anything done under the Companiesâ Creditors Arrangement Act or any other similar Act of Parliament or law of any foreign jurisdiction â is deemed not to be a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Corporation for 180 days, or any longer period specified by the Governor in Council, beginning on the day on which it becomes a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), any information obtained by the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries in relation to entities in which they hold investments, other than wholly-owned subsidiaries, is privileged and a director, officer, employee or agent or mandatary of or adviser or consultant to the Corporation or any of its subsidiaries must not knowingly communicate, disclose or make available the information or permit it to be communicated, disclosed or made available.
Privileged information may be communicated, disclosed or made available if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is communicated, disclosed or made available for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of this Act and any related legal proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is communicated, disclosed or made available for the purpose of prosecuting an offence under any other Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is communicated, disclosed or made available to the Minister of National Revenue solely for the purpose of administering or enforcing the Income Tax Act or the Excise Tax Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: it is communicated, disclosed or made available with the written consent of the person to which it relates.

Subsection.Text: The authorized capital of the Corporation is an amount determined by the Minister of Finance, divided into shares with a par value of $100 each.

If the Board recommends that the designated Minister subscribe for unissued shares of the Corporation, the designated Minister may, with the concurrence of the Minister of Finance if the designated Minister is not the Minister of Finance, subscribe at par for the number of shares that the designated Minister considers desirable, and the amount of each subscription is to be paid to the Corporation out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the amounts that the Board requires.
The shares of the capital stock of the Corporation are not transferable and are to be held in trust for His Majesty in right of Canada.

Section.Text: At the request of the Corporation, the Minister of Finance may lend money to the Corporation out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund on the terms and conditions that the Minister of Finance fixes.
In sections 26 and 27, former Corporation means the Canada Development Investment Corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Copy of order provided to Director

Subsection.Text: The designated Minister must provide the Director, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Business Corporations Act, with a copy of the order made under section 388 of the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1.
For the purposes of the Canada Business Corporations Act, the copy of the order is deemed to be a satisfactory notice referred to in subsection 188(7) of that Act.

Section.Text: If the designated Minister is not the Minister of Finance, the Minister of Finance must transfer all the shares of the former Corporation to the designated Minister to hold in trust for His Majesty in right of Canada.
For greater certainty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection 13(5), the directors of the former Corporation, including the chairperson and chief executive officer, who hold office immediately before the day on which this section comes into force continue to hold that office in the Corporation for the remainder of the term for which they were appointed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property and rights of the former Corporation are the property and rights of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation is liable for the obligations of the former Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: unless the context otherwise requires, every reference to the former Corporation in any contract, instrument or act or other document executed or signed by the former Corporation is to be read as a reference to the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an existing cause of action, claim or liability to prosecution relating to the former Corporation is unaffected;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding pending by or against the former Corporation may be continued by or against the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a conviction against, or ruling, order or judgment in favour of or against, the former Corporation may be enforced by or against the Corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the by-laws of the former Corporation continue as the by-laws of the Corporation.

Section.Text: Section 8 and subsection 17(2) are deemed to have applied to the subsidiary referred to in section 261 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2024, No. 1 as of December 16, 2024.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Access to Information Act

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la Corporation de dÃ©veloppement des investissements du Canada

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference to âsection 21â.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

Section.Text: The Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act is amended by adding the following after section 10.3:

Subject to the regulations, on the request of an individual, an organization shall, as soon as feasible, disclose the personal information that it has collected from the individual to an organization designated by the individual if both organizations are subject to a data mobility framework.
The Governor in Council may, after consulting with the Office of the Privacy Commissioner, make regulations respecting the disclosure of personal information under section 10.4, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting data mobility frameworks and prescribing

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: safeguards that must be put in place by organizations to enable the secure disclosure of personal information under section 10.4 and the secure collection of that information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: parameters for the technical means for ensuring interoperability in respect of the disclosure and collection of that information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specifying organizations that are subject to a data mobility framework; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for exceptions to the requirement to disclose personal information, including exceptions related to the protection of proprietary or confidential commercial information.

Section.Text: Regulations made under section 10.5 may distinguish among different classes of activities, information or organizations.
Subsection 11(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An individual may file with the Commissioner a written complaint against an organization for contravening a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2 or for not following a recommendation set out in Schedule 1.

Section.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A complainant may, after receiving the Commissionerâs report or being notified under subsection 12.2(3) that the investigation of the complaint has been discontinued, apply to the Court for a hearing in respect of any matter in respect of which the complaint was made, or that is referred to in the Commissionerâs report, and that is referred to in clause 4.1.3, 4.2, 4.3.3, 4.4, 4.6, 4.7 or 4.8 of Schedule 1, in clause 4.3, 4.5 or 4.9 of that Schedule as modified or clarified by Division 1 or 1.1, in subsection 5(3) or 8(6) or (7), in section 10 or in Division 1.1 or 1.2.

Section.Text: Paragraph 16(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: order an organization to correct its practices in order to comply with Divisions 1 to 1.2;

Section.Text: Subsection 17.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Commissioner believes on reasonable grounds that an organization has committed, is about to commit or is likely to commit an act or omission that could constitute a contravention of a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2 or a failure to follow a recommendation set out in Schedule 1, the Commissioner may enter into a compliance agreement, aimed at ensuring compliance with this Part, with that organization.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 18(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may, on reasonable notice and at any reasonable time, audit the personal information management practices of an organization if the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe that the organization has contravened a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2 or is not following a recommendation set out in Schedule 1 and for that purpose may

Section.Text: Paragraph 24(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: encourage organizations to develop detailed policies and practices, including organizational codes of practice, to comply with Divisions 1 to 1.2; and

Section.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any person who has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has contravened or intends to contravene a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2 may notify the Commissioner of the particulars of the matter and may request that their identity be kept confidential with respect to the notification.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 27.1(1)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has disclosed to the Commissioner that the employer or any other person has contravened or intends to contravene a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has refused or stated an intention of refusing to do anything that is a contravention of a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has done or stated an intention of doing anything that is required to be done in order that a provision of Division 1, 1.1 or 1.2 not be contravened; or

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Paragraphs 2(3)(b) and (c) of the Broadcasting Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the right to privacy of individuals; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the commitment of the Government of Canada to enhance the vitality of official language minority communities in Canada and to support and assist their development, taking into account their uniqueness, diversity and historical and cultural contributions to Canadian society, as well as to foster the full recognition and use of both English and French in Canadian society.

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the English version of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes the objective of protecting the health, safety and security of the public;
Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that human pathogens and toxins pose varying levels of risk to the health, safety and security of the public;

Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that a lack of full scientific certainty regarding the risks posed by certain human pathogens and toxins is not to be used as a reason to postpone measures that protect the health, safety and security of the public;

Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that human pathogens and toxins evolve and can be altered and that new human pathogens and toxins appear continually, therefore creating unique challenges in meeting the objective of protecting the health, safety and security of the public;

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Act is amended by adding the following after the fourth paragraph:

Provision.Text: And whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that preventing the theft of sensitive information in relation to human pathogens and toxins contributes to the objective of protecting the health, safety and security of the public;

Section.Text: Section 2 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The purpose of this Act is to establish a safety and security regime to protect the health, safety and security of the public against the risks posed by human pathogens and toxins.

Subsection.Text: The definitions controlled activity, human pathogen, Risk Group 2, Risk Group 3, Risk Group 4 and toxin in subsection 3(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: controlled activity, in respect of a human pathogen or toxin, means any of the following activities:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: possessing, handling or using it;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: producing it;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: storing it;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: permitting any person access to it;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: transferring it;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: importing or exporting it;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: releasing or otherwise abandoning it; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: disposing of it.â(activitÃ© rÃ©glementÃ©e)

Definition.Text: human pathogenâmeans a micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is listed in the registry or in Part 2 of the schedule; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not listed in the registry or in Part 2 of the schedule but falls into Risk Group 2, Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4.â(agent pathogÃ¨ne humain)

Definition.Text: Risk Group 2âmeans a category of human pathogens that pose a moderate risk to the health of individuals and a low risk to public health and includes the human pathogens listed under Risk Group 2 in the registry. They are able to cause serious disease in a human but are unlikely to do so. Effective treatment and preventive measures are available and the risk of spread of disease caused by those pathogens is low. â(groupe de risque 2)

Risk Group 3âmeans a category of human pathogens that pose a high risk to the health of individuals and a low risk to public health and includes the human pathogens listed under Risk Group 3 in the registry. They are likely to cause serious disease in a human. Effective treatment and preventive measures are usually available and the risk of spread of disease caused by those pathogens is low.â(groupe de risque 3)

Risk Group 4âmeans a category of human pathogens that pose a high risk to the health of individuals and a high risk to public health and includes the human pathogens listed under Risk Group 4 in the registry. They are likely to cause serious disease in a human. Effective treatment and preventive measures are not usually available and the risk of spread of disease caused by those pathogens is high.â(groupe de risque 4)
toxinâmeans a substance that is produced by, or derived from, a micro-organism and that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may or may not be listed in the registry and poses a moderate to high risk to the health of individuals;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is listed in the registry and poses a moderate to high risk to the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon, with the risk and minimum quantity at which it poses that risk indicated in accordance with paragraph 9(2)(a); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is listed in Part 1 of the schedule.â(toxine)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: registryâmeans the registry established under subsection 9(1).â(registre)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: foreign entityâmeans a foreign economic entity, foreign entity, or foreign state as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Foreign Interference and Security of Information Act.â(entitÃ© Ã©trangÃ¨re)
terrorist groupâhas the same meaning as in subsection 83.01(1) of the Criminal Code.â(groupe terroriste)

Section.Text: Paragraph 4(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a drug in dosage form or a device whose sale is permitted or otherwise authorized under the Food and Drugs Act or a human pathogen or toxin contained in such a drug or device.

Section.Text: Section 5 of the English version of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
This Act is binding on His Majesty in right of Canada or a province.
Section 6 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who knowingly conducts any activity referred to in section 7 involving a human pathogen or toxin must take all reasonable precautions to protect the health, safety and security of the public against the risks posed by that activity.
Sections 7 to 9 of the Act and the heading before section 10 are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person must not knowingly conduct any controlled activity unless a licence has been issued by the Minister that authorizes the activity.
Despite the definition controlled activity in subsection 3(1), the following activities are not controlled activities:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any activity to which the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 applies that involves

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 2,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are not prescribed by regulation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: toxins that are not prescribed by regulation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: export authorized under the Export and Import Permits Act that involves

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 2,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are not prescribed by regulation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: toxins that are not prescribed by regulation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any activity to which the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 applies that involves the following human pathogens or toxins, unless the person who conducts the activity also conducts controlled activities to which that Act does not apply:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are prescribed by regulation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 4, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: toxins that are prescribed by regulation; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: export authorized under the Export and Import Permits Act that involves the following human pathogens or toxins, unless the person who exports also conducts controlled activities to which that Act does not apply:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are prescribed by regulation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 4, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: toxins that are prescribed by regulation.

Section.MarginalNote: Human pathogens and toxins â schedule
Section.Text: Despite section 7, a person must not conduct any activity referred to in that section in relation to a human pathogen or toxin listed in the schedule.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must establish and update a registry that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: lists any substance that, in the opinion of the Minister, is produced by, or derived from, a micro-organism and poses a moderate to high risk to the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may list

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein if they are of the opinion that it falls into Risk Group 2, Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any substance if they are of the opinion that it is produced by, or derived from a micro-organism and it poses a moderate to high risk to the health of individuals.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must indicate in the registry

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a substance referred to in paragraph (1)(a), that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it poses a moderate to high risk to the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the minimum quantity at which, in the opinion of the Minister, it poses that risk; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein referred to in subparagraph (1)(b)(i), the risk group into which, in the opinion of the Minister, it falls.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for any substance referred to in subparagraph (1)(b)(ii) that is listed in the registry, identify in the registry the minimum quantity at which, in the opinion of the Minister, the substance poses the risk referred to in that subparagraph.
The Minister must delete from the registry a micro-organism, nucleic acid, protein or substance if the Governor in Council adds it to the schedule under subsection 10(1).
The Minister must, in the registry,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amend the risk group of a human pathogen if the Minister is of the opinion that it falls into a risk group that is different from the one under which it is listed in the registry;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: add the references referred to in paragraph (2)(a) if the Minister is of the opinion that the toxin that is listed in the registry poses a risk referred to in paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: remove the references referred to in paragraph (2)(a) if the Minister is of the opinion that the toxin that is listed in the registry does not pose a risk referred to in paragraph (1)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: amend the minimum quantity that is listed for a toxin if the Minister is of the opinion that it is not the minimum quantity at which the toxin poses a risk referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or subparagraph (1)(b)(ii).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to the registry â name
Subsection.Text: The Minister may amend the registry by modifying the name of a human pathogen or toxin listed in the registry.
The Minister must make the registry accessible to the public by electronic means and by any other means that the Minister considers appropriate.

The Minister may, subject to any terms and conditions that they specify, delegate to an officer or employee of the Public Health Agency of Canada established under section 3 of the Public Health Agency of Canada Act, any of the powers that the Minister is authorized to exercise or any of the duties and functions that they are authorized to perform under subsections (1) to (7).

For greater certainty, section 5 of the Public Health Agency of Canada Act does not apply if the Minister delegates any of the powers they are authorized to exercise or any of the duties and functions that they are authorized to perform under subsection (8).
The registry is exempt from sections 3, 5 and 11 of the Statutory Instruments Act.
An advisory committee established under subsection 14(1) of the Public Health Agency of Canada Act is to, on a periodic basis, provide advice to the Minister in respect of the registry.
The Minister may at any time request the advisory committee to provide advice in respect of the registry.
The advisory committee must make available to the public the advice given to the Minister.
The portion of paragraph 10(1)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: add a substance to Part 1 of the schedule if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is produced by, or derived from, a micro-organism and poses a moderate to high risk to

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the health of individuals, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon, and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 10(1)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: add a micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein to Part 2 of the schedule if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(1) of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing (c).
Subsection 10(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may, by regulation, on the Ministerâs recommendation, delete a substance, micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein from the schedule if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that it is in the public interest to allow one or more of the activities referred to in section 7 to be authorized in relation to that substance, micro-organism, nucleic acid or protein.

Section.Text: The heading before section 11 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Consequences of Updating the Registry or Schedule

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 11(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Within 30 days after the date that an update to the registry under subsection 9(1) is publicly accessible, every person who, as a result of the update to the registry, no longer has lawful possession of a human pathogen or toxin must
Subsection 11(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Within 14 days after the date of publication of a regulation made under subsection 10(1), every person who, as a result of the regulation, no longer has lawful possession of a human pathogen or toxin must dispose of it in accordance with the regulations, if any.
Subsection 11(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person contravenes subsection 7(1) or section 8 by reason only that they possess a human pathogen or toxin in the circumstances described in subsection (1) or (2) if they transfer or dispose of it or obtain a licence or a variation of their existing licence authorizing possession of it, in accordance with subsection (1) or (2).
