If a human pathogen or toxin is listed in the registry, then no person may be convicted of an offence â in relation to that human pathogen or toxin â for the contravention of this Act or the regulations unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the registry with the human pathogen or toxin listed in it was reasonably accessible to the person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the human pathogen or toxin was listed in the registry; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the registry indicated, in relation to the human pathogen or toxin,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the risk group into which the human pathogen falls,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the toxin poses a moderate to high risk to the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon, the references referred to in paragraph 9(2)(a), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the minimum quantity at which the toxin poses a moderate to high risk to the health of individuals, if any.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 11(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a human pathogen or toxin is listed in the registry, then no person may be convicted of an offence or subjected to an administrative monetary penalty â in relation to that human pathogen or toxin â for the contravention of any provision of this Act or the regulations unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention,
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a licence holder has reasonable grounds to suspect that a human pathogen or toxin has been released inadvertently from a facility in the course of a controlled activity authorized by the licence, the licence holder must, without delay, inform the Minister of the release and provide the Minister with the information referred to in subsection (3) that is under the licence holderâs control.
Paragraph 12(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: dispose of the inadvertently produced human pathogen or toxin in accordance with the regulations, if any, or, if it is not listed in the schedule, transfer it to a facility where controlled activities in relation to that human pathogen or toxin are authorized.

Section.Text: The portion of section 13 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a licence holder has reasonable grounds to suspect that an incident involving a human pathogen or toxin that is in their possession has, or may have, caused disease in an individual, the licence holder must, without delay, inform the Minister of the incident and provide the Minister with the following information that is under the licence holderâs control:
Sections 14 to 16 of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a licence holder has reasonable grounds to suspect that a human pathogen or toxin that was in their possession has been stolen or is otherwise missing, the licence holder must, without delay, inform the Minister and provide the Minister with any information relating to the incident that is under their control and that the Minister may require. The licence holder must also take reasonable measures to locate the missing human pathogen or toxin.

If a person conducting controlled activities under the authority of a licence has reasonable grounds to suspect that any of the incidents described in subsection 12(1) or (2) or section 13 or 14 has occurred, the person must, without delay, inform the licence holder.

No information provided under sections 12 to 15 by a licence holder or a person conducting controlled activities under the authority of a licence may be used or received against that person in any criminal proceedings that are subsequently instituted against them, other than with respect to a contravention of section 17 or in respect of an allegation that the licence holder or person conducting controlled activities has shown wanton or reckless disregard for the health, safety or security of other persons.
Section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a licence holder has reasonable grounds to suspect that an incident meets all of the conditions below, the licence holder must inform the Minister of the incident and provide them, without delay, with the information related to the incident that the Minister requires that is under the licence holderâs control:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the incident is not one referred to in subsection 12(1) or (2) or section 13 or 14;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it involves human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are prescribed by regulation, human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 4 or toxins prescribed by regulation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is specified in regulations.

Section.Text: If a person conducting controlled activities under the authority of a licence has reasonable grounds to suspect that any of the incidents described in subsection 12(1) or (2) or section 13, 14 or 14.1 has occurred, the person must, without delay, inform the licence holder.
Section 16 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No information provided under sections 12 to 15 by a licence holder or a person conducting controlled activities under the authority of a licence may be used or received against that person in the following proceedings that are subsequently instituted against them:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: proceedings in respect of a violation enforced by an administrative monetary penalty; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: criminal proceedings, other than with respect to a contravention of section 17 or in respect of an allegation that the licence holder or person conducting controlled activities has shown wanton or reckless disregard for the health, safety or security of other persons.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 18(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, issue a licence that authorizes any controlled activity in any facility if the Minister is of the opinion that the conduct of the controlled activity in the facility poses no undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public.
Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Despite subsection (1), the Minister may issue a licence only if,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case that the applicant is an individual, the applicant and the biological safety officer designated for the licence are ordinarily resident in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case that the applicant is an organization, the applicant is incorporated, formed or otherwise organized in Canada and its representative and the biological safety officer designated for the licence are ordinarily resident in Canada.

Subsection.Text: In exercising the power conferred by subsection (1), the Minister may consider whether an applicant or any organization with which an applicant is or was affiliated owes an amount referred to in section 65.
Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

An application for a licence to authorize controlled activities in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are prescribed by regulation, human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 4 or toxins that are prescribed by regulation must include the information prescribed by regulation and make reference, in accordance with the regulations, to the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any contribution or other funding that originates from outside Canada that the applicant received or expects to receive in relation to their activities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the applicant is an organization, any person or foreign entity that has or is seeking to have any direct or indirect ownership or influence over the organization that would result in control in fact of the organization; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the applicant is an individual, any person or foreign entity that has or is seeking to have any direct or indirect influence over any activities of the applicant that are governed by this Act or the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 18(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the Minister refuses to issue a licence, the Minister must notify the applicant in writing of the reasons for the refusal.
Subsection 18(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A licence authorizes the controlled activities that are specified in it and is subject to any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate to protect the health, safety and security of the public.
Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

If the Minister considers it appropriate to impose licence conditions for the conduct of controlled activities to which the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992 applies, they must consult with the Minister of Transport and may consult with any other person the Minister considers appropriate.

The failure of the Minister to comply with the requirement to consult under subsection (4.1) does not exempt the licence holder and all persons conducting the controlled activities authorized by the licence from the obligation to comply with the licence conditions.
Paragraph 18(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the licence holderâs name and, if applicable, the name of the individual who is designated as a representative for the licence holder;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the biological safety officerâs name;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 18(5) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: a description of each part of the facility that is prescribed by the regulations made for the purposes of section 33.1;

Subsection.Text: Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obligation of the licence holder â information
Subsection.Text: If a licence authorizes controlled activities in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 and are prescribed by regulation, human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 4 or toxins that are prescribed by regulation, the licence holder must provide the Minister with the information prescribed by regulation and inform them, in accordance with the regulations, of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any contribution or other funding that originates from outside Canada that the licence holder received or expects to receive in relation to their activities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the licence holder is an organization, any person or foreign entity that has or is seeking to have any direct or indirect ownership or influence over the organization that would result in control in fact of the organization; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the licence holder is an individual, any person or foreign entity that has or is seeking to have any direct or indirect influence over any activities of the licence holder that are governed by this Act or the regulations.

Section.Text: Section 19 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, on the Ministerâs own initiative or on the application of a licence holder, vary a licence if the conditions referred to in subsections 18(1) and (1.1) are satisfied.
Despite subsection (1), the Minister may, if they are of the opinion that exigent circumstances exist, vary the licence even if the conditions referred to in subsection 18(1.1) are not satisfied.
A licence that has been varied under subsection (1.1) is valid for a period of up to 90 days after the day of the variance.

In exercising the power conferred by subsection (1), the Minister may consider whether the licence holder, any organization with which a licence holder is or was affiliated or a person conducting controlled activities under the authority of the licence owes an amount referred to in section 65.
The Minister may vary the licence on the Ministerâs own initiative only if the Minister first gives the licence holder a reasonable opportunity to make representations.

If the Minister varies the licence, the Minister may specify in writing any measures to be taken to protect the health, safety and security of the public that the variation in conditions may necessitate.
Subsection 20(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may suspend or revoke a licence in any of the following situations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is of the opinion that a controlled activity authorized by the licence is conducted in a manner that is contrary to this Act or the regulations or poses an undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a licence holder who is an individual or the biological safety officer designated for the licence is not ordinarily resident in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a licence holder that is an organization is incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada or that its representative or the biological safety officer designated for the licence is not ordinarily resident in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Minister suspends or revokes a licence, the Minister may specify in writing any measures to be taken to protect the health, safety and security of the public that the suspension or revocation may necessitate.

Section.Text: Subsection 22(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that there is a serious and imminent danger to the health, safety or security of the public, the Minister must notify the licence holder orally of the decision to suspend or revoke their licence.

Section.Text: Subsection 23(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When a request for a review is made, the Minister may specify in writing any measures to be taken to protect the health, safety and security of the public pending the Ministerâs final decision.

Section.Text: Section 31 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A licence holder must establish and maintain a list of all persons authorized by the licence holder to access the facility â including remote access by a means of telecommunication â to which the licence applies, including persons holding a security clearance for that facility and visitors. The licence holder must provide the Minister with that list if requested to do so.
Section 33 of the Act is replaced by the following

Section.MarginalNote: Access to facilities â security clearance
Section.Text: An individual must not enter â to conduct controlled activities â the part of a facility in which controlled activities are authorized in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4 and are prescribed by regulation or in relation to toxins prescribed by regulation unless they hold a security clearance for that part of the facility.

Subsection.Text: Except in accordance with the regulations, any individual who does not conduct controlled activities authorized in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4 and are prescribed by regulation or in relation to toxins prescribed by regulation is prohibited from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: entering a facility in which controlled activities are authorized in relation to those human pathogens or toxins;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: remotely accessing that facility by a means of telecommunication; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: accessing by any means, sensitive information, as defined by regulation, in relation to those human pathogens or toxins.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (1)(a) and (b) do not apply to an individual who holds a security clearance referred to in section 33 that authorizes entry to part of a facility referred to in paragraph (1)(a) in which controlled activities are authorized in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4 and are prescribed by regulation or in relation to toxins prescribed by regulation.

Moreover, if an individual holds a security clearance that does not authorize them to enter certain parts of a facility referred to in paragraph (1)(a) in which controlled activities are authorized in relation to human pathogens that fall into Risk Group 3 or Risk Group 4 and are prescribed by regulation or in relation to toxins prescribed by regulation, the individual may enter those parts or remotely access them by a means of telecommunication only if they do so in accordance with the regulations.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 35:

Subsection.Text: Despite sections 33 and 33.1, the Minister may, on any conditions that they consider appropriate, exempt any individual from the requirement to hold a security clearance under section 33 or to comply with the regulations referred to in section 33.1 if the Minister is of the opinion that the exemption

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: poses no undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is warranted by reason of exigent circumstances related to the health, safety or security of the public.

Subsection.Text: An exemption ceases to have effect on the earliest of the following days:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is specified by the Minister in the exemption;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the Minister revokes the exemption; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is one year after the day on which the exemption takes effect.

Subsection.Text: Within 30 days of exercising the power to exempt under subsection (1), the Minister must cause a report of the exercise, including the Ministerâs reasons for granting the exemption, to be referred for review to the standing committee of each House of Parliament that normally considers matters related to national security.â

Section.Text: Section 36 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant must, before a licence may be issued, designate as a biological safety officer for the requested licence, an individual who must have the qualifications set out in the regulations. The individual designated may also be the applicant.

A designation takes effect on the day on which the designated individual provides the Minister with their written consent to the designation or the day on which the individual begins to act as a biological safety officer, whichever is earlier.
The biological safety officer must exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions set out in the regulations.
A designation does not have the effect of limiting the obligations of the licence holder or any other person under this Act.
If an individual ceases to act as a biological safety officer, the licence holder must, without delay, designate another individual and inform the Minister of the new designation.
An applicant that is an organization must, before a licence may be issued, designate an individual as its representative for the requested licence.

The representative is the point of contact for any matters in relation to the licence and may exercise any of the licence holderâs powers and perform any of their duties and functions under this Act or that are prescribed by regulations, if any.
A designation does not have the effect of limiting the obligations of the licence holder or any other person under this Act.
If an individual ceases to act as a representative, the licence holder must, without delay, designate another individual and inform the Minister of the new designation.

Section.Text: Subsection 38(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The information that is to be provided may include information

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: regarding the human pathogens or toxins in the possession of the applicant, licence holder or biological safety officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: regarding the persons having access to the human pathogens or toxins referred to in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: regarding the facility in which the controlled activities are authorized or in respect of which an application for a licence has been submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: regarding the controlled activities that are authorized by a licence or in respect of which an application for a licence has been submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that is necessary to enable Canada to fulfil its international obligations; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that is necessary to enable Canada to submit Confidence-Building Measures in connection with the Biological and Toxin Weapons Convention.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the disclosure is necessary to address a serious and imminent danger to the health, safety or security of the public;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(1) of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is necessary to enable Canada to submit Confidence-Building Measures in connection with the Biological and Toxin Weapons Convention.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Except in the circumstances described in paragraph (1)(b) or (d), before disclosing the information to any person other than His Majesty in right of Canada or an agent of His Majesty, the Minister must obtain the personâs written agreement that they will maintain the confidentiality of the information unless they are required by law to disclose it.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 40:
In sections 40.2 and 41, document includes any report, book and electronic data and any other thing containing information.

Section.MarginalNote: Provision of documents, information or material

Subsection.Text: An inspector may, for a purpose related to verifying compliance or preventing non-compliance with any provision of this Act or the regulations, order a person to provide, in the manner specified by the inspector, any document, information or material specified by the inspector.
A person who is ordered by an inspector to provide a document, information or material must do so in the manner specified by the inspector.
Subsection 41(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to section 42, an inspector may, for the purpose of verifying compliance or preventing non-compliance with any provision of this Act or the regulations, enter at any reasonable time any place or conveyance in which the inspector believes on reasonable grounds that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an activity to which this Act or the regulations applies is conducted;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any material, equipment or document relevant to the administration of this Act or the regulations is located; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an activity could be conducted under a licence for which an application is under consideration by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: An inspector may be accompanied by any individual who the inspector believes is necessary to help them exercise their powers or perform their duties or functions under this section.
Paragraphs 41(2)(i) to (m) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: use or cause to be used any computer system, as defined in subsection 342.1(2) of the Criminal Code, or any means of telecommunication found there to examine electronic data that is contained in or available to the computer system or means of telecommunication;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: require any person in the place or conveyance to produce any document found there for examination or reproduction; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: examine, reproduce or cause to be reproduced, in whole or in part, any document found there and remove any reproductions.

Subsection.Text: Section 41 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsection (1), an inspector is considered to have entered a place or conveyance when they remotely access it by a means of telecommunication.

An inspector who enters â by remote access by a means of telecommunication â a place or conveyance that is not accessible to the public must do so with the knowledge of the owner or person in charge of the place or conveyance and only for the period necessary for the purpose referred to in subsection (1).
Subsection 41(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Lâinspecteur peut ordonner au propriÃ©taire du vÃ©hicule quâil entend visiter ou Ã la personne qui en a la possession, la responsabilitÃ© ou la charge de lâimmobiliser ou de le conduire en tout lieu oÃ¹ il peut effectuer la visite.
Section 41 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
An owner or person who, under subsection (3), is ordered to stop or move a conveyance, must stop or move it.
Subsections 41(5) to (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The owner or person in charge of a place or conveyance that is entered by an inspector who is carrying out their functions and every individual in that place or conveyance must give the inspector and any individual referred to in subsection (1.1) all reasonable assistance and provide them with any information that the inspector may reasonably require.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibition â obstruction and false statements
Subsection.Text: A person must not knowingly obstruct or hinder, or make a false or misleading statement either orally or in writing to, an inspector who is carrying out their functions or to any individual referred to in subsection (1.1).

An inspector who is carrying out their functions and any individual referred to in subsection (1.1) accompanying the inspector may enter on and pass through or over private property, and they are not liable for doing so.

Section.Text: Subsections 43(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an inspector has reasonable grounds to believe that a licence holder or any person in a place or conveyance where controlled activities are taking place is not compliant or is not likely to comply with one or more provisions of this Act or the regulations, the inspector may order the licence holder or person to carry out any measure that the inspector considers necessary to remedy or prevent the non-compliance.
A licence holder or person who is ordered by an inspector to carry out such a measure must comply with the order and, in doing so, does not contravene this Act or the regulations.

An inspector who orders a measure to be carried out must withdraw the order if they no longer have reasonable grounds to believe that the licence holder or person is not compliant or not likely to comply with this Act or the regulations.
Paragraph 46(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it does not pose a serious and imminent danger to the health, safety or security of the public; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 46(3) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, on hearing an application, the provincial court judge is satisfied that the applicant is entitled to possession of the seized thing and that it does not pose a serious and imminent danger to the health, safety or security of the public but is not satisfied with respect to paragraph (2)(c), the judge may order that the thing be restored to the applicant

Section.Text: Section 47 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If no application is made for the restoration of a thing seized under this Act within 60 days after the day of its seizure, or an application has been made but no order of restoration is made after the application has been heard, the seized thing is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

If an inspector has seized a thing and its owner, or the person in possession of it at the time of its seizure, consents in writing to its forfeiture, the thing is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.
Subject to section 48, the Minister may dispose of a seized thing that is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada in any manner that the Minister directs.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 52:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes a provision designated under paragraph (12)(a) commits a violation and is liable to an administrative monetary penalty of not more than $50,000 in the case of an individual and $250,000 in the case of any other person.
The purpose of the penalty is to promote compliance with the provisions of this Act or the regulations and not to punish.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Ministerâs powers â notice of violation
Subsection.Text: The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish the form of notices of violations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: designate individuals, or classes of individuals, who are authorized to issue notices of violations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establish, in respect of each violation, a short-form description to be used in notices of violation.

Subsection.Text: If an individual designated under subsection (3) has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has committed a violation, the designated individual may issue a notice of violation and cause it to be served on the person. The notice of violation must include the information prescribed by regulation.
A violation that is committed or continued on more than one day constitutes a separate violation in respect of each day on which it is committed or continued.
Proceedings in respect of a violation may be instituted no later than two years after the day on which the subject matter of the proceedings becomes known to the Minister.
After proceedings in respect of a violation are ended, the Minister may make public the nature of the violation, the name of the person who committed it and the amount of the penalty imposed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Directors, officers and agents or mandatries
Subsection.Text: If a person other than an individual commits a violation under this Act or the regulations, any of the personâs directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the violation is a party to the violation, whether or not the person is proceeded against under this Act or the regulations.

In a proceeding for a violation under this Act or the regulations, it is sufficient proof of the violation to establish that it was committed by the employee of the person named in the notice of violation, who is acting within the scope of their employment, or the agent or mandatary of the person named in the notice of violation, who is acting within the scope of their authority, whether or not the employee, agent or mandatary is identified or proceeded against for the violation, unless the person named in the violation establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the violation was committed without the personsâs knowledge or consent; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person exercised all due diligence to prevent its commission.

Subsection.Text: If any act or omission can be proceeded with as a violation or as an offence, the Minister may commence proceedings in respect of that act or omission as a violation or recommend that it be proceeded with as an offence, but proceeding with it as a violation precludes proceeding with it as an offence, and proceeding with it as an offence precludes proceeding with it as a violation.
For greater certainty, a violation is not an offence and, accordingly, section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply in respect of a violation.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting an administrative monetary penalties scheme, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designating any of the following as violations:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: non-compliance with the requirements in subsection 12(1), paragraph 12(2)(a) or section 13, 14, 14.1 or 15 that are in respect of notification or the provision of information, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 18(2.1), (6) or (6.1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 18(7) in relation to any licence conditions that are in respect of notification and the provision of information,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of section 30, 31 or 32,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of the requirement in subsection 36(1) to designate an individual who holds the qualifications set out in regulations, as a biological safety officer,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 36(3) in relation to any powers, duties and functions that are in respect of notification and the provision of information,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 36(5) or 36.1(4),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 38(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of subsection 40.2(2), 41(3.1) or (5) or 44(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: the contravention of a provision of a regulation made under paragraph 66(1)(k), and

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: non-compliance with any other requirements that are in respect of notification and the provision of information that are prescribed by regulation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the classification of each violation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information referred to in subsection (4);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the proceedings in respect of a violation; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the penalties, including in relation to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the determination of, or the method of determining the penalty applicable for each violation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment of the penalty imposed.

Subsection.Text: Any regulation made under any of paragraphs (12)(a) to (e) must provide that a person named in the notice of violation has the right to request a review of the decision that the person has committed a violation or of the penalty imposed, or both.
If the person named in the notice of violation pays, in the time and manner specified in the notice, the amount of the penalty set out in the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the amount is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister must accept that amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty in respect of the violation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: Instead of paying the amount of the penalty set out in the notice, the person may, in the time and manner specified in the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount of the penalty set out in the notice is $5,000 or more, request to enter into a compliance agreement with the Minister that ensures the personâs compliance with the provision to which the violation relates; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request a review by the Minister, in accordance with the regulations, of the decision that the person has committed a violation or of the penalty imposed, or both.

Subsection.Text: After considering a request by a person named in the notice of violation, the Minister may enter into a compliance agreement with that person on any terms and conditions that are satisfactory to the Minister. The terms and conditions may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: include a provision for the deposit of reasonable security, in a form and amount satisfactory to the Minister, as a guarantee that the person will comply with the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the reduction, in whole or in part, of the penalty for the violation.

Subsection.Text: A person who enters into a compliance agreement with the Minister is, on doing so, deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the compliance agreement was entered into.

A notice from the Minister that they are satisfied that a person who has entered into a compliance agreement has complied with it ends the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation. Any security given by the person under the compliance agreement must then be returned to the person.

If the Minister is of the opinion that a person who has entered into a compliance agreement has not complied with it, the Minister must cause a notice of default to be provided to the person informing them that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: instead of being liable to pay the amount of the penalty set out in the compliance agreement, the person is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of default, an amount that is twice the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation, and for greater certainty, subsection 52.1(1) does not apply in respect of that amount; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the security is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Once provided with the notice of default, the person may not deduct from the amount set out in the notice of default any amount they spent under the compliance agreement and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of default; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the notice provides for the forfeiture of the security given under the compliance agreement, that security is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If the person pays the amount set out in the notice of default in the time and manner specified in the notice of default, the Minister must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the amount owing in respect of the violation and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to enter into compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: If the Minister refuses to enter into a compliance agreement is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in the notice of violation, the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation.
If a person pays the amount set out in the notice of violation in the specified time and manner,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the amount is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty in respect of the violation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If a person does not pay the amount set out in the notice of violation in the specified time and manner, they are deemed to have committed the violation identified in the notice of violation.
A person must not be found to be liable for a violation under this Act if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent its commission.

Despite subsection (1), a person to whom the Minister causes a notice of default to be provided under subsection 52.3(4) does not have a defence by reason that the person exercised due diligence to comply with the compliance agreement.

Every rule and principle of the common law that renders any circumstance a justification or excuse in relation to a charge for an offence under this Act or the regulations applies in respect of a violation to the extent that it is not inconsistent with this Act and the regulations.

Section.Text: Sections 53 to 56 of the Act are replaced by the following:
Subject to sections 54 to 58, every person who contravenes this Act or the regulations is guilty of an offence and liable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contravention with respect to a human pathogen that falls into Risk Group 2, on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $250,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in all other cases,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months, or to both.

Section.Text: Every person who contravenes section 6 and, as a result, creates a risk to the health, safety or security of the public is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years.

Section.MarginalNote: Wanton or reckless breach of duty
Section.Text: Every person who contravenes section 6 and who shows wanton or reckless disregard for the health, safety or security of other persons and, as a result, creates a risk to the health, safety or security of the public is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 55:

Every person who knowingly and without lawful authority communicates sensitive information as prescribed by regulation to a foreign entity or terrorist group is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 57(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who knowingly contravenes section 8 is guilty of an offence and liable on conviction by indictment to a fine of not more than $5,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years, or to both.
Paragraphs 57(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months, or to both.

Section.Text: Section 58 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who intentionally releases or otherwise abandons a human pathogen or toxin in contravention of this Act or the regulations is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for life, if the result causes death or is likely to cause death to any individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for a term of not more than 14 years, if the result creates a risk to the health, safety, or security of the public.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 59 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: No person who establishes that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the contravention of this Act or the regulations may be convicted of that offence, except for an offence under

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 59(c) of the Act is repealed.
Section 59 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 56;

Section.Text: Section 61 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an offence under this Act or the regulations is committed or continued on more than one day, the person who committed the offence is liable to be convicted for a separate offence for each day on which it is committed or continued.
Subsection 62(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Summary conviction proceedings for an offence under this Act or the regulations may be instituted no later than two years after the day on which the subject matter of the proceedings becomes known to the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 63 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Directors, officers and agents or mandataries
Section.Text: If a person other than an individual commits an offence under this Act or the regulations, any of the personâs directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for the offence, whether or not the person has been prosecuted or convicted.
The portion of section 64 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Offences by employees or agents or mandataries
Section.Text: In a prosecution for an offence under this Act or the regulations, it is sufficient proof of the offence to establish that it was committed by the accusedâs employee acting within the scope of their employment, or the accusedâs agent or mandatary acting within the scope of their authority, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified or prosecuted for the offence, unless the accused establishes that

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 65 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The following constitute debts due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such in any court of competent jurisdiction:

Subsection.Text: Section 65 of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any administrative monetary penalty required to be paid under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is required to be paid under a compliance agreement entered into under subsection 52.3(1);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any amount set out in a notice referred to in paragraph 52.3(4)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of any reasonable expenses incurred in attempting to recover an amount referred to in paragraph (d), (e) or (f).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 66(1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: defining any term that is used but not defined in this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the safety and security of activities to which this Act or the regulations made under it apply;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conduct of controlled activities, including in relation to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: containment levels for human pathogens or toxins, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the decontamination of material, equipment, places, conveyances and persons contaminated by human pathogens or toxins;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting licensing, including the conditions that must be met for a licence to be issued, the conditions that must be complied with under a licence, the renewal, suspension and revocation of a licence and the variation of an existing licence;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(c) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding âandâ at the end of subpargraph (iv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: security requirements in relation to information technology;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 66(1)(d) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting access, including remote access by a means of telecommunication, to facilities in which controlled activities are authorized, including

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting incidents referred to in section 14.1;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the human pathogens and toxins for the purposes of subparagraphs 7(2)(c)(i) and (iii) and (d)(i) and (iii) and section 33;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the human pathogen and toxins for the purposes of subparagraphs 7(2)(c)(i) and (iii) and (d)(i) and (iii), section 14.1, subsections 18(2.1) and (6.1) and sections 33 and 33.1;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 66(1)(g) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting security clearances for the purposes of section 33 and regulations made under paragraph (g.1), including

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of section 33.1, the conditions to be met, which could include requirements for a security clearance, to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: enter a facility in which controlled activities are authorized in relation to human pathogens or toxins that are referred to in paragraph 33.1(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: remotely access a facility by a means of telecommunication, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: access sensitive information referred to in paragraph 33.1(1)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (g.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the management of any sensitive information;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(h) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 66(1)(i) of English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the qualifications, powers, duties and functions of biological safety officers;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the powers, duties and functions of the representative for the purposes of subsection 36.1(2);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the establishment, content and maintenance of inventories of human pathogens and toxins, as well as the provision of reports on those inventories;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (i.1):

Paragraph.Label: (i.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting sensitive information for the purpose of section 56;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(l) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the provision of information to the Minister that is necessary for the administration of this Act and the regulations;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (l.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting information that is to be provided to the Minister under subsection 18(2.1) or (6.1);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: exempting, on any conditions that the Governor in Council deems appropriate, any person or class of persons, any activity or any human pathogen or toxin from the application of any provision of this Act or the regulations if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that the exemption is in the public interest and poses no undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(o) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: o)
Paragraph.Text: prÃ©voir toute autre mesure dâordre rÃ©glementaire prÃ©vue par la prÃ©sente loi.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(p) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 67(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may make an interim order containing any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under section 66 if the Minister is of the opinion that prompt measures are required to address a serious or imminent danger to the health, safety or security of the public.
Paragraph 67(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 90 days after the day on which it is made, unless it is approved by the Governor in Council,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Pour lâapplication des dispositions de la prÃ©sente loi â exception faite du prÃ©sent article â, la mention des rÃ¨glements pris en vertu de la prÃ©sente loi vaut mention des arrÃªtÃ©s dâurgence; en cas de renvoi Ã la disposition habilitante, elle vaut mention du passage des arrÃªtÃ©s dâurgence comportant les mÃªmes dispositions quâun rÃ¨glement pris en vertu de cette disposition.

Section.Text: Sections 68 and 69 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Incorporation by reference â limitation removed

Subsection.Text: The limitation set out in paragraph 18.1(2)(a) of the Statutory Instruments Act to the effect that a document must be incorporated as it exists on a particular date does not apply to any power to make regulations under this Act.

A regulation may incorporate by reference documents â as they exist on a particular date or as they are amended from time to time â that the Minister produces jointly with another government for the purpose of harmonizing the regulation with other laws.

Section.Text: Schedules 1 to 5 to the Act are replaced by the schedule set out in Schedule 5 to this Act.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 2(1)(c) of the English version of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other factor that is relevant to the protection of the health, safety or security of the public.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
The Minister may renew a licence on application of the licence holder, for further periods set out in subsection (2), if the requirements in subsections 18(1) to (1.2) of the Act are met.
The portion of paragraph 4(1)(f) before subparagraph (i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who discovers during the conduct of a controlled activity that they are inadvertently in possession of a human pathogen or toxin that is not listed in the schedule to the Act in respect of which that controlled activity is not authorized by the licence must take all of the following steps:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(2)(c) of the English version of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide the biological safety officer with any other information that is relevant to preventing any undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public.

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to the Minister â risk reduction
Subsection.Text: If a human pathogen for which controlled activities are authorized by a licence is modified to the extent that it poses a lower risk to the health of individuals or to public health, the licence holder must, without delay, notify the Minister.
The portion of subsection 10(1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The following human pathogens and toxins are prescribed for the purposes of the Act:
Paragraph 10(1)(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: toxins that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are on that List and listed in the registry, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are present in a part of a facility in a quantity that is equal to or greater than the minimum quantity at which, in the opinion of the Minister, they pose a moderate to high risk to the health, safety or security of the public due to a reasonable risk of intentional use as a biological weapon.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(2) of the Regulations and the table in section 10 are repealed.
The portion of section 14 of the English version of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The Minister must issue a security clearance if the Minister determines that the applicant does not pose an undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public after considering the information obtained under sections 12 and 13 and taking the following factors into account:

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 14(b)(iii) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (iii)
Paragraph.Text: is or has been a member of a terrorist group or is or has been involved in â or contributes or has contributed to â the activities of such a group,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 14(c) of the English version of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether there are reasonable grounds to suspect that the applicant is in a position in which there is a risk that they could be induced to commit an act or to assist or abet any person to commit an act that might constitute an undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public;

Section.Text: Subsection 21(1) of the English version of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister must revoke a security clearance if the Minister determines that the holder of the security clearance poses an undue risk to the health, safety or security of the public after considering any of the information described in paragraphs 20(a) to (c).

Section.Text: Section 26 of the Regulations is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The words and expressions used in this section and sections 454 and 455 have the same meaning as in the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act.

On the day on which section 406 comes into force, any person who, under paragraph 7(2)(c) or (d) of the Human Pathogens or Toxins Act, conducts controlled activities that are not authorized by their licence, must, within 90 days after that day, either apply to the Minister to vary their licence to authorize those activities or stop conducting those activities.
A person referred to in subsection (2) does not contravene subsection 7(1) of the Human Pathogens or Toxins Act if they comply with subsection (2).

Section.Text: After the day on which section 423 comes into force, a licence holder that is an organization must, without delay, provide the Minister with the name of its representative.
The validity period of a licence may be extended for a period of up to 60 days beginning on the day on which this Act receives royal assent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the validity period of the licence ends within 30 days after the day on which this Act receives royal assent; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, the licence holder, its representative, if any, or the biological safety officer designated for the licence do not meet the requirements referred to in subsection 18(1.1) of the Human Pathogen and Toxins Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 402(3), section 413, subsections 415(3), (8) and (9), sections 421 and 422, subsections 442(4), (6) to (9) and (14) and subsection 450(2) come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 409(4), sections 414 and 431 and subsection 441(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 433 and subsections 436(3) and 442(12) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 109(c) and (d) of the Customs Tariff are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: destroyed in the manner that the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness may direct, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subject to regulations made under section 109.1, donated to a registered charity, as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act, in the manner that that Minister may direct and for any purpose that that Minister may specify; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: not damaged before their destruction or donation.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 109:
The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations prescribing the goods or classes of goods that may be donated for the purposes of subparagraph 109(c)(ii).
This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 3(1) of the Export and Import Permits Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: to ensure that the supply and distribution of the article in Canada or internationally are consistent with Canadaâs economic security interests; or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: to respond to acts, policies or practices of a foreign country or an association of countries that might be detrimental to Canadaâs economic security interests.

Section.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (e), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: to ensure that the supply and distribution of the article in Canada or internationally are consistent with Canadaâs economic security interests.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: The definition Canadian aviation document in subsection 3(1) of the Aeronautics Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Canadian aviation documentâmeans, subject to subsections (3) and (4), any licence, permit, accreditation, certificate or other document issued by the Minister under Part I to or with respect to any person or in respect of any aeronautical product, aerodrome, facility or service;â(document dâaviation canadien)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: goodsâmeans anything that may be taken or placed on board an aircraft, or that may be brought into an aerodrome or other aviation facility, including personal belongings, baggage, cargo and conveyances;â(bien)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(3) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (b), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an authorization issued under section 7.42.

Subsection.Text: Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
An authorization granted under subsection 4.3(1), (1.1) or (3) is not a Canadian aviation document.

Section.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to any regulations under paragraph 4.9(w), this Part applies in respect of aeronautics to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all persons and all aeronautical products and other things in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all persons outside Canada who hold Canadian aviation documents;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all Canadian aircraft outside Canada and all passengers and crew members on Canadian aircraft outside Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: all persons and goods on board aircraft bound for Canada.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.1:

Subsection.Text: If a provision of this Part or of any regulation, notice, order, security measure or emergency direction made under this Part requires a notice or other document to be served personally or by registered or certified mail, it may also be served by electronic means.
Service by electronic means may be proved by a record of electronic transmission setting out the date and time of transmission.
Service by electronic means is considered effective on the day on which it is sent, as indicated on the record of transmission.

Section.Text: Subsection 4.2(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (n) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (n.1)
Paragraph.Text: manage the use of remotely piloted aircraft systems and equipment or systems that can cause interference with the operation of remotely piloted aircraft systems; and

Section.Text: Section 4.7 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In sections 4.71 to 4.85, screening means a screening, including a search, carried out in the manner and under the circumstances prescribed in aviation security regulations, security measures, emergency directions or interim orders.
Paragraph 4.71(2)(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation security responsibilities to develop and document security management systems or other systems in relation to aviation security and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation security responsibilities to develop and document processes, procedures, programs or plans in relation to aviation security and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (k.2)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation security responsibilities to develop documents, such as manuals, in relation to aviation security and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (k.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the content and requirements of a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document referred to in any of paragraphs (k) to (k.2);

Paragraph.Label: (k.4)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation security responsibilities to comply with the provisions or requirements of a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document referred to in any of paragraphs (k) to (k.2);

Section.Text: Section 4.87 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person authorized by the Minister to verify compliance with aviation security regulations, security measures, emergency directions or interim orders, or to test the effectiveness of equipment, systems and processes used with respect to aircraft, aerodromes and other aviation facilities, does not commit an offence or violation if the person commits any act or omission that is required in the course of any such verification or testing and that would otherwise constitute a contravention of an aviation security regulation, a security measure, an emergency direction or an interim order.
The Act is amended by adding the following before section 4.9:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.9:
Without limiting the generality of section 4.9, regulations may be made under that section

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation safety responsibilities to develop and document safety management systems or other systems in relation to aviation safety and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation safety responsibilities to develop and document processes, procedures, programs or plans in relation to aviation safety and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation safety responsibilities to develop documents, such as manuals, in relation to aviation safety and to keep them up to date;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the content and requirements of a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (c); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: requiring a person or class of persons having aviation safety responsibilities to comply with the provisions or requirements of a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (c).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.91:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Heading.TitleText: Prohibitions and Restrictions on the Operation of Aircraft

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.1:

Heading.TitleText: Systems, Processes, Procedures, Programs, Plans and Documents

Section.Text: In sections 5.12 to 5.14, regulatory requirement means a requirement of a regulation made under subsection 4.71(1) or section 4.9 or a requirement of a security measure.

If a person has developed and is keeping up to date a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document under a regulatory requirement and the Minister is of the opinion that the system, process, procedure, program, plan or document has deficiencies that risk compromising aviation safety or security, the Minister may, by notice sent to the person, require the person to take the necessary corrective measures within a period specified by the Minister.

If a person has developed and is keeping up to date a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document under a regulatory requirement and the Minister is of the opinion that the person is carrying out an operation referred to in that system, process, procedure, program, plan or document in a manner that risks compromising aviation safety or security, the Minister may, by notice sent to the person, require the person to take the necessary corrective measures within a period specified by the Minister.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to the following documents:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a document developed for the purpose of documenting a system, process, procedure, program or plan under a regulatory requirement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a document developed under a regulatory requirement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a document the provisions or requirements of which a person is to comply with under a regulatory requirement; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a notice referred to in section 5.12 or 5.13.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.3:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of promoting aviation safety and security, the Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish and administer programs under which a person or organization may provide information related to aviation safety or security; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: enter into arrangements or agreements with any person or organization respecting the provision of information related to aviation safety or security.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the programs, arrangements and agreements referred to in subsection (1) and, without restricting the generality of the foregoing, may make regulations prescribing circumstances for the purposes of paragraph 5.32(j) or 5.33(f).

Section.Text: Information obtained by the Minister under a program, arrangement or agreement referred to in subsection 5.31(1) is confidential and must not be disclosed by any person unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person or organization that provided the information consents in writing to its disclosure;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information is required to be provided under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information was also obtained by means other than under the program, arrangement or agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is required under an Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is required for compliance with a subpoena or document issued or order made by a court, person or body with jurisdiction to compel the production of information or for compliance with rules of court relating to the production of information;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a contravention of any provision of this Part or any regulation, notice, order, security measure or emergency direction made under this Part, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accident or a security incident that must be reported under this Act or an accident that must be reported under the Canadian Transportation Accident Investigation and Safety Board Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to a contravention of subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to a contravention of any provision of this Part â other than subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1) â or any regulation, notice, order, security measure or emergency direction made under this Part and the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the contravention was committed knowingly or under circumstances amounting to gross negligence or, in Quebec, gross fault;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is, in the Ministerâs opinion, necessary to deal with

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a significant risk, direct or indirect, to aviation safety or the safety of the public, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an immediate threat to aviation security, the security of any aircraft or aerodrome or other aviation facility or the safety of the public, passengers or crew members; or

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is authorized in the circumstances prescribed by regulations.

Section.Text: Any information provided under a program, arrangement or agreement referred to in subsection 5.31(1) is not to be used against the person who provided it in any proceedings in respect of a contravention under this Act unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information is required to be provided under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information was also obtained by means other than under the program, arrangement or agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a contravention of any provision of this Part or any regulation, notice, order, security measure or emergency direction made under this Part, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accident or a security incident that must be reported under this Act or an accident that must be reported under the Canadian Transportation Accident Investigation and Safety Board Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to a contravention of subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the information relates to a contravention of any provision of this Part â other than subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1) â or any regulation, notice, order, security measure or emergency direction made under this Part and the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the contravention was committed knowingly or under circumstances amounting to gross negligence or, in Quebec, gross fault; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances prescribed by regulations exist.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6.41(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to give effect to an international standard; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to give effect to an international agreement, convention or arrangement to which Canada is a party.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6.41(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Authorized deputy may make interim order
Subsection.Text: The Minister may authorize, subject to any restrictions or conditions that the Minister may specify, their deputy to make, for any reason referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (e), an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Part.
Subsection 6.41(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An interim order has effect from the day on which it is made, as if it were a regulation made under this Part, and ceases to have effect one year after the day on which it is made unless it is approved by the Governor in Council within that one-year period.
Paragraph 6.41(3)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where no such regulation is made, three years after the day on which the interim order is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 7.21(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may suspend, or refuse to issue, amend or renew, a Canadian aviation document if a certificate under section 8.11 has been issued to the Minister in respect of the applicant for, or the holder of, the document or in respect of the owner or operator of an aircraft, aerodrome, airport or other facility to which the document relates.
Subsection 7.3(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: except as authorized under this Part and subject to subsection (1.1), intentionally interfere with the operation of a remotely piloted aircraft system, or with the performance of the duties of any crew member of a remotely piloted aircraft system, while the system is powered on;

Subsection.Text: Section 7.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Paragraph (1)(d.1) does not apply to a person who is carrying out activities that they are authorized to carry out for the purpose of defence.
Subsections 7.3(3.1) to (5.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

An individual who is convicted of an offence under this Part punishable on summary conviction is liable to a fine not exceeding $150,000 and, in the case of an offence referred to in subsection (1), to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year or to both fine and imprisonment.
A corporation that is convicted of an offence under this Part punishable on summary conviction is liable to a fine not exceeding $1,500,000.

Despite subsection (5), if the ANS Corporation is convicted of contravening an order made under subsection 4.91(1), the ANS Corporation is liable to a fine not exceeding $1,500,000 for each day or part of a day the offence continues.
Subsection 7.3(7.1) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.41:
