Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on any terms that the Minister considers appropriate, authorize any person or class of persons to interfere with the operation of a remotely piloted aircraft system or with the performance of the duties of any crew member of a remotely piloted aircraft system if the issuance of authorization is, in the Ministerâs opinion, in the public interest or necessary for aviation safety or security.

The Minister may suspend, cancel or amend an authorization if the suspension, cancellation or amendment is, in the Ministerâs opinion, in the public interest or necessary for aviation safety or security.

Section.Text: The heading before section 7.6 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Vicarious liability â owners of aircraft
Section.Text: If a person may be charged with and found guilty of an offence under this Part relating to an aircraft, the registered owner of the aircraft may be charged with and found guilty of the offence â whether or not the person is identified or prosecuted for the offence â unless, at the time of the offence, the aircraft was in the possession of a person other than the owner without the ownerâs consent.

Section.MarginalNote: Vicarious liability â operators of aircraft
Section.Text: If a person may be charged with and found guilty of an offence under this Part relating to an aircraft, the operator of the aircraft may be charged with and found guilty of the offence â whether or not the person is identified or prosecuted for the offence â unless, at the time of the offence, the aircraft was in the possession of a person other than the operator without the operatorâs consent.

If a crew member may be charged with and found guilty of an offence under this Part relating to an aircraft, the pilot-in-command of the aircraft may be charged with and found guilty of the offence â whether or not the crew member is identified or prosecuted for the offence â unless the offence was committed without the consent of the pilot-in-command.

In a prosecution of any of the following persons for an offence under this Part, it is sufficient proof of the offence to establish that the act or omission that constitutes the offence was committed by an employee or agent or mandatary of the person, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified or prosecuted for the offence:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the registered owner of an aircraft;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the operator of an aircraft;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the operator of an aerodrome or other aviation facility;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an air traffic service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an organization that holds a Canadian aviation document authorizing the maintenance of an aeronautical product or the provision of a maintenance service.

Section.Text: Sections 7.51 to 7.54 do not apply to an offence relating to a contravention of subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1).

Subsection.Text: A person is not to be found guilty of an offence under this Part if they establish that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.
Subsection (1) does not apply to an offence relating to a contravention of subsection 7.3(1) or 7.41(1).
Paragraphs 7.6(1)(a) to (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designate as a provision the contravention of which may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 7.61 to 8.2, any provision of this Part or of any regulation, notice, order or security measure made under this Part, or any provision of the Secure Air Travel Act or of any regulation or direction made under that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribe the maximum amount payable for each violation, not to exceed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $150,000, in the case of an individual, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $1,500,000, in the case of a corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7.6(2) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.6:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes a provision designated under paragraph 7.6(1)(a) commits a violation and is liable to a penalty not exceeding the maximum amount prescribed under paragraph 7.6(1)(b).

A violation that is committed or continued on more than one flight or segment of a flight constitutes a separate violation for each flight or segment of a flight on which the violation is committed or continued.
If a contravention of a provision designated under paragraph 7.6(1)(a) may be proceeded with as a violation or as an offence, proceeding with it in one manner precludes proceeding in the other.
For greater certainty, a violation is not an offence and, accordingly, section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply in respect of a violation.
A person is not to be found liable for a violation if they establish that they exercised all due diligence to prevent its commission.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vicarious liability â owners of aircraft
Subsection.Text: If a person may be proceeded against under this Part for a violation relating to an aircraft, the registered owner of the aircraft may be proceeded against for the violation â whether or not the person is identified or proceeded against for the violation â unless, at the time of the violation, the aircraft was in the possession of a person other than the owner without the ownerâs consent.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vicarious liability â operators of aircraft
Subsection.Text: If a person may be proceeded against under this Part for a violation relating to an aircraft, the operator of the aircraft may be proceeded against for the violation â whether or not the person is identified or proceeded against for the violation â unless, at the time of the violation, the aircraft was in the possession of a person other than the operator without the operatorâs consent.

If a crew member may be proceeded against under this Part for a violation relating to an aircraft, the pilot-in-command of the aircraft may be proceeded against for the violation â whether or not the crew member is identified or proceeded against for the violation â unless the violation was committed without the consent of the pilot-in-command.

In any proceedings under this Part against any of the following persons for a violation, it is sufficient proof of the violation to establish that the act or omission that constitutes the violation was committed by an employee or agent or mandatary of the person, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified or proceeded against for the violation:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the registered owner of an aircraft;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the operator of an aircraft;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the operator of an aerodrome or other aviation facility;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an air traffic service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an organization that holds a Canadian aviation document authorizing the maintenance of a aeronautical product or the provision of a maintenance service.

Section.MarginalNote: Assurance of compliance â no notice served

Subsection.Text: If the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that a person who has not been served with a notice of violation under section 7.7 has committed a violation, the Minister may enter into an assurance of compliance with the person that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: identifies the violation and provides that the person will comply with the provision to which the violation relates within the period and subject to the terms and conditions specified in the assurance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the amount and form of any security that, pending compliance with the assurance, is to be deposited with the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the penalty that the person would have been liable to pay for the violation if the assurance had not been entered into.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may extend the period specified under paragraph (1)(a) if the Minister is satisfied that the person is unable to comply with the assurance of compliance for reasons beyond the personâs control.
A person who enters into an assurance of compliance is deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into, unless they request a review under subsection (4).

A person who enters into an assurance of compliance may, within 48 hours after the assurance is signed, unless a notice of default has been served under subsection (6), file a request with the Tribunal for a review of the facts of the violation, in which case the assurance is deemed to be a notice of violation and a review of the facts of the violation and the amount of the penalty is deemed to have been requested under section 7.91.
If the Minister is satisfied that a person has complied with an assurance of compliance, the Minister shall cause a notice to that effect to be served on the person and, on the service of the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no further proceedings may be taken against the person with respect to the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(b) shall be returned to the person.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person has not complied with an assurance of compliance, the Minister shall cause a notice of default to be served on the person to the effect that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice of default, twice the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance without taking into account the limit specified in paragraph 7.6(1)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(b) is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The notice of default shall indicate the date, which shall be 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, on or before which a request for a review may be filed and the address where the request may be filed and shall include the particulars concerning the procedure for requesting a review.
On the service of a notice of default, the person served has no right of set-off or compensation against any amount that they spent under the assurance of compliance.
Any security deposited under subsection (1) shall be returned to the person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice is served under subsection (6) and the person pays double the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance of compliance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member determines under section 8 or an appeal panel decides under section 8.1 that the person has complied with the assurance.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7.7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that a person has committed a violation, the Minister may issue a notice of violation in respect of the alleged contravention and shall cause it to be served on the person by personal service or by registered or certified mail sent to the person at their latest known address.
The portion of subsection 7.7(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Le procÃ¨s-verbal est Ã©tabli en la forme que le gouverneur en conseil peut fixer par rÃ¨glement. Y sont notamment indiquÃ©sâ¯:
Paragraph 7.7(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the violation;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 7.7(2)(b) and (c) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: sous rÃ©serve des rÃ¨glements pris en vertu de lâalinÃ©a 7.6(1)b), le montant quâil dÃ©termine, conformÃ©ment aux critÃ¨res quâil peut Ã©tablir Ã cette fin, et qui doit Ãªtre payÃ© Ã titre de pÃ©nalitÃ© pour la contravention lorsque la personne ne dÃ©sire pas comparaÃ®tre devant un conseiller pour prÃ©senter ses observations sur les faits reprochÃ©s;

Section.Text: Section 7.8 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le destinataire du procÃ¨s-verbal doit soit payer la pÃ©nalitÃ©, soit dÃ©poser une requÃªte en rÃ©vision des faits reprochÃ©s ou du montant de la pÃ©nalitÃ©.
Section 7.9 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Payment of specified amount precludes further proceedings
Section.Text: If a person who is served with or sent a notice under subsection 7.7(1) pays the amount specified in the notice in accordance with the requirements set out in the notice, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Part shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.

Section.MarginalNote: Request for compliance agreement â notice served
Section.Text: Instead of paying the penalty set out in the notice of violation, the person served with the notice may, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice, request to enter into a compliance agreement with the Minister under section 7.902 that ensures the personâs compliance with the provision to which the violation relates.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on application made under section 7.901, enter into a compliance agreement with a person who has been served with a notice of violation on any terms and conditions that are satisfactory to the Minister. The terms and conditions may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: include a provision for the deposit of reasonable security, in a form and amount satisfactory to the Minister, as a guarantee that the person will comply with the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the reduction, in whole or in part, of the penalty for the violation.

Subsection.Text: A person who enters into a compliance agreement with the Minister under subsection (1) is deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the agreement was entered into.
If the Minister is satisfied that a person has complied with a compliance agreement, the Minister shall cause a notice to that effect to be served on the person, at which time

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(a) shall be returned to the person.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person has not complied with a compliance agreement, the Minister shall cause a notice of default to be served on the person to the effect that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: instead of being liable to pay the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation, the person is liable to pay, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice of violation, twice the amount of that penalty, without taking into account the limits specified in paragraph 7.6(1)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under paragraph (1)(a) is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: On the service of a notice of default, the person served has no right of set-off or compensation against any amount that they spent under the compliance agreement and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay the amount set out in the notice of default within the time and in the manner specified in the notice of violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the notice provides for the forfeiture of the security deposited under the compliance agreement, that security is forfeited to His Majesty in right of Canada and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If a person served with a notice of default pays the amount set out in the notice of default within the time and in the manner specified in the notice of violation, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Part shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to enter into compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: If the Minister refuses to enter into a compliance agreement requested under section 7.901, the person who made the request may, within the time specified by the Minister and in the manner specified in the notice of violation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pay the amount of the penalty set out in the notice of violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: file a request for a review under subsection 7.91(1).

Subsection.Text: If a person pays the amount set out in the notice of violation within the time specified by the Minister and in the manner specified in the notice of violation, the Minister shall accept the amount as and in complete satisfaction of the amount of the penalty for the violation and no further proceedings under this Part shall be taken against the person in respect of that violation.
Subsection 7.91(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Le destinataire du procÃ¨s-verbal qui veut faire rÃ©viser la dÃ©cision du ministre Ã lâÃ©gard des faits reprochÃ©s ou du montant de la pÃ©nalitÃ© dÃ©pose une requÃªte auprÃ¨s du Tribunal Ã lâadresse indiquÃ©e dans le procÃ¨s-verbal, au plus tard Ã la date limite qui y est indiquÃ©e, ou dans le dÃ©lai supÃ©rieur Ã©ventuellement accordÃ© Ã sa demande par le Tribunal.
Section 7.91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

A person who is served with a notice of default under subsection 7.62(6) and who wishes to have the Ministerâs decision made under that subsection reviewed shall, on or before the date specified in the notice â or within any further time that the Tribunal on application may allow â file a written request for a review with the Tribunal.
Subsection 7.91(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
On receipt of a request, the Tribunal shall appoint a time and place for the review and shall notify the Minister and the person who filed the request of the time and place in writing.
Subsection 7.91(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The burden of establishing that a person has committed a violation or has not complied with the assurance of compliance referred to in the notice of default is on the Minister.
Subsection 7.91(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
A person who is alleged to have committed a violation is not required, and shall not be compelled, to give any evidence or testimony in the matter.
Section 7.91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Despite subsection 7.61(5), for the purposes of a request for review under subsection (1.1) a person does not have a defence by reason that the person exercised all due diligence to comply with the assurance of compliance.

Section.Text: Section 7.92 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person who fails to pay the amount of the penalty specified in a notice of violation within the specified time and who does not file a request for a review is deemed to have committed the violation alleged in the notice.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 8 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: At the conclusion of a review, the member of the Tribunal who conducts the review shall without delay inform the Minister and the person alleged to have committed a violation

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 8(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has not committed a violation, in which case, subject to section 8.1, no further proceedings under this Part shall be taken against the person in respect of the alleged violation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has committed a violation and, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 7.6(1)(b), of the amount that must be paid to the Tribunal by or on behalf of the person and the time within which it must be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has complied with the assurance of compliance, in which case, subject to section 8.1, no further proceedings under this Part shall be taken against the person in respect of the alleged violation; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that the person has not complied with the assurance of compliance, in which case, the member confirms the decision made by the Minister under subsection 7.62(6).

Section.Text: Subsections 8.1(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The appeal panel of the Tribunal assigned to hear an appeal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under paragraph 8(a) or (b), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and, subject to any regulations made under paragraph 7.6(1)(b), substitute its own decision; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under paragraph 8(c) or (d), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and substitute its own decision.

Subsection.Text: After making its decision, the appeal panel shall inform the person and the Minister of its decision without delay and specify the period within which any amount determined by the appeal panel to be payable to the Tribunal is to be paid.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8.1:

The Minister may obtain from the Tribunal or the member, as the case may be, a certificate in the form prescribed by the Governor in Council setting out the amount of the penalty required to be paid by a person who fails, within the time required,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to pay the amount of a penalty set out in a notice of violation, or in a notice of default served under subsection 7.62(6) or 7.902(4), or to file a request for a review under section 7.91;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount set out in a determination made under paragraph 8(b) or in a decision confirmed under paragraph 8(d) or file an appeal under section 8.1; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to pay an amount determined under subsection 8.1(4).

Section.Text: Subsection 8.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On production in any superior court, a certificate issued under section 8.11 shall be registered in the court and, when so registered, a certificate has the same force and effect, and proceedings may be taken in connection with it, as if it were a judgment in that court obtained by His Majesty in right of Canada against the person named in the certificate for a debt of the amount set out in the certificate.

Section.Text: The heading before section 8.4 and sections 8.4 and 8.5 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following before section 8.7:

Section.Text: Regulations described in paragraph 4.71(2)(k.4) of the Aeronautics Act may be made under subsection 4.71(1) of that Act in relation to a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document that a person established, developed or otherwise has in accordance with a regulation that was made under that subsection before the day on which this section comes into force.

Regulations described in paragraph 4.901(e) of the Aeronautics Act may be made under section 4.9 of that Act in relation to a system, process, procedure, program, plan or document that a person established, developed or otherwise has in accordance with a regulation that was made under that section before the day on which this section comes into force.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by replacing âsubsections 4.79(1), 6.5(5), 22(2) and 24.2.(4)â opposite âAeronautics Actâ with âsubsection 4.79(1), section 5.32 and subsections 6.5(5), 22(2) and 24.2(4)â.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendment to the Budget Implementation Act, 2019, No. 1

Section.Text: Section 272 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2019, No. 1 is repealed.
The Canada Transportation Act is amended by adding the following after section 49:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation described in subsection (2) or that varies or suspends any requirement or condition set out in such a regulation, if the Minister is of the opinion that it is in the public interest to do so, having regard to the purposes of the Act of Parliament under which the regulation may be made, and if the interim order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: gives effect to an international standard; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensures compliance with Canadaâs international obligations.

Subsection.Text: A regulation referred to in subsection (1) is a regulation that may, under a provision of an Act of Parliament for which the Minister is responsible, be made by the Governor in Council or the Minister.
Before making an interim order, the Minister must consult with any person or organization that the Minister considers appropriate in the circumstances.
An interim order takes effect on the day on which it is made or on any later day specified in it and ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day before the day on which a regulation that has the same effect as the interim order comes into force, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is three years â or any shorter period specified in the interim order â after the day on which the interim order takes effect.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of administration and enforcement, an interim order is deemed to be the regulation that may contain the provision contained in the interim order or in which the requirement or condition varied or suspended by the interim order is set out, and any provisions related to administration and enforcement that apply in respect of that regulation, including provisions related to offences and punishment, apply in respect of the interim order.

An interim order may designate one or more of its provisions as subject to an administrative monetary penalty regime under the Act described in subsection (2) if that Act authorizes the designation of provisions of a regulation described in that subsection as being subject to the regime.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an interim order.

The Minister must ensure that an interim order is accessible to the public unless the Minister is of the opinion that it would be inappropriate due to exceptional circumstances, such as if making the interim order accessible compromises public safety.

A person is not liable to be found guilty of an offence or subjected to an administrative sanction for a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at that time, the person had been notified of the interim order.

Section.Text: Paragraph 12(b) of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the 16 Justices of Appeal, $338,800 each;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 24(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

TitleText.XRefExternal: Administrative Tribunals Support Service of Canada Act

Subsection.Text: The definition administrative tribunal in section 2 of the Administrative Tribunals Support Service of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: administrative tribunalâmeans a body referred to in Schedule 1 or 2.â(tribunal administratif)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: territorial bodyâmeans a body established under an Act of the legislature of a territory.â(organisme territorial)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the Minister may, by order, amend Schedule 2 to add, amend or delete the name of a territorial body.

The Minister may add the name of a territorial body to Schedule 2 only if the Minister is of the opinion that there is a satisfactory funding arrangement for the provision of support services and of facilities to the territorial body.

If a territorial body is composed of members of a body established by an Act of Parliament, the Minister must consult the minister responsible for the body established by that Act before adding the name of the territorial body to Schedule 2.
An order made under subsection (1) is not a statutory instrument as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act. However, the order must be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 18:

The Service may spend revenues that it receives in a fiscal year for the provision of any services and facilities to a territorial body referred to in Schedule 2 to offset expenditures that it incurs in that fiscal year.
The schedule to the Act is numbered as Schedule 1.
The heading of Schedule 1 to the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule 1, the Schedule 2 set out in Schedule 6 to this Act.
Section 216 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Tribunalâmeans the Environmental Protection Tribunal of Canada established by subsection 243(1).â(Tribunal)

Section.Text: Paragraph 235(6)(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a statement that a request for a review may be made to the Tribunal; and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 241(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: As long as the Tribunal is not seized of a request for the review of an order, the enforcement officer may, after giving reasonable notice,

Section.Text: The heading before section 243 and sections 243 to 248 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: There is established a tribunal, to be known as the Environmental Protection Tribunal of Canada, consisting of members appointed by the Minister on either a full-time or a part-time basis.
The Minister is to appoint one of the members as Chairperson of the Tribunal.

Section.Text: The Tribunalâs head office is to be in the National Capital Region, as defined in section 2 of the National Capital Act.

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson has supervision over and direction of the activities of the Tribunal, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the apportionment of work among members and the assignment of members to hear matters brought before the Tribunal and, when the Tribunal sits in panels, the assignment of members to panels; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the management of the Tribunalâs internal affairs.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Chairperson absent or unable to act
Subsection.Text: If the Chairperson is absent or unable to act or if the office is vacant, the Minister is to designate another member of the Tribunal to perform the functions of the Chairperson.

Section.Text: Members of the Tribunal are to be appointed to hold office during good behaviour for a renewable term of not more than three years, but may be removed by the Minister at any time for cause.

A person is not eligible to be appointed as a member of the Tribunal unless the person is knowledgeable about the conservation and protection of the Canadian environment, environmental and human health, administrative law as it relates to environmental regulation or traditional aboriginal ecological knowledge.
Members of the Tribunal must not accept or hold any office or employment inconsistent with their functions under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 249(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Members of the Tribunal are to be paid the remuneration that is fixed by the Governor in Council.
The portion of subsection 249(2) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Members of the Tribunal are entitled to be paid
Section 249 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Members of the Tribunal are deemed to be employees for the purposes of the Government Employees Compensation Act and to be employed in the federal public administration for the purposes of any regulations made under section 9 of the Aeronautics Act.
Full-time members of the Tribunal are deemed to be employed in the public service for the purposes of the Public Service Superannuation Act.

Section.Text: Sections 250 to 252 of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the term of a member of the Tribunal ends before they have made a decision in respect of a matter, they may, with the authorization of the Chairperson, continue, during a period of not more than 180 days, to act as a member in respect of that matter.
Section 253 of the Act is replaced by the following:
No criminal or civil proceedings lie against a member of the Tribunal for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in performing any of their functions under this Act.
Section 255 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Members of the Tribunal are servants of His Majesty in right of Canada for the purposes of the law of tort or of extracontractual civil liability.
Sections 256 and 257 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any person to whom an order is directed may, by notice in writing given to the Tribunal within 30 days after the day on which the person receives a copy of the written order or the day on which the oral order is given, make a request to the Tribunal for a review of the order.

The Chairperson, or any member of the Tribunal that they may designate, may extend the period within which a request for a review may be made if, in their opinion, it is in the public interest to do so.

Section.Text: When the Tribunal receives a notice requesting the review of an order, the Chairperson must conduct the review or designate a member, or a panel of three members, of the Tribunal to conduct it.
Subsection 258(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the request for a review does not suspend the operation of an order.

Section.Text: Sections 261 and 262 of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Any summons issued or order made under subsection 260(1) may be made a summons or order of the Federal Court or of the superior court of a province and is enforceable in the same manner as a summons or order of that court.

To make a summons issued or order made under subsection 260(1) a summons or order of the Federal Court or of the superior court of a province, the courtâs usual practice and procedure in such matters may be followed or a certified copy of the summons or order may be filed with the courtâs registrar.
Section 266 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal must, within 15 days after the day on which the review is completed, render a decision, with written reasons, and provide all persons to whom the order was directed and the Minister with a copy of the decision and the reasons.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 267(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Chairperson may make rules
Subsection 267(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chairperson may make rules under subsection (1) with respect to the review of orders made under any Act of Parliament that provides for the review of those orders in accordance with sections 257 to 271 of this Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by replacing âa review officerâ, âthe review officerâ and âreview officersâ with âthe Tribunalâ and âreview officerâsâ with âTribunalâsâ, with any necessary modifications, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Text: subsection 258(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 263 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 264;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 265 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 267(1)(b) and (c);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 269;

Paragraph.Text: section 271;

Paragraph.Text: paragraph 280.1(1)(b) and subsections 280.1(2) and (3);

Paragraph.Text: paragraph 280.2(1)(b) and subsection 280.2(2); and

TitleText.XRefExternal: Environmental Violations Administrative Monetary Penalties Act

Subsection.Text: The definitions Chief Review Officer and review officer in section 2 of the Environmental Violations Administrative Monetary Penalties Act are repealed.
Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Tribunalâmeans the Environmental Protection Tribunal of Canada established by subsection 243(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999.â(Tribunal)

Section.Text: Sections 15 to 17 of the Act are replaced by the following:

A person, ship or vessel that is served with a notice of violation may, within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, or within any longer period that the Tribunal allows, make a request to the Tribunal for a review of the penalty or the facts of the alleged violation, or both.

Section.MarginalNote: Variation or cancellation of notice of violation
Section.Text: At any time before the Tribunal receives a request for a review in respect of a notice of violation, a person designated under paragraph 6(b) may cancel the notice of violation or correct an error in it.
When the Tribunal receives a request made under section 15, the Chairperson of the Tribunal must conduct the review or designate a member, or a panel of three members, of the Tribunal to conduct it.
Subsection 19(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Tribunal may summon any person to appear as a witness and may order the witness to

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: produce any documents and things that the Tribunal considers necessary for the purpose of the review.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

After giving the person, ship or vessel that requested the review and the Minister reasonable notice orally or in writing of a hearing and allowing a reasonable opportunity in the circumstances for the person, ship or vessel and the Minister to make oral representations, the Tribunal must determine whether the person, ship or vessel committed a violation.
Subsection 20(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the Tribunal determines that the penalty for the violation was not determined in accordance with the regulations, it must correct the amount of the penalty.

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal must render its determination, with reasons, in writing within 30 days after the day on which the review is completed and, without delay, provide the Minister and the person, ship or vessel to which the determination relates with a copy of the determination and reasons.
Section 22 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the Tribunal determines that a person, ship or vessel has committed a violation, the person, ship or vessel is liable for the amount of the penalty as set out in the determination.
Section 23 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A determination made under section 20 is final and binding and, except for judicial review under the Federal Courts Act, is not subject to appeal or review by any court.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and sections 550 to 552.

Definition.Text: Chief Administratorâhas the same meaning as in section 2 of the Administrative Tribunals Support Service of Canada Act.â(administrateur en chef)

Chief Review Officerâmeans the review officer appointed as Chief Review Officer under subsection 244(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, as it read immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force.â(rÃ©viseur-chef)

review officerâmeans a person appointed as a review officer under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, as it read immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force.â(rÃ©viseur)

Tribunalâmeans the Environmental Protection Tribunal of Canada established by subsection 243(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 as it reads on the day on which this Division comes into force.â(Tribunal)

Subsection.Text: A contract entered into before the day on which this Division comes into force by the Chief Review Officer or a review officer respecting the provision of services or material, in relation to the exercise of their powers and the performance of their duties and functions under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, is deemed to have been entered into by the Chief Administrator.

Unless the context otherwise requires, in a contract referred to in subsection (1), every reference to the Chief Review Officer or to a review officer is to be read as a reference to the Chief Administrator.

The person who holds the office of Chief Review Officer immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force continues in office as the Chairperson of the Tribunal for the remainder of the term for which they were appointed.

A person who holds the office of review officer immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force continues in office as a member of the Tribunal for the remainder of the term for which they were appointed.

Section.MarginalNote: Pending requests for review or other matters

Subsection.Text: Any request for review or other matter pending before the Chief Review Officer, a review officer or a panel of review officers immediately before the day on which this Division comes into force is to be continued before the Tribunal.
An order or decision made by the Chief Review Officer, a review officer or a panel of review officers is deemed to have been made by the Tribunal and is enforceable as such.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Division.

Definition.Text: Corporationâmeans the Freshwater Fish Marketing Corporation established by subsection 3(1) of the Freshwater Fish Marketing Act.â(Office)
Ministerâmeans the member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada designated as Minister for the purposes of the Freshwater Fish Marketing Act.â(ministre)

Section.Text: The purpose of this Division is to authorize the taking of various measures for the divestiture and dissolution of all or any part of the Corporation.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council and on any terms that the Governor in Council considers appropriate, take any of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of the property of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: procure an addition to, or other material change in, the objects or purposes for which the Corporation is incorporated or the restrictions on the businesses or activities that it may carry on;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: procure the amalgamation of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: procure the dissolution of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: procure the incorporation of a corporation whose securities, on incorporation, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for His Majesty in right of Canada and sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of those securities;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: procure the formation of any other entity whose securities, on formation, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for His Majesty in right of Canada and sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of those securities; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: acquire securities of a corporation or of any other entity that, on acquisition, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for His Majesty in right of Canada and sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of those securities.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may do anything or cause anything to be done that is necessary for, or incidental to, a measure taken under subsection (1).

For greater certainty, if the Minister considers it appropriate to carry out the purposes of this Division, the Minister may appoint a liquidator, who is to hold office during pleasure, to administer the divestiture and dissolution of the Corporation.

On appointment of a liquidator, the Chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Corporation, the President of the Corporation and the other directors of the Corporation cease to hold their respective offices and the liquidator may exercise all of the powers and perform all the duties and functions of the Corporation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Part X of Financial Administration Act
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, declare that any provision of Part X of the Financial Administration Act does not apply to a corporation referred to in paragraph (1)(e) or to another entity referred to in paragraph (1)(f) that is a corporation.

The Corporation, any corporation referred to in paragraph 555(1)(e) or any other entity referred to in paragraph 555(1)(f) â or any entity that is wholly owned, or wholly held or controlled, directly or indirectly, by the Corporation, the corporation or the other entity â may, with the approval of the Governor in Council and on any terms that the Governor in Council considers appropriate, take any of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of its property;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of its liabilities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: issue securities and sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of those securities;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: reorganize its capital structure;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: acquire the property of a corporation or any other entity;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: procure an addition to, or other material change in, the objects or purposes for which it is incorporated or formed or the restrictions on the businesses or activities that it may carry on;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: procure the incorporation of a corporation whose securities, on incorporation, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for it;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: procure the formation of any other entity whose securities, on formation, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for it;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: acquire securities of a corporation or any other entity that, on acquisition, would be held by, on behalf of or in trust for it;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: sell or otherwise dispose of some or all of the securities of a corporation or any other entity that are held by, on behalf of or in trust for it;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: procure its amalgamation;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: procure its dissolution; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: do anything that is necessary for, or incidental to, a measure taken under any of paragraphs (a) to (l).

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister and on any terms that the Governor in Council considers appropriate, by order, direct the Corporation, any corporation referred to in paragraph 555(1)(e) or any other entity referred to in paragraph 555(1)(f) â or any entity that is wholly owned, or wholly held or controlled, directly or indirectly, by the Corporation, the corporation or the other entity â to take, or cause any wholly-owned or wholly held or controlled entity of the Corporation, the corporation or the other entity to take a measure referred to in subsection (1).

The directors of the Corporation or the corporation referred to in paragraph 555(1)(e) or any persons acting in a similar capacity with respect to the other entity referred to in paragraph 555(1)(f) must comply with an order made under subsection (2).
Compliance with an order made under subsection (2) is deemed to be in the best interests of the Corporation, the corporation or the other entity.

As soon as feasible after the Corporation, corporation or entity implements an order and completes any actions that are required to be taken in connection with that implementation, it must notify the Minister that it has done so.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (2).

The Minister is to cause a copy of an order made under subsection 556(2) to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the order is made.

However, if the Minister is of the opinion that publishing information contained in the order would be detrimental to the commercial interests of Canada, of the Corporation or of any corporation or entity referred to in the order, the Minister is to cause a copy of the order to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the Minister is notified that the order has been implemented.

Before the Minister forms an opinion as to whether publishing information contained in the order would be detrimental, the Minister must consult the Board of Directors of the Corporation or of the corporation or the person or group of persons acting in a similar capacity with respect to the other entity.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer â property, rights or interests
Section.Text: Subject to sections 559 and 560, on the day on which the Corporation is dissolved, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister and on any terms that the Governor in Council considers appropriate, transfer, or direct to be transferred, the control, management and administration of any property, rights or interests held by the Corporation to any Minister, department or agency of the Government of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The property of the Corporation is to be applied in satisfaction of its debts and liabilities, and of the charges, costs and expenses incurred in respect of its divestiture and dissolution.
All charges, costs and expenses incurred in respect of the divestiture and dissolution of the Corporation are payable out of the property of the Corporation in priority to all other claims.

Any surplus that remains after the satisfaction of the debts and liabilities of the Corporation and the charges, costs and expenses incurred in respect of its divestiture and dissolution belongs to His Majesty in right of Canada.
Any debts and liabilities of the Corporation that remain unsatisfied on the day on which the Corporation is dissolved become the debts and liabilities of His Majesty in right of Canada.

Section.Text: The Corporation is dissolved.

On or after the day on which the Corporation is dissolved, every reference to the Corporation in any contract, instrument, act or other document executed or signed by the Corporation in its own name is to be read, unless the context otherwise requires, as a reference to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: Any action, suit or other legal proceeding in respect of an obligation or liability incurred by the Corporation in respect of its divestiture or dissolution may be brought against His Majesty in right of Canada in any court that would have had jurisdiction if the action, suit or other legal proceeding had been brought against the Corporation.

Any action, suit or other legal proceeding to which the Corporation is party that is pending in any court on the day on which the Corporation is dissolved may be continued by or against His Majesty in right of Canada in like manner and to the same extent as it could have been continued by or against the Corporation.

Section.Text: Despite the provisions of any contract, agreement or order in council, no person who is appointed to hold office as a member of the Board of Directors of the Corporation has any right to any compensation, damages, indemnity or other form of relief from His Majesty in right of Canada or from any employee or agent of His Majesty for ceasing to hold that office or for the abolition of that office, by the operation of this Division.

The Canada Student Financial Assistance Act is amended by adding the following after section 6.3:

Section.MarginalNote: Exception â certain designated educational institutions
Section.Text: The Minister shall deny the provision of financial assistance to all those who are qualifying students in relation to a designated educational institution outside Canada that is private and for-profit and offers courses at a post-secondary school level.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6.4:

If a province has suspended or denied the provision of financial assistance in relation to a class of qualifying students, of designated educational institutions or of programs of studies at designated educational institutions, the Minister may do the same in relation to that class if the Minister is satisfied that there are compelling reasons to believe that the provision of the financial assistance would

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate the commission by the qualifying students or designated educational institutions of an offence under this Act or any other Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: constitute a risk to the integrity of the provision of financial assistance under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: expose the qualifying students or His Majesty in right of Canada to financial risk.

Subsection.Text: Section 6.31 of the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act applies as of August 1, 2029 in relation to a qualifying student who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is registered in a program of studies during the loan year beginning on August 1, 2025;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of that program of studies, receives financial assistance under that Act during that loan year or received financial assistance under that Act during a previous loan year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: continues that program of studies at the same designated educational institution.

Subsection.Text: Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in subsection (1) have the same meaning as in the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 212(1)(a) of the Canada Business Corporations Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dissolve a corporation by issuing a certificate of dissolution under this section on the grounds that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation has not commenced business within three years after the date shown in its certificate of incorporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation has not carried on its business for three consecutive years,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation is in default for a period of one year in sending to the Director any fee or any notice, document or other information required by this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation does not have any directors or is in the situation described in subsection 109(4), or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the Director is notified by the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness that the corporation is a listed entity as defined in subsection 83.01(1) of the Criminal Code; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 212(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless cause to the contrary has been shown or an order has been made by a court under section 246, the Director may issue a certificate of dissolution in the form that the Director fixes. However, if subsection (2) applies, the certificate is not to be issued until after the end of the period referred to in that subsection.
